#1 4.7★

GroupMixer↗ opens tool website

Almost every group generation usecase

Free, no accountmulti-roundconstraintsprivacy-friendlystrong-exports
Multi-round: YesRepeat encounter limits: YesUnique-contact: YesHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: YesAttribute balance: YesCustom group capacities: YesPartial attendance by session: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: YesAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: YesCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: YesDeterministic seed / reproducibility: YesEditable/random team names: PartialEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: YesFixed person placement: YesGender/label balancing: YesGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: YesImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: YesMulti-round: YesNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: YesPair meeting matrix: YesPartial attendance by session: YesPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: YesPer-session: YesPerson/group visit count: YesPinned people / fixed placements: YesPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: YesResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: YesBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: YesSaved scenarios/workspaces: YesScenario bundle: YesSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: YesSoft prefer-together: YesSolver telemetry/result metadata: YesSolver tuning controls: YesCSV export: YesCSV import: YesStorage management/cleanup: YesTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: YesVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: YesZoom CSV: Yes
Quality5★
This is the result-quality outlier in the comparison. GroupMixer goes above and beyond: it uses a custom-built solver engine designed specifically for social-golfer-like, multi-session group assignment cases. For this problem class, that domain-specific machinery can outperform general tools such as OR-Tools and enterprise constraint solvers such as Gurobi or Hexaly by a huge margin, because the solver encodes extensive SGP-specific theory, construction logic, local search, repeat minimization, constraints, capacities, partial attendance, and diagnostics directly into the search. It is plausibly at or beyond the level a user can expect from current academic SGP literature for many practical cases.
Features4.8★
Extremely deep for a group generator: multi-session scheduling, participant availability, editable groups, custom capacities and minimums, per-session capacity overrides, hard/soft constraints, weighted objectives, repeat reduction, imports, exports, saved scenarios/workspaces, saved result history, scenario import/export bundles, solver telemetry/result metadata, diagnostics, pair matrices, and API access. The main missing areas are presentation gimmicks, self-join links, and arbitrary non-group-generator customization.
Exports4.8★
Excellent output coverage: CSV-style exports, copy/print/share workflows, Zoom breakout CSV export, scenario bundles, result diagnostics, and API output. Not a literal 5/5 because it does not cover every presentation/export format such as polished image/PDF result exports or embeddable result widgets.
Privacy4.5★
Very strong for a web-based group generator, mainly because normal solving runs in the browser and does not upload participant names, group assignments, or full scenario contents to GroupMixer servers. Drafts, saved scenarios, saved results, and UI state are stored locally in the browser via IndexedDB/localStorage/sessionStorage/service-worker cache. It does not deserve a perfect score because Vercel hosting sees normal technical request logs, Vercel Web Analytics / Convex telemetry may receive page views and coarse product events using a sessionStorage-based session id, solver failures may send anonymized scenario structure, and feedback can transmit whatever the user submits. Far ahead of ad-tech-heavy competitors: no account is required, no participant roster upload is needed for normal use, and there is no mass third-party vendor consent stack.
Ease4.6★
The basic landing-page flow is easy: paste names, choose groups or group size, and generate. The advanced grid/workspace is necessarily denser than one-click splitters because it exposes sessions, capacities, constraints, imports, diagnostics, and saved scenarios.
Design4.1★
Modern and much more polished than most utility sites. Some screens are naturally dense because the tool exposes advanced setup, and parts of the expert workspace/mobile experience could be smoother.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

The strongest all-around group generator here if the user needs more than a one-off random split, but the advanced workspace is naturally denser than toy randomizers.

Pricing: Free browser-based tool. No account, subscription, or paywall for the solver, imports, exports, scenario storage, or result diagnostics.

Pros

  • Purpose-built solver engine for social golfer-style cases
  • Deep constraint support
  • Multi-round repeat reduction
  • Runs in the browser with no account
  • Strong import/export options, including Zoom CSV and API access
  • Scenario manager with saved workspaces and result history
  • Scenario import/export bundles, diagnostics, and storage management

Cons

  • Relatively new and unknown
  • Hard to discover
  • Advanced workspace has a learning curve
  • No self-join flow or presentation gimmicks
#2 4.5★

ChatGPT Pro Extended Reasoning↗ opens tool website

Complex natural-language grouping drafts when cost and verification are acceptable

Paid or subscriptionmulti-roundconstraintsstrong-exportsai-assisted
Multi-round: YesRepeat encounter limits: YesUnique-contact: YesHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: YesAttribute balance: YesCustom group capacities: YesPartial attendance by session: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: YesCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: YesDeterministic seed / reproducibility: PartialEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: YesFixed person placement: YesGender/label balancing: YesGoogle Sheets import: PartialHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: YesImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: YesMulti-round: YesNo account: NoPair meeting count targets: YesPair meeting matrix: YesPartial attendance by session: YesPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: YesPer-session: YesPerson/group visit count: YesPinned people / fixed placements: YesPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: YesResult comments: YesResult quality diagnostics: YesBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: YesSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: YesSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: YesSoft prefer-together: YesSolver telemetry/result metadata: YesSolver tuning controls: YesCSV export: YesCSV import: YesStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: YesUnique-contact: YesVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: YesWeighted soft: YesZoom CSV: Yes
Quality5★
Extremely strong result quality because the model can perform highly advanced reasoning and implement advanced algorithms matching the exact problem ad-hoc, leading to almost always extremely high quality results.
Features5★
Very flexible in natural language: arbitrary group sizes, multi-round schedules, balancing requests, keep-together/keep-apart rules, pinned people, custom output formats, and iterative revisions. It can basically do whatever you ask for. The limitation is validation: these are model-generated plans, not hard solver features with guaranteed feasibility.
Exports5★
Can produce the output in whatever format the user asks for.
Privacy2.1★
A poor default fit for sensitive rosters. Pro is a consumer plan, not the same as Business/Enterprise no-training defaults; data use depends on settings and policies. Users should not paste student/personnel data unless their account and consent setup supports it.
Ease4.5★
Its about as easy as it gets for basically arbitrary features/any feature you can imagine. You just describe whatever you want in natural language.
Design4.4★
Same polished ChatGPT interface as the free version, with better model/tool access. It is chat-first rather than a group-generator UI.
Account friction2★
Requires a paid Pro subscription and account. The cost and plan management are real friction compared with free web group generators.
Review notes

Summary

Extremely capable, but also extremely expensive.

Pricing: Paid ChatGPT Pro plan; pricing has varied by Pro tier/region and is far more expensive than typical group-generator tools. Business/Enterprise privacy defaults are different from consumer Pro.

Pros

  • Can handle essentially arbitrary grouping requirements
  • Can write and run ad hoc optimization scripts
  • Excellent multi-round/repeat-minimization capability when prompted well
  • Can validate results and compute diagnostics
  • Can produce CSV, Excel-style tables, Zoom-ready CSV, Markdown, JSON, or custom formats
  • Natural-language interface is extremely flexible
  • Can research, reason about edge cases, and iterate quickly

Cons

  • Very expensive for this use case
  • Consumer Pro privacy caveats for sensitive rosters
  • Requires an account and paid subscription
  • Not a purpose-built group-generator UI
  • Can be extremely slow, 10 min - 1 hour for generations with more complex constraints
  • Quality depends on prompting and verification
#3 4.3★

Social Golfer Online↗ opens tool website

Golf leagues, trips, tournaments, and other multi-round schedules where avoiding repeat pairings matters

Free with ads/upsellsmulti-roundconstraintsstrong-exports
Multi-round: YesRepeat encounter limits: YesUnique-contact: YesAttribute balance: PartialCustom group capacities: YesPartial attendance by session: YesBrowser: YesCSV export: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: PartialAttribute balance: PartialAttribute total/sum: PartialCopy results to clipboard: PartialCustom group capacities: YesDeterministic seed / reproducibility: PartialEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: YesGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: YesMulti-round: YesNo account: NoPair meeting count targets: PartialPair meeting matrix: YesPartial attendance by session: YesPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: YesPer-session: YesPerson/group visit count: PartialPinned people / fixed placements: YesPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: YesResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: YesBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: YesSaved scenarios/workspaces: YesScenario bundle: YesSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: PartialSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: YesSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: PartialCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: YesTeam leader / representative assignment: PartialUnique-contact: YesVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: PartialZoom CSV: No
Quality4.6★
Very high for multi-round repeat minimization. Genetic/local search, history-aware scheduling, conflict scoring, and separation rules are far beyond random assignment.
Features4.5★
Excellent: multi-session scheduling, repeat minimization, absences, fixed positions, separation rules, dynamic sizes, session locks, matrix report, offline calculation, drag/drop correction, JSON export, and share links. However it does not support arbitrary attribute balance/keep together/keep apart except through the workaround of pinning people.
Exports4.4★
Strong: guest share links, print-ready itinerary reports, matrix reports, data archive/JSON export, and group instructions. Some print/PDF features are tier-limited.
Privacy3.6★
No ad stack and the site says it does not sell or share data, but it stores email/profile/event data and uses PostHog analytics/session infrastructure. Not local-only.
Ease3.3★
Powerful but not instant. Users need to register, add members, create sessions, and understand a golf scheduling workflow.
Design4★
Modern, clear, and purpose-built. The landing page explains complex scheduling well, though the app is more specialized than a generic group maker.
Account friction2★
Full use requires account registration. Guests can view shared assignments without login, but organizers cannot just paste names and generate anonymously.
Review notes

Summary

One of the strongest real competitors for repeat-minimizing multi-round grouping. Narrowly golf-branded and account-gated, but algorithmically far beyond normal random splitters.

Pricing: Free during beta for individual non-commercial use. The site says beta registrants are grandfathered into Lifetime Premium at $0 if paid tiers are introduced; agent/club/pro features may require direct access or future tiers.

Pros

  • Real multi-round repeat minimization
  • Genetic/local-search algorithm
  • Session history and pair matrix
  • Absences and last-minute swaps
  • Fixed positions and separation rules
  • Dynamic group sizes
  • Guest share links
  • Offline-capable calculation
  • JSON/data export

Cons

  • Organizer account required
  • Golf-specific language and workflow
  • Beta/as-is service caveats
  • Some print/pro features are tier-limited
  • Not a quick anonymous random splitter
  • Stores event/member data on the service
#4 4.2★

Clever Groups↗ opens tool website

Preference-based classroom, camp, event, and accommodation grouping

Free with ads/upsellsconstraintsself-joinstrong-exports
Hard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: PartialAttribute balance: YesCustom group capacities: YesPartial attendance by session: PartialBrowser: PartialNo account: PartialCSV export: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: PartialCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: YesDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: PartialEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: PartialFixed person placement: PartialGender/label balancing: YesGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: PartialImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: PartialPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: PartialPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: PartialPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: YesBrowser: PartialSaved class roster import: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: YesSaved scenarios/workspaces: YesScenario bundle: PartialSelf-join participant link: YesShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: PartialSoft prefer-together: YesSolver telemetry/result metadata: YesSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: YesCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: PartialUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: PartialZoom CSV: No
Quality4.1★
Strong for single-session preference/category grouping. Satisfaction scoring and category/blocking-preference handling make it far better than random splitters. It is not a multi-round social-golfer optimizer and may need manual tweaking for hard real-world allocations.
Features4.4★
Very strong for preference-based grouping: participant preferences, teacher blocking preferences, categories, category balancing, custom group sizes, guest preference entry, spreadsheet import/export, saved teams, multiple teams, group history, drag-and-drop adjustments, satisfaction scoring, and CSV export. Missing multi-round repeat minimization, pair matrix, and advanced solver tuning.
Exports4★
Good output surface: CSV export, displayed groups, group history, satisfaction metrics, and registered workflows for importing/exporting student and preference lists. No Zoom-specific export, print/PDF focus, or public share result page in the try-it flow.
Privacy2.8★
Mixed. The site has detailed GDPR/CCPA/COPPA-style policy language and says it does not sell/share personal information, but the full workflow stores names/preferences in a hosted service with accounts, cookies, Fathom/Cookiebot/Cloudflare, Zoho/Backblaze/Hostgator-style subprocessors, and child-consent complexity. Not local/private like browser-only tools.
Ease3.2★
The no-registration try-it flow is usable, but the full product has a lot of concepts: teams, students, preferences, blocking preferences, categories, group entries, category entries, satisfaction scores, and guest links. Powerful, not trivial.
Design3.3★
Functional but not slick. It feels like a mature school/admin web app rather than a modern lightweight tool. The cookie banner is heavy, but the grouping workflow is understandable once learned.
Account friction3★
Try-it mode works without registration, which is good. Real use strongly pushes account creation for saving, spreadsheet import, multiple teams, and student self-entry; students/guests may also join via links/cookies.
Review notes

Summary

One of the strongest reviewed tools for single-session preference/category grouping. It is cloud/account-heavy and not a repeat-pair optimizer, but the actual grouping model is serious.

Pricing: Free try-it mode without registration. Registration unlocks saved teams, multiple teams, spreadsheet import, and student self-entry workflows; site copy says the service has been free, but subscription/payment infrastructure exists in the privacy policy.

Pros

  • Preference-based group optimization
  • Blocking preferences / keep-apart style controls
  • Category balancing and custom group sizes
  • Guest/student self-entry links
  • Spreadsheet import/export in registered workflow
  • Satisfaction scoring and group history
  • CSV export and drag-and-drop tweaking

Cons

  • Account needed for the full useful workflow
  • Hosted roster/preference data and child-consent complexity
  • Heavy cookie/privacy banner
  • Not a multi-round repeat optimizer
  • UI has admin-app complexity
  • No Zoom-specific export
#5 4.1★

ClassroomGroupGenerator.com↗ opens tool website

Teachers who want browser-saved rosters, constraints, presentation mode, and classroom-tool integration

Free, no accountconstraintsstrong-exports
Hard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: YesAttribute balance: YesCustom group capacities: PartialPartial attendance by session: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: YesGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: YesImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: PartialPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: YesRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: YesScenario bundle: YesSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: PartialCSV import: YesStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: PartialUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.8★
Much better than basic random splitters because it supports keep-together, keep-apart, gender modes, group size/count, and balancing strategy. It is a single-session classroom generator rather than a multi-round optimizer.
Features4.2★
Strong classroom feature depth: saved rosters, CSV/TXT import, group size/count, gender modes, always-together and do-not-group-with rules, absent handling via roster state, task assignment, presentation mode, reshuffle, and saved boards.
Exports4.1★
Good output surface for teachers: generated group cards, reshuffle, download, presentation/whiteboard use, saved local class state, and share/import/export claims. It is not a spreadsheet-first or Zoom-specific exporter.
Privacy3.6★
Better than ad-heavy utilities and the free mode can keep class data in browser storage. The live page loaded Google Analytics, Microsoft Clarity, Crisp, and related scripts; the privacy policy also describes account/cloud storage of student names and classroom data.
Ease4★
The initial class setup takes a couple of steps, but the actual grouping workflow is clear: create roster, open Group Generator, choose size/count, generate. Guest mode helps a lot because no signup is needed for the basic workflow.
Design4.2★
Modern, readable classroom-tool interface with a good whiteboard/presentation feel. It is busier than a single-purpose generator because it is part of a whole classroom tools suite.
Account friction4.6★
The reviewed workflow works in guest mode without signup. Account/pro upgrade is mainly for cloud sync, multi-device use, and paid classroom-suite features.
Review notes

Summary

One of the stronger classroom-specific group generators reviewed: real roster management, constraints, presentation mode, and usable exports without requiring signup. Privacy is decent but not local-only because the site uses analytics/session tooling and cloud sync is part of the product.

Pricing: Free guest workflow with browser-only storage and no signup required. Pro is advertised at $2.99/month for cloud saving, multi-device sync, AI tools, support, and related classroom-suite features.

Pros

  • No-signup guest workflow
  • Saved class rosters in browser storage
  • Always-together and do-not-group-with rules
  • Gender distribution modes
  • Group by size or count
  • Presentation/whiteboard workflow
  • Download and share/import/export surfaces

Cons

  • Broader classroom-tool suite adds UI complexity
  • Analytics/session replay/support scripts around roster workflows
  • Cloud sync requires account/pro path
  • No multi-session repeat optimization
  • Not Zoom/spreadsheet-export focused

Quality3.6★
Above simple random splitters because multiple pools, restrictions, and roles can materially improve classroom groups. Not a solver-grade repeat/balance optimizer.
Features4★
Strong: multiple name lists, group count or names-per-group, roles, restrictions, group-name modes, colors, and generation history. Missing true multi-session repeat optimization and detailed diagnostics.
Exports4.5★
Excellent for a free tool: copy, PDF, print, and history are visible in the result workflow.
Privacy3.1★
No account required and state is stored locally, but the page loads Google Tag Manager and has no obvious dedicated privacy page for the tool.
Ease3.7★
The basic flow works, but the settings panel is dense. It trades simplicity for real controls.
Design3.1★
Plain and utilitarian. Not beautiful, but the controls are organized well enough.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

One of the better lightweight classroom grouping tools reviewed. Not a full multi-session optimizer, but it has real classroom grouping controls and strong outputs.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

Pros

  • Multiple name lists/pools
  • Number of groups or names per group
  • Role assignment
  • Restrictions / keep-apart style controls
  • Configurable group names
  • Copy, PDF, and print
  • Generation history
  • No account required

Cons

  • Dense utilitarian UI
  • No true multi-session repeat minimization
  • No pair matrix or diagnostics
  • No spreadsheet/Zoom-specific export
  • Google Tag Manager present
  • Privacy documentation is thin
#7 4★

Good-Enough Golfers↗ opens tool website

Simple social-golfer-style multi-round schedules

Free, no accountmulti-roundconstraintsprivacy-friendlystrong-exports
Multi-round: YesRepeat encounter limits: PartialUnique-contact: YesHard keep-apart: YesAttribute balance: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: PartialAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: PartialGender/label balancing: PartialGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: YesNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: PartialPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: PartialPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: PartialResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: YesBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: YesSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: PartialSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: YesCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: YesUnique-contact: YesVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: PartialZoom CSV: No
Quality4★
Good for its niche. It uses a genetic/near-solver approach and conflict score to reduce repeated pairings across rounds, with hard and soft pair constraints. It is not guaranteed optimal and can struggle with hard cases, but it beats almost every simple random splitter for multi-round mixing.
Features3.8★
Very strong for social-golfer-style repeat minimization: group count, group size, number of rounds, group leaders, player names, hard never-together pairs, softer prefer-split groups, conflict score, local autosave, print, and CSV export. Weak outside that niche: no arbitrary capacities, partial attendance, rich attributes, per-session constraints, or UI-guided balancing.
Exports3.5★
Useful but not flashy: clean print view and CSV download with a pivot-style view of player/group sequences. No share links, Zoom export, PDF button, or visual reporting beyond the conflict score.
Privacy4.8★
Excellent. The page explicitly says names are never sent to servers and all processing happens on the user’s own computer. No obvious ads, Google Analytics, cookies, or tracking scripts were visible; source is public. Only caveat is the CDN-loaded Immutable dependency.
Ease3.2★
The sliders and text boxes are manageable, but the mental model is mathy. Users need to understand groups, people per group, rounds, leaders, conflict score, and pair constraints. Not hard for technical facilitators, less friendly for casual teachers.
Design2.4★
Plain and dated. It is usable, but it looks like a small open-source solver demo rather than a polished product.
Account friction5★
No account, no signup, no payment. Just open the page and run it.
Review notes

Summary

Strong at one important thing: multi-round repeat minimization. The UI is rough and constraint support is narrow, but this is much more serious than basic random splitters.

Pricing: Free and open source; optional tip jar. No account or subscription required.

Pros

  • Multi-round repeat minimization
  • Hard never-together pair constraints
  • Soft prefer-split groups
  • Group leaders option
  • Conflict score
  • CSV export and print view
  • Runs locally with excellent privacy
  • Free and open source

Cons

  • Dated solver-demo UI
  • Not beginner-friendly
  • No partial attendance or per-session setup
  • No rich attribute balancing
  • No share links or Zoom export
  • Near-solver, not guaranteed optimal
#8 4★

Grouper.school↗ opens tool website

Account-based classroom grouping with Google Classroom/Clever rosters and student attributes

Free, no accountconstraints

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: YesGoogle Sheets import: PartialHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: PartialImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: NoPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: YesPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: YesRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: PartialSaved class roster import: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: YesScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: NoCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality4★
Strong single-session classroom grouping quality because groups are built around student attributes and teacher knowledge rather than pure randomization. Not a multi-round repeat minimizer.
Features4.1★
Strong classroom feature set: Google Classroom/Clever rosters, custom attributes, gender/proficiency/leader/conflict examples, do-not-group-with, absent marking, recent grouping reuse, Live Mode, and paid expansion to unlimited attributes/roles.
Exports3.3★
Good classroom presentation and saved/recent grouping workflow, but no obvious spreadsheet/Zoom/export-first output surface from the public materials.
Privacy4★
Good for a hosted education product: no advertising, no sale of personal data, student records not used for targeted advertising, COPPA/FERPA language, encryption, and vendor list. Hosted/account-based with analytics, Intercom, Google, Posthog, and roster integrations.
Ease3.4★
The product looks easy once set up, but signup and roster integration come before actual grouping. That is more friction than paste-and-generate tools.
Design4.6★
Very polished, classroom-specific interface and landing page. The attribute demos and Live Mode positioning are clear.
Account friction1.6★
Account is required. Email signup sends a magic link; Google/Clever are also offered.
Review notes

Summary

A strong dedicated classroom grouping product if an account-based roster workflow is acceptable. It is polished and privacy-conscious for a hosted education tool, but less attractive for users who want instant no-login grouping or local-only roster handling.

Pricing: Free plan includes randomized/custom groups, smart groups using up to two student attributes, do-not-group-with, absent marking, recent groupings for 7 days, Live Mode, Google Classroom/Clever auto-roster, and up to 40 students per class. Pro is advertised at $6.99/month per teacher annually and adds unlimited attributes, roles, permanent saved groupings, larger classes, AI-assisted imports, co-teacher support, and priority support.

Pros

  • Polished classroom-specific product
  • Google Classroom and Clever roster import
  • Attribute-based smart grouping
  • Do-not-group-with and absence handling
  • Live Mode presentation
  • Free tier with useful grouping features
  • Strong education privacy language

Cons

  • Account required
  • Hosted roster workflow
  • No obvious export-first workflow
  • Permanent saved groupings and unlimited attributes are paid
  • No multi-session repeat optimization
#9 4★

Shuffly↗ opens tool website

Skill- and gender-balanced random teams with strong free exports

Free with ads/upsellsconstraintsstrong-exports
Attribute balance: YesBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialResult quality diagnostics: PartialAttribute total/sum: YesCopy results to clipboard: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: YesCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: YesFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: YesGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: YesMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: PartialSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: PartialSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality4★
Balances team size and skill totals and can incorporate gender and same-surname spread. It reports skill totals, but it does not optimize repeats, enforce arbitrary constraints, or expose solver quality metrics.
Features4★
Strong for a lightweight team generator: skill balancing, gender balancing, same-surname split option, editable team names, manual paste entry, reshuffle, exports, saved participants/groups/setups for accounts, and paid file import/PDF. Missing hard arbitrary constraints, capacities, multi-round repeat planning, self-join, APIs, and deeper diagnostics.
Exports4.4★
Excellent for the category. Live results exposed text copy plus visible image, PDF, XLSX, and CSV controls; free-plan copy/image/XLSX/CSV exports are advertised, while PDF is paid.
Privacy3.4★
Better than ad-tech randomizer sites but not perfect. The no-account FAQ claims local/browser processing and no server storage for anonymous use. The privacy policy also mentions Firebase storage for logged-in data, Stripe for payments, Google Analytics, Microsoft Clarity, IP/location collection, and localStorage preferences.
Ease4.2★
The three-step flow is clear, accepts one participant per line, parses skill/gender annotations, and worked without signup. File import is visible but paid, and advanced saved-data workflows require registration.
Design4.4★
Modern, readable, and purpose-built. Parsed participants, team settings, options, results, and export controls are all presented clearly.
Account friction4.5★
No account was needed for the tested generation flow. Registration is optional for saving participant libraries, groups, setup history, and account-based reloads.
Review notes

Summary

One of the stronger lightweight web tools: real skill/gender balancing and excellent free exports, but not a multi-round or constraint-heavy optimizer.

Pricing: Free plan supports up to 20 participants, 2 groups, manual entry, skill balancing, copy, PNG, Excel/CSV export, reshuffle, and 1 saved setup. Base is €3.99/month with 100 participants, 10 groups, file import, PDF export, and 5 saved setups. Premium is €7.99/month with 999 participants, 50 groups, unlimited saved setups, custom integrations, and advanced algorithms.

Pros

  • Generated balanced teams successfully in live testing
  • Skill-based balancing from inline numeric ratings
  • Optional gender balance
  • Same-surname split option
  • Custom team names and reshuffle
  • Copy, image, Excel, and CSV outputs on the free plan
  • No account required for basic generation
  • Saved participants, groups, setups, and history for registered users

Cons

  • Free plan limits shuffles to 20 participants
  • Excel/CSV file import and PDF export require paid plans
  • No multi-round repeat minimization
  • No arbitrary hard keep-together/keep-apart constraints
  • No custom group capacities or partial attendance
  • Uses analytics/Clarity and stores account data in Firebase for logged-in users
#10 4★

Team Shake↗ opens tool website

Mobile-first balanced team generation for teachers and coaches

Paid or subscriptionconstraintsstrong-exports
Hard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: YesAttribute balance: YesCustom group capacities: YesPartial attendance by session: YesNo account: PartialCSV export: YesPresentation: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: PartialCopy results to clipboard: PartialCustom group capacities: YesDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: YesFixed person placement: PartialGender/label balancing: YesGoogle Sheets import: PartialHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: YesImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: PartialPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: YesPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: PartialPresentation: PartialRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: NoSaved class roster import: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: YesSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: YesCSV import: YesStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.8★
Good single-session team quality. It can balance by strength/gender and honor keep-together/keep-apart rules where possible, which is far above plain random splitters. It does not optimize repeated sessions or provide solver diagnostics, so it is not in the same class as a multi-round optimizer.
Features4★
Strong for group generation. It supports 1–64 teams, selection by team count or team size, balanced or random teams, strength balancing, gender balancing, separate male/female teams, equal-ability groups, absences, keep-together subteams, keep-apart oppose teams, saved rosters, configurable team names/colors, and list import/export. Missing the higher-end stuff: multi-session scheduling, repeat minimization, diagnostics, and arbitrary weighted constraints.
Exports4.3★
Very good output surface for a mobile app: share teams as text, image, CSV, or Excel; email/social sharing; print support; saved lists; backups for moving lists/settings between devices. It lacks web-native share pages, Zoom breakout export, and schedule-style outputs.
Privacy4.6★
Strong. The app privacy policy says Rhine-o does not collect, store, or process user information; entered names are not shared with the developer; App Store privacy says data is not collected; and COPPA compliance is stated. Caveats are normal platform backup/sync services and user-triggered sharing through email/social apps.
Ease3.7★
Basic use is simple once the app is installed: add names, choose number of teams or people per team, and generate. The advanced controls add setup work, and the app-store/mobile-only workflow is more friction than a browser tool.
Design2.8★
Functional but dated. The app is clearly built around utility rather than polish. The mobile workflow is practical for teachers/coaches, but it does not feel modern.
Account friction3.2★
No Team Shake account is needed, which is good. It is not an instant web tool: users need an app-store account/device, installation, and usually a paid app purchase.
Review notes

Summary

One of the stronger actual group generators on the list for single-session teams. It lacks multi-session/repeat optimization and modern web sharing, but its grouping controls are legitimately useful.

Pricing: Paid app-store purchase; developer lists it at $0.99 USD, with actual pricing varying by platform/region. No separate Team Shake account or subscription is needed after purchase.

Pros

  • Creates arbitrary 1–64 teams
  • Can choose by team count or team size
  • Strength and gender balancing
  • Keep-together and keep-apart rules
  • Absent/present handling
  • CSV/XLS import and export
  • Strong no-data-collected privacy posture

Cons

  • Paid app-store workflow
  • Mobile/native rather than browser-first
  • Dated interface
  • No multi-session repeat minimization
  • No quality diagnostics or pair matrix
  • No Zoom breakout export
#11 4★

TeamMaker App↗ opens tool website

Local, no-account team making with strong classroom presentation and exports

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: PartialGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: YesImage/PDF export: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: PartialNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: PartialPresentation: YesRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: PartialResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: YesScenario bundle: YesSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: NoCSV import: YesStorage management/cleanup: YesTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.2★
Together/apart constraints and manual editing make results more usable than random splitters. It does not balance attributes or optimize repeated pairings across sessions.
Features4★
Excellent practical feature depth for a free browser tool: CSV import, random picker, together/apart constraints, manual drag editing, staging, projector mode, seating plans, multiple saved teams, JSON backup/restore, and export. Missing advanced optimization like attributes and repeat minimization.
Exports4.4★
Very strong output surface: JPG, PDF, JSON backups, team JSON import/export, seating-plan JSON, and projector mode. It lacks spreadsheet/Zoom CSV export and a copy button.
Privacy3.7★
Good for a hosted free tool because roster/team data is stored in the browser and no account is required. The page loads Google Analytics/Tag Manager, external CDNs, and Ko-fi resources, so it is not a fully private/offline tool.
Ease4.2★
The main workflow is easy: paste names, load roster, choose team mode/size, generate. The app has many extra panels, so it is more complex than one-button splitters, but the basics are clear.
Design4.1★
Modern, responsive, and practical. The interface is busier than tiny randomizers because it includes rosters, teams, seating, export, projector mode, and saved teams.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A strong practical team maker: local storage, constraints, projector mode, seating plans, and PDF/JPG/JSON exports. It lacks attribute balancing and repeat optimization, but it is far above normal random splitters.

Pricing: Free, with a Ko-fi tip link. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • CSV import and pasted-name roster loading
  • Together/apart constraints
  • Manual team editing with staging
  • Projector mode
  • Seating plan builder
  • JPG/PDF exports and JSON backup/restore
  • Browser-local storage for rosters and teams

Cons

  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance across generated rounds
  • No spreadsheet or Zoom breakout CSV export
  • More complex than a tiny one-shot randomizer
  • Uses external analytics/CDN resources
#12 3.9★

Instructron Group Generator↗ opens tool website

Teachers who want quick random groups with basic balancing and keep-apart rules

Free with ads/upsellsconstraintsprivacy-friendlystrong-exports
Hard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: PartialAttribute balance: YesCustom group capacities: PartialPartial attendance by session: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: PartialCSV export: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: PartialFixed person placement: PartialGender/label balancing: PartialGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: PartialImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: PartialPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: PartialPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: PartialPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: PartialResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: YesSolver telemetry/result metadata: PartialSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: YesCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: PartialZoom CSV: No
Quality3.6★
Clearly above plain random splitting because it can balance or cluster by performance level, honor keep-apart rules, prefer keep-together pairs, and use profile signals in upgraded workflows. Not a social-golfer-style repeat optimizer and likely heuristic rather than a transparent solver.
Features3.8★
Strong for a classroom grouping tool: group size/count modes, performance-level strategy, keep-apart rules, keep-together preferences, drag-and-drop edits, locked groups, manual add/remove groups, CSV export, print/PDF, initials-only and neutral-color display. Advanced profile signals and classroom import are gated behind account/upgrade paths. No multi-session repeat minimization or pair matrix.
Exports4★
Good output surface: CSV export, print/PDF, editable result cards, initials-only display, neutral-color display, and local/account plan saving in the broader workflow. No Zoom breakout export or public share link.
Privacy4★
Better than most free web tools. The policy says no selling, no targeted advertising, no student-data model training, minimal student identifiers, encryption, subprocessors, and teacher controls. The page uses analytics.instructron.com and Cloudflare, and account/classroom use stores roster/profile/activity data, so it is not a local-only privacy story.
Ease4.1★
The basic flow is clear: add names, choose group size or group count, optionally set levels and rules, then generate. There are more controls than a simple splitter, but they are relevant and mostly understandable.
Design4.2★
Modern, readable, and teacher-oriented. The page is much more polished than most utility randomizers, with sensible controls and result cards. The upgrade prompts are visible but not overwhelming.
Account friction4★
The quick group generator works without an account, which is good. Classroom import, saved plans, and richer profile-based grouping push users toward signup and paid plans.
Review notes

Summary

One of the better modern classroom group generators. It is not a multi-session repeat optimizer, and some of the best profile-based features sit behind the account/upgrade path, but the free tool already beats normal random splitters.

Pricing: Free no-account group generator for quick manual use. Account creation is pushed for classroom import and saved/reusable plans; paid plans appear to unlock deeper student filters/profile signals and broader Instructron classroom features.

Pros

  • No-account quick mode
  • Group size or number-of-groups mode
  • Balanced or similar performance grouping
  • Keep-apart rules
  • Keep-together preferences
  • Drag-and-drop edits and lock controls
  • CSV export and print/PDF
  • Stronger privacy posture than ad-heavy randomizers

Cons

  • Best roster/profile features require account or upgrade
  • No multi-session repeat optimization
  • No pair matrix or repeat diagnostics
  • No Zoom breakout export
  • Hosted classroom data if using full workflow
  • Heuristic quality is not very transparent
#13 3.8★

Quality3.3★
Above normal random splitters because it can avoid recent repeats where possible and Pro adds keep-apart/leaders. Not a full optimizer.
Features3.7★
Good lightweight depth: pairs/trios, avoid last grouping, random picker, local saved lists, copy/download/print, plus Pro keep-apart pairs, leaders, larger/custom groups, export, and saved lists.
Exports4★
Strong for this tier: copy, download, print, and Pro CSV/clean layouts.
Privacy4.6★
Strong. The page claims local browser storage for names and no account for free use; no obvious third-party scripts were loaded on the tested page.
Ease4★
The quick path is clear, though the add-names/generate flow is a little more button-heavy than the simplest tools.
Design3.8★
Clean, teacher-friendly, and focused. Not flashy, but much better than utility-site clutter.
Account friction1.5★
Free use needs no account. Pro/payment is required for almost anything, even creating groups of 4 or more people.
Review notes

Summary

A genuinely good lightweight group maker. The free tier is limited, but privacy, output controls, and paid constraint features are better than most random splitters.

Pricing: Free basic tool. Buddy Matcher Pro is advertised as a £3.99 one-off upgrade for larger/custom groups, keep-apart pairs, leaders, saved lists, export, and print layouts.

Pros

  • No account for free use
  • Names saved locally in browser
  • Pairs/trios in free tool
  • Avoid recent repeats where possible
  • Copy, download, and print
  • Saved lists
  • Pro keep-apart pairs and leaders
  • One-off Pro price

Cons

  • Groups of 4+ and custom sizes are Pro
  • Keep-apart and leaders are Pro
  • Not a full multi-session solver
  • No attribute balancing beyond leaders
  • No pair matrix or diagnostics
  • Free workflow has extra buttons

Quality3★
Above plain random splitting because avoid/keep constraints and seeded/security modes are real. It is a bounded-retry random assignment, not a full optimizer for balance or repeats.
Features3.7★
Very good for a one-session random team tool: secure CSPRNG mode, seeded reproducibility, deduplication, team count/size modes, keep-together and avoid-pair constraints, custom team names, and settings-only share URLs. No multi-round repeat optimization, attendance by session, attribute balancing, or pair matrix.
Exports4.6★
Excellent output surface for this tier: copy all, copy each team, download TXT/CSV/JSON, clear results, and copy a settings URL that excludes names by default.
Privacy4.1★
Good for roster privacy because names/results run locally and are not uploaded. The site has consent-based analytics/advertising cookies, Google tags, and an ad/cookie policy, so it is not completely tracker-free.
Ease4.2★
The basic flow is easy: paste names, choose team count or team size, generate. Advanced options add complexity but are hidden well enough.
Design4★
Clean, modern, and information-dense without being chaotic. The cookie banner exists, but the actual tool UI is solid.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

Strong for a simple random team generator: local processing, real constraint options, reproducible mode, and excellent exports. It does not solve multi-session or attribute-balancing problems.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

Pros

  • Runs locally in browser
  • Secure CSPRNG mode
  • Seeded reproducible mode
  • Team count or team size
  • Keep-together and avoid-pair constraints
  • Copy all / copy team
  • TXT, CSV, and JSON downloads
  • Settings-only share URL

Cons

  • No multi-session repeat optimization
  • No attribute balancing
  • Constraints are best-effort, not a full solver guarantee
  • No Zoom-specific export
  • Consent-based analytics/ads are present
  • No saved rosters/workspaces

Summary

A strong browser-side random team generator with real advanced rules and usable outputs, though not a multi-round optimizer.

Pricing: Free web tool; no account required in tested flow.

Pros

  • Generated tested roster successfully
  • TXT/CSV file input
  • Number-of-teams and team-size modes
  • Named team presets
  • Locks, exclusions, and skill levels
  • Copy Results and Download TXT
  • Reshuffle same teams
  • No account required

Cons

  • Line and CSV-style input
  • Participant attributes and weights

  • Keep-groups-together option

  • Multiple rounds with repeat-pairing reduction
  • Custom team names/themes
  • Copy, CSV, and JSON outputs

Cons

  • No detailed solver/scoring report

Attribute balance: YesCustom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesResult quality diagnostics: YesCopy results to clipboard: YesGender/label balancing: PartialPaste names: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: PartialGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: YesBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.5★
Better than random-only tools because it can balance skill and explain imbalance.
Features3.5★
Skill levels, roles, snake draft, team-size mode, and balance reporting are real depth.
Exports3.2★
Copy teams and export a formatted report; no spreadsheet-specific CSV export seen.
Privacy4.5★
No observed third-party requests in the tested page and a no-tracking positioning.
Ease3.6★
Adding people individually is slower than paste-first tools, but sample data and controls are clear.
Design3.6★
Clean utility UI with a lot of surrounding navigation.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

A genuinely useful lightweight team generator. Skill balancing and diagnostics make it stand out among simple web tools.

Pricing: Free web tool. The site presents itself as no-account and no-tracking.

Pros

  • Team-count and team-size modes
  • Optional skill levels
  • Role labels
  • Snake-draft skill balancing
  • Balance report
  • Copy teams
  • Export formatted report
  • No account required

Cons

  • No bulk textarea paste workflow visible
  • No CSV/spreadsheet export
  • No hard keep-together or keep-apart rules
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No saved scenarios
#18 3.7★

Classroomscreen Group Maker↗ opens tool website

Teachers already using Classroomscreen who want visual classroom groups, saved name lists, exclusions, and manual adjustment

Free, no accountconstraintsprivacy-friendly
Hard keep-apart: YesCustom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: PartialPresentation: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesEditable/random team names: YesFixed person placement: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: PartialGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: PartialPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: PartialPresentation: YesRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: PartialSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: YesWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.2★
Above random splitters because teacher exclusions/priorities and manual drag/drop adjustment materially improve classroom assignments. Not solver-grade repeat minimization.
Features3.8★
Strong for classroom grouping: saved name lists, group count/size controls, editable names/colors, rotate groups, drag/drop movement, and keep-apart/prioritize rules. No multi-round repeat optimizer or pair matrix.
Exports2.6★
Good visual display and screen-saving workflow, but not a strong export tool. No clear CSV/spreadsheet/PDF group export surface for results.
Privacy4.2★
Much better than ad-funded utilities: no ads, explicit no-sale language, EU company, and clear processor/security disclosures. Student names can be stored when using accounts, so it is not local-only.
Ease3.4★
The widget is approachable for teachers, but using it as a group maker means entering the Classroomscreen app/settings flow rather than a single standalone form.
Design4.4★
Excellent classroom-first visual design. This is built to be projected and used during lessons.
Account friction2.8★
A no-account app flow exists, but saved name lists and practical reuse require Basic/Pro account features; Pro is needed for saving/sharing screens beyond the free limits.
Review notes

Summary

A strong classroom grouping widget with meaningful teacher controls. Not a multi-session optimizer, and the platform/account context adds friction, but it is much more serious than basic random splitters.

Pricing: Basic is free and includes all widgets plus saving up to three name lists. Pro is about $36/year and adds saving/sharing screens, folders, unlimited name lists, custom backgrounds, widget themes, and other classroom-screen features.

Pros

  • Polished classroom projection UI
  • Saved name lists
  • Group size/group count controls
  • Editable group names and colors
  • Keep-apart/prioritize rules
  • Drag-and-drop manual adjustment
  • No advertising on the app

Cons

  • Not a standalone paste-and-export tool
  • Account needed for saved list workflows
  • Pro needed for stronger saving/sharing
  • No CSV/spreadsheet result export
  • No multi-session repeat optimization
  • Student names may be stored in account workflows
#19 3.7★

digtools Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Browser-side teams with skills, NG pairs, leaders, save/load, CSV, text, and image output

Free, no accountconstraintsstrong-exports
Hard keep-apart: YesBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialCopy results to clipboard: YesDeterministic seed / reproducibility: PartialGender/label balancing: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: PartialEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: PartialGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: YesUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.5★
Better than random-only tools because skill balancing and exclusion pairs can shape assignments.
Features4★
Strong for a free tool: split modes, skill balancing, NG/exclusion pairs, organizer picks, saved lists, reshuffle, and local browser processing claims.
Exports4.2★
Text, CSV, and image export are visible after generation.
Privacy1.5★
The tool says data is processed locally. The page also loads Google ads/consent, Tag Manager/Analytics, Cloudflare challenge, and related ad infrastructure.
Ease3.7★
Simple lists work quickly, while advanced conditions are available without overwhelming the first screen.
Design3.8★
Clean tool panel, clear result cards, and good explanatory documentation.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A strong free browser tool with real advanced grouping controls and useful exports.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • By team count or team size
  • Skill balancing
  • Exclusion/NG pairs
  • Leader/organizer picks
  • Local saved lists
  • Reshuffle
  • Text, CSV, and image outputs

Cons

  • No multi-round repeat optimization verified
  • No pair matrix
  • Consent/ad stack is heavy
  • Some console errors appeared during testing
#20 3.7★

Randomizer.uk Create Groups↗ opens tool website

Multi-criteria random groups with proof/share/export options

Free with ads/upsellsconstraintsstrong-exports
Hard keep-apart: YesAttribute balance: YesCustom group capacities: PartialBrowser: PartialNo account: YesCSV export: YesResult quality diagnostics: PartialCopy results to clipboard: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: PartialFixed person placement: YesGender/label balancing: YesGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: YesPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: PartialSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: PartialSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: YesCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: PartialUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.4★
Better than basic random splitters because pots, separation, and fixed placement materially shape assignments. Randomized retries, not solver-grade optimization or multi-round repeat minimization.
Features3.8★
Strong for a random group generator: multiple pots, separation rules, imposed group placement, group-leader-style placement, and repeated random draws until criteria match. Missing multi-session repeat minimization, pair matrix, soft weighted preferences, and polished diagnostics.
Exports4.2★
Strong output surface: result display, copy, CSV/TXT/HTML-style downloads, draw links, and proof/publication language. Some sharing/proof/customization features are account or paid-adjacent, and there is no Zoom-specific export.
Privacy3.2★
Better than ad-tech-heavy tools in the tested page because no obvious third-party requests were loaded. Not local-only: form/share/proof/account flows imply server-side handling, and the legal/privacy posture is not as transparent as the best local/browser-only tools.
Ease2.9★
The interface is more pleasant and structured than many old randomizer pages, but the powerful parts depend on learning pots, bracket separation syntax, and curly-brace fixed-placement syntax.
Design3.4★
Nicer than the initial score implied: the page is plain, compact, and reasonably well organized for a utility tool. It is not a polished planning workspace, but the controls are clearer than many random-group generators.
Account friction4.2★
Basic group creation can be used without registration. Account or paid flows are pushed for sharing, layout customization, proof/certification, and GDPR/publication settings.
Review notes

Summary

The same capable multicriteria random grouping engine as Zufallsgenerator, localized in English. Pleasant for a utility randomizer and much stronger than basic splitters, but not a full planning optimizer.

Pricing: Free basic group/team generation with no registration required. Paid/dedicated publication options appear for sharing, layout customization, proof/certification, and GDPR/publication settings.

Pros

  • Multiple pots/category pools
  • Keep-apart syntax
  • Fixed group placement syntax
  • Repeated draws until criteria match
  • CSV/TXT/HTML-style downloads
  • Copy and draw-link options
  • No obvious third-party requests on tested page

Cons

  • Syntax-driven controls take some learning
  • No true multi-session repeat optimization
  • No pair matrix or detailed diagnostics
  • Sharing/proof/customization features are account or paid-adjacent
  • Not a modern planning workspace
#21 3.7★

Randraw Multicriteria Team↗ opens tool website

No-account multicriteria grouping with pots, separation rules, and exports

Free, no accountconstraintsprivacy-friendlystrong-exports

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: PartialAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: PartialGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: PartialPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: PartialUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.3★
Constraint-aware single-round random generation, but no visible optimization diagnostics or repeat-minimization mode.
Features4★
Pots, distribute-apart syntax, imposed team membership, share links, and exports make it much stronger than simple random splitters.
Exports4.1★
TXT, CSV, HTML, and share-link workflows were visible after generation.
Privacy4★
The live test observed only randraw.com requests.
Ease3.3★
Powerful syntax and pots require reading instructions; basic pasted-name generation works.
Design2.7★
Functional but old-fashioned and less approachable than modern tools.
Account friction4.2★
Generation works without an account, though account creation is promoted for saved links/history.
Review notes

Summary

One of the better no-account single-round constraint generators, despite an old-fashioned UI.

Pricing: Free for ephemeral no-account draws; account/signup is promoted for saved sharing/history.

Pros

  • No account required for basic generation
  • Pots for distributing categories
  • Keep-apart/distribute syntax
  • Imposed team placement syntax
  • TXT/CSV/HTML export
  • Ephemeral share links

Cons

  • Older UI
  • Rule syntax is not beginner-friendly
  • No repeat minimization
  • No quality diagnostics
  • Saved history requires account
#22 3.7★

Zapadisk Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Balancing sport/player teams by level, gender, and multi-day attendance

Free, no accountconstraints
Hard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialAttribute balance: YesPartial attendance by session: YesBrowser: YesNo account: YesResult quality diagnostics: YesAttribute total/sum: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: PartialCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: YesGoogle Sheets import: PartialHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: YesPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: PartialPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: YesBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: PartialSolver tuning controls: YesCSV export: NoCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: YesZoom CSV: No
Quality4★
Much better than random-only splitters for its target use case because it optimizes level, gender, attendance per day, and gender per day, then reports remaining differences and score. It is not a broad constraint solver.
Features3.8★
Strong for its niche: spreadsheet paste, numeric level balancing, gender balancing, attendance-by-day balancing, weighted criteria, local-search runs, generated sample data, score/difference diagnostics, and hacky together/apart control through attendance values. No generic attributes, custom capacities, saved rosters, multi-round schedules, or modern result editing.
Exports2★
Results, summaries, and diagnostics are displayed on the page, but there is no copy button, CSV/spreadsheet export, print/PDF, share link, Zoom export, or saved result history.
Privacy3.8★
Good but not perfect. The optimizer appears to run locally in the browser and the project is open source, with no observed generation API upload. The page still loads Google Analytics and has no clear privacy policy on the tool page.
Ease2.5★
Powerful but not beginner-simple. Users must prepare spreadsheet rows in the exact name/level/gender/attendance-day format and understand runs, team count, and objective weights.
Design2.1★
Old, sparse Materialize-style page. It is readable and transparent, but not polished, mobile-first, or friendly compared with modern group tools.
Account friction5★
No account, signup, or payment is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A niche but genuinely smarter team balancer. It is rough and export-light, but much more assignment-aware than ordinary random team splitters.

Pricing: Free open-source web tool. No account is required.

Pros

  • Balances level, gender, and attendance by day
  • Weighted local-search optimization rather than one-shot random split
  • Spreadsheet-style paste input
  • Detailed team difference and score diagnostics
  • Runs locally in the browser
  • Open source and no account required

Cons

  • Strict, non-obvious input format
  • Old sparse UI
  • No copy/export buttons
  • No saved rosters or result history
  • Narrow player/team model
  • Uses Google Analytics and lacks clear privacy documentation

Review notes

Summary

The same capable multicriteria random grouping engine as Randomizer.uk, localized for German-speaking users. Pleasant for a utility randomizer and much stronger than basic splitters, but not a full planning optimizer.

Pricing: Free basic tool. Account/promoted paid options appear for sharing, layout customization, proof/certification, and full GDPR/publication settings.

Pros

  • Multiple pots/category pools
  • Keep-apart syntax
  • Fixed group placement syntax
  • Repeated draws until criteria match
  • CSV/TXT/HTML-style downloads
  • Copy and draw-link options
  • No obvious third-party requests on tested page

Cons

  • German-only UI
  • Syntax-driven controls take some learning
  • No true multi-session repeat optimization
  • No pair matrix or detailed diagnostics
  • Sharing/proof/customization features are account or paid-adjacent
  • Not a modern planning workspace

Quality3.3★
Better than random-only tools because skill-balanced and captains modes can improve sports-style team fairness.
Features4★
Skill ratings, balanced snake draft, captains, team-name styles, leftover handling, copy modes, history, and balance score are strong for a free casual tool.
Exports3.7★
Rosters/names/CSV copy modes and Copy All are available; download-file export was not verified.
Privacy1.8★
No account is needed, but Ahrefs, Google ads/consent, Analytics, Tag Manager, and fonts loaded.
Ease3.2★
