Design3.7★
Modern, dense UI with clear team cards, preview, stats, and history.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A feature-rich free team generator for sports/classroom teams, short of true constraints or multi-round optimization.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Bulk add
  • Skill ratings
  • Random, balanced snake-draft, and captains modes
  • By teams or players per team
  • Team-name styles
  • Leftover handling
  • Copy modes, history, and balance score

Cons

  • No keep-apart/keep-together constraints
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No full diagnostic matrix
  • Default sample roster can confuse use
  • Ad/analytics scripts present
#25 3.6★

GroupEng↗ opens tool website

Open-source command-line grouping with distribute, aggregate, cluster, and balance rules

Free, no accountconstraints
Attribute balance: YesCustom group capacities: PartialNo account: YesCSV export: PartialResult quality diagnostics: YesAttribute total/sum: YesGender/label balancing: YesMinimum group sizes: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: YesCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: YesGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: YesMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: YesBrowser: NoSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: PartialSoft prefer-together: PartialSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: PartialCSV import: YesStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: PartialZoom CSV: No
Quality4★
Meaningfully more intelligent than random splitters because it optimizes rule satisfaction and balance.
Features4.1★
Strong assignment model for its age: distribute, aggregate, cluster, balance, rule priorities, CSV data, random runs, and output statistics.
Exports3.3★
Outputs groupings and statistics, but not modern browser exports, share links, or visual reports.
Privacy4.6★
Local/open-source execution is a major advantage for sensitive class data.
Ease1.7★
High friction compared with web tools: users must download/run Python and prepare CSV plus a rule description file.
Design1.4★
Documentation-style page, not an interactive product UI.
Account friction4★
No account, but installation/configuration friction is real.
Review notes

Summary

Powerful for technical instructors who can use files and Python, unsuitable for quick no-code grouping.

Pricing: Free and open source under AGPLv3.

Pros

  • Open source AGPLv3
  • Runs locally
  • CSV-based class data
  • Distribute attributes across groups
  • Aggregate matching attributes
  • Cluster underrepresented students
  • Balance numeric scores
  • Outputs rule-satisfaction statistics

Cons

  • Not a web app
  • Requires Python and config files
  • Old/experimental project
  • No modern UI
  • No built-in repeat-pairing workflow

Quality3.4★
Better than pure random-only splitters because it supports equal groups, gender/label balancing, simple preset together/separate rules, and representative picking. Fundamentally a single-session randomizer, not a repeat-minimizing or constraint-optimizing scheduler.
Features3.6★
Strong for a random team picker: pasted names, CSV import, gender/label balancing, custom team names, simple preset together/separate grouping, representatives, self-join links, saved files, presentation settings, and visual customization. It is not a serious multi-session optimizer.
Exports4.4★
Very strong output options for this category: CSV download, result image export/share, result links, share links, and browser/cloud file storage. No Zoom-specific export, Excel-native export, or true solver diagnostics.
Privacy1.6★
Bad for sensitive rosters. The tool uses Google Analytics plus AdThrive/CafeMedia advertising infrastructure and a consent-manager stack. The privacy policy says user content/inputs, IP address, device/software identifiers, analytics data, advertising data, and share-link data may be collected; share links include entries and are stored in an online database.
Ease3.8★
The basic flow works, but it is not as simple as the best plain splitters. The wheel UI, menus, file/storage controls, self-join mode, ads, and settings create a lot of surface area before you get to the group result.
Design3.7★
Visually more engaging than most utility randomizers, especially for live reveals. The downside is that the interface is busy, ad-supported, and cluttered compared with focused group-planning tools.
Account friction3.8★
Basic host grouping works without an account. Persistent cloud file storage, subscriber storage, and some self-join/file workflows push users toward login/subscriber storage, so it is not frictionless.
Review notes

Summary

A capable presentation-focused random team picker with more features than most randomizers, but the busy UI and ad-tech-heavy privacy keep it below the serious planning tools.

Pricing: Free basic team picker with ads. Account/subscriber storage is relevant for persistent cloud files and larger quotas, but the basic team-picking workflow is free.

Pros

  • Visual wheel-style presentation
  • CSV import and CSV/image/result-link outputs
  • Gender or label balancing
  • Self-join grouping link
  • Custom team names and representative picking
  • Simple preset together/separate rules

Cons

  • Ad-tech and analytics privacy concerns
  • No multi-session repeat optimization
  • Advanced storage/self-join features push toward account/cloud use
  • Busy interface compared with simple list splitters

Exports3.8★

Design3.4★

Review notes

Summary

A credible one-off team grouping tool with leader distribution and export support.

Pricing: Free public tool. No account is required for the tested workflow; the broader site has login/sign-up navigation.

Pros

  • No account required for generation
  • Separate leader/exclusive-member input
  • Equal or random distribution mode
  • Copy results
  • Excel export
  • Readable team cards

Cons

  • No repeat avoidance
  • No attribute balancing beyond leader spread
  • No saved roster workflow
  • No diagnostics
  • Broader site promotes login/sign-up

Quality3.3★

Exports4★

Ease3.6★

Design3.4★

Account friction5★

Review notes

Summary

One of the stronger utility-site team generators. CSV/JSON export and skill-based distribution are real advantages, though privacy and site clutter are not ideal.

Pricing: Free web tool with site account/trial navigation elsewhere. No account is required for the team-generator workflow.

Pros

  • Textarea input
  • Team-count and team-size modes
  • Custom team names
  • Random, snake, and skill-based distribution modes
  • Optional team statistics
  • Copy, CSV, and JSON output
  • No account required

Cons

  • Ads and consent/session cookies
  • No hard keep-apart or keep-together constraints
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No saved scenario workspace
  • Site chrome is heavier than the tool needs

Summary

A strong lightweight random team generator. Captain mode, skill ratings, and local saved groups give it more practical depth than most simple tools.

Pros

  • Paste or individual-add input
  • Team-count and team-size modes
  • Captain mode
  • Skill ratings
  • Copy Teams button
  • Browser crypto randomness
  • Local saved groups
  • No account required

Cons

  • Analytics privacy caveats
  • No CSV/spreadsheet export
  • No hard keep-apart or keep-together constraints
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning

#30 3.5★

GroupMixes↗ opens tool website

Teachers who want quick mixed-ability classroom groups

Free with ads/upsellsconstraintsstrong-exports
Hard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialAttribute balance: YesCustom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: YesSaved participant/team lists: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: YesGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.4★
Better than plain random splitters because it can balance skill/gender labels and respect keep-together/keep-apart rules with warnings. Best-effort single-session grouping, not a real solver for hard constraints or repeated sessions.
Features3.5★
Good feature depth for a simple group generator: arbitrary group count up to 12, students per group up to 10, skill levels, gender labels, keep-together and keep-apart rules, best-effort warnings, drag-and-drop edits, local autosave, copy, print, and CSV export. Missing custom per-group capacities, multi-session repeat minimization, pair matrix, and advanced constraint weighting.
Exports4★
Strong for this tier: copy, print, and CSV export with group/student/skill/gender columns. No Zoom-specific export, share link, PDF download, or saved result history beyond local browser state.
Privacy2.6★
Mixed. The app autosaves rosters in browser local storage and does not require an account, which is good. The page loads Google AdSense, Google Tag Manager/Analytics, Ahrefs analytics, and ad scripts, so it is not a clean privacy environment for student names.
Ease4.2★
The workflow is approachable: paste names, choose number of groups or students per group, optionally set skill/gender and rules, generate, then drag students between groups. More setup than a basic splitter, but teacher-friendly.
Design4★
Modern, readable, and clearly built around classroom grouping. The step-based layout and group cards are much better than most generic randomizer sites.
Account friction5★
No account or sign-up required for the generator. This is frictionless compared with spreadsheet/account-based workflows.
Review notes

Summary

One of the better simple classroom group generators: real grouping controls and exports, but no multi-session/repeat optimization and privacy is weakened by ads/analytics.

Pricing: Free, ad-supported web tool. No sign-up required for the group generator.

Pros

  • Skill and gender balancing
  • Keep-together and keep-apart rules
  • Drag-and-drop result editing
  • Copy, print, and CSV export
  • No account required
  • Local autosave in browser

Cons

  • AdSense/analytics/ad scripts on the page
  • No multi-session repeat optimization
  • No custom per-group capacities
  • Rules are best-effort
  • No Zoom export or shareable result link
#31 3.5★

Playpass Golf Schedule Maker↗ opens tool website

Golf rotating-partner and round-robin schedules

Free, no accountmulti-roundconstraintsstrong-exports
Multi-round: YesRepeat encounter limits: PartialUnique-contact: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: PartialPair meeting count targets: PartialResult comments: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: YesNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: PartialPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: PartialResult comments: PartialResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: PartialCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: PartialVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.4★
For its narrow rotating-partner/round-robin use case, the schedule is coherent and complete. It does not provide solver diagnostics or broad fairness controls.
Features3.5★
Round-robin and rotating-partner schedule modes, dates, windows, game length, groups, courses, standings, score editing, sharing, and publishing are useful for sports rotations. It lacks arbitrary constraints and participant attributes.
Exports4★
The generated schedule page, share/publish controls, standings, and game list are much better than display-only tools, though CSV/PDF export was not the primary visible flow in the tested draft.
Privacy2.7★
Participant labels and schedules are hosted by Playpass; the draft can exist without an account, but this is not a local/private tool.
Ease3.8★
The setup wizard is understandable and the public form produced a schedule without login, though it is more league-scheduler than paste-names simple.
Design3.7★
Clean sports-SaaS interface with readable setup steps, schedule pages, and standings. It is busier than a purpose-built group generator.
Account friction4.6★
The tested generator created a usable draft without signup. Broader management and publishing workflows push users toward the platform.
Review notes

Summary

A real, self-serve golf rotation scheduler with stronger output than most simple randomizers, but narrow to sport/league schedules rather than general-purpose grouping.

Pricing: Free public schedule generator with no signup required for the tested draft schedule. Playpass also sells broader sports-management features for registration, payments, memberships, bookings, waivers, websites, and publishing workflows.

Pros

  • Public generator created a schedule without signup
  • Rotating-partner and round-robin sports modes
  • Generated standings and individual game rows
  • Share/publish workflow
  • Useful for golf leagues and recurring sports play

Cons

  • Narrow sports-league model rather than a general group solver
  • No paste-name roster workflow in the tested path
  • No arbitrary keep-apart/keep-together constraints
  • No participant attribute balancing
  • Hosted platform privacy tradeoffs

Quality3★
More practical than plain random because attendance, rules/preferences, and manual adjustment are available. No scoring report or repeat-matrix diagnostics.
Features3.6★
Multiple classes, attendance toggles, default group size, drag/drop, add group, projector mode, copy list, import/export, and apparent per-student rule controls are useful for teachers.
Exports2.9★
Copy list and data export/import are present. No CSV/PDF/image/Zoom export.

Design2.8★

Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A surprisingly practical teacher tool with local storage, attendance, drag/drop, and import/export, despite rough UI.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • No third-party hosts observed
  • Multiple classes
  • Attendance checklist
  • Default group size
  • Drag-and-drop adjustment
  • Projector mode
  • Copy list
  • JSON import/export

Cons

  • Rough standalone UI
  • No CSV/PDF/export files
  • Rule controls are dense
  • No diagnostics or pair matrix
  • No polished documentation

Quality4★
High for multi-round golf pairings. It directly optimizes repeated encounters and time slots, though within rigid preset inputs.
Features3.5★
Strong for the narrow use case: 2- to 8-somes, preset golfer counts up to 100, 2-52 events, pairing optimization, and time-slot optimization. Weak on custom constraints and data entry.
Exports2.8★
Event tables and optimization tabs are useful. No obvious copy, CSV, PDF, names, share link, or saved project workflow.
Privacy4.6★
Excellent compared with most free tools: no cookies observed and only first-party/jQuery/Kendo scripts on the calculator page.
Ease3★
Only three dropdowns and Build Schedule, but the output is numeric and golf-specific rather than friendly.
Design1.8★
Very dated. Functional, but it looks and feels like an old calculator widget.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

Dated and inflexible, but algorithmically much more serious than basic random splitters. Strong for preset golf pairing schedules.

Pricing: Free web calculator. No account required.

Pros

  • Free
  • No account required
  • Multi-event schedule generation
  • 2- to 8-some options
  • Golfer counts up to 100
  • Up to 52 events
  • Pairing optimization diagnostics
  • Time-slot optimization diagnostics
  • Very light privacy footprint

Cons

  • Dated interface
  • Numeric golfer labels only
  • Preset counts only
  • No partial attendance
  • No custom constraints
  • No modern export/share workflow
  • Golf-specific and inflexible

A genuinely good lightweight team picker. Gender balancing and representative selection make it more useful than most basic splitters.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

  • No account required
  • Bulk import
  • Gender distribution strategies
  • Auto-selected representatives
  • Custom team names
  • Polished UI
  • Local-data privacy claims

Cons

  • No multi-round repeat optimization
  • No hard keep-apart/keep-together constraints
  • No visible CSV/PDF export
  • Animated result flow can be slow
  • Analytics/ad script present

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.4★
Balanced random teams only, without constraints or repeat minimization.
Features3★
By team count/max size, bench leftovers, dedupe, and generated names are useful simple-tool features.
Exports2.8★
Copy result is available; no CSV/PDF/export was verified.
Privacy4.2★
The page says generation is local, and only the site plus Google fonts were observed.
Ease4★
Paste names, choose split mode, and generate.
Design3.6★
Readable modern cards with themed team names.
Account friction4.8★
No account is needed for the generator, although account navigation exists.
Review notes

Summary

A polished simple splitter with useful bench/dedupe options, despite a console error.

Pricing: Free. No account is required; the broader Spacebar Count site has account/pro navigation.

Pros

  • No account required
  • By team count or max team size
  • Bench leftover option
  • Remove duplicates option
  • Generated team names
  • Copy result

Cons

  • No constraints
  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No CSV/export verified
  • Console error observed
#37 3.4★

TeamGenerator.net Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Sports and game teams balanced by skill ratings and roles

Free with ads/upsellsconstraints
Attribute balance: YesCustom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesResult quality diagnostics: PartialAttribute total/sum: YesCopy results to clipboard: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: YesCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: PartialSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.6★
Better than random-only splitters for sports and games because it balances player counts, skill averages, and roles. It is not a broad constraint solver or multi-round optimizer.
Features3.6★
Above-average for sports/game teams: skill ratings, roles/positions, by-team-count and by-players-per-team modes, local saved lists, import/export, Copy All, per-team copy, and regenerate. Missing bulk paste, arbitrary attributes, hard constraints, repeat planning, saved result history, and deeper diagnostics.
Exports3.2★
Copy All and per-team copy are useful, and saved-list import/export helps with rosters. There is no CSV/spreadsheet result export, TXT/JSON download, image/PDF, Zoom export, share link, or report format.
Privacy1.4★
Poor. The tool says data is saved only in the browser and no generation API upload was observed, but the page uses Google Analytics, Google AdSense/DoubleClick, Funding Choices, and a Google-managed consent dialog saying data may be shared with 210 partners and precise geolocation may be processed.
Ease3★
The UI is clear, but adding every participant one at a time with stars and optional role is slower than paste-based rosters. Saved-list import/export helps repeat use.
Design3.8★
Modern, readable, and focused, with good visual team cards and star/role display. Ads and consent UI add page noise.
Account friction5★
No signup, login, or payment is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A genuinely useful skill/role balancer for sports and games, but slower to load rosters than paste-based tools and not privacy-friendly.

Pricing: Free ad-supported web tool. No account is required.

Pros

  • Balances by 1–5 star skill ratings
  • Distributes roles/positions across teams
  • Number-of-teams and players-per-team modes
  • Local saved lists with import/export
  • Copy All and per-team copy
  • No account required

Cons

  • No bulk paste roster workflow
  • No CSV/spreadsheet result export
  • No hard constraints or repeat avoidance
  • No saved result history
  • Ad/analytics consent stack with 210-partner language
  • Sports/player model is narrower than general group planning
#38 3.4★

Toolora Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Private browser-side random teams with copy and settings share

Free with ads/upsellsprivacy-friendly
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCopy results to clipboard: YesPaste names: YesShareable result links: PartialStorage management/cleanup: Partial
Show all features

Quality2.8★
Fair random teams with even sizes, but no optimization beyond that.
Features2.5★
Good basic cleanup and split-mode options, but no real planning rules.
Exports2.3★
Copy is useful; share restores settings/input rather than being a robust result export.
Privacy4.4★
Strong browser-side posture with no observed third-party scripts, though share URLs can encode names.
Ease4.3★
Clear paste-and-generate workflow with both team-count and team-size modes.
Design3.8★
Clean, modern, and not buried under ads.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

One of the better simple random splitters: clean, local, and practical. It still lacks constraints, imports/exports, repeat planning, and diagnostics.

Pricing: Free browser tool. No account or paid tier is needed for the team generator.

Pros

  • By team count or people per team
  • Trim, skip-blank, and dedupe toggles
  • Cryptographic Fisher-Yates shuffle
  • Copy result button
  • Browser-only privacy positioning
  • No account required

Cons

  • No CSV/spreadsheet export
  • Share URL can expose roster names
  • No custom team names
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No diagnostics

Exports3.4★

Summary

A strong simple splitter with useful workflow controls, held back by tracking scripts and no advanced grouping logic.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Paste/import workflow
  • By count or by size
  • Quick exclude controls
  • History
  • Copy and export controls
  • Local calculation claim

Cons

  • No constraints
  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No diagnostics
  • Tracking scripts present

UK-style form/class allocation using friends and must-avoid relationships

Free, no accountconstraintsstrong-exports
Hard keep-apart: YesCustom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: YesResult quality diagnostics: PartialExcel export: PartialGender/label balancing: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: PartialFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: PartialGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: PartialSoft prefer-together: YesSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: YesStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.6★
Potentially strong for its narrow school-form allocation problem because it accounts for friendships/avoid rules and manual swaps.
Features3.7★
Specialized strengths: CSV/template workflow, close friends, wider friends, cannot-be-with rules, workload/gender-style columns, manual swap checks, forms/classes modes, and result download.
Exports3.2★
Download Results CSV is available after processing. No PDF/image/share link or rich report.
Privacy3.8★
No account and very few third-party requests beyond jsDelivr in testing. Privacy documentation is minimal.
Ease2★
The workflow is not beginner-friendly: users need the template and correct relationship columns, then wait for processing.
Design1.8★
Very plain Bootstrap-style interface. Functional, not polished.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

Niche but genuinely useful for form allocation; too brittle and specialized for general group generation.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • CSV/template import
  • Forms and classes modes
  • Close/wider friend relationship handling
  • Cannot-be-with rules
  • Manual swap workflow
  • Download results CSV

Cons

  • Sparse UI
  • Brittle input format
  • No general-purpose pasted-name quick mode
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No modern export/report options
  • Narrow school-form use case

Quality2.5★
Balanced random groups with reproducibility and reroll controls. No optimization beyond random splitting.
Features3.4★
Good randomizer features: two split modes, organizer/representative selection, seed, saved lists, recent runs, share link, group lock/reroll, and sample datasets. No real constraint or attribute model.
Exports4★
Copy groups, TXT export, CSV export, share link, per-group copy, and run-history copy are all useful.
Privacy1.1★
Poor for rosters: Google ads, Tag Manager/Analytics, Microsoft Clarity, Cloudflare/CDN scripts, and ad-quality domains loaded.
Ease3.8★
The main flow is understandable, though the page has many controls and ads around the workbench.
Design3.6★
The workbench is more polished than most SEO-tool randomizers, with useful counters, preview text, run history, and per-group actions.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

One of the better plain-random tools because of seed, history, saved lists, locks, share, and TXT/CSV outputs.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • By team count or members per team
  • Organizer/representative selection
  • Deterministic seed
  • Saved lists and recent runs
  • Share link
  • Group lock/reroll controls
  • Copy, TXT, and CSV output

Cons

  • No attribute balancing
  • No keep-apart/keep-together rule model verified
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No pair matrix or diagnostics
  • Heavy ad/analytics footprint

Small free speed-dating or networking rotation events

Multi-round: YesRepeat encounter limits: YesUnique-contact: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesSelf-join participant link: YesGender/label balancing: YesPair meeting count targets: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: YesGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: YesNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: PartialPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: YesResult comments: PartialResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: YesShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: PartialSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: PartialVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: PartialZoom CSV: No
Quality3.5★
For small speed-dating/networking events, the matching controls are meaningful. It does not provide broad optimization diagnostics.
Features3.7★
Opposite-gender, same-gender, any-gender networking, age preferences, strict age matching, mutual-match messaging, timing, round counts, and start modes are substantial for this niche.
Exports2.2★
Like FastRounds, it is built for live running rather than exporting a reusable static schedule.
Privacy2.5★
Hosted attendee/event flow with matching and optional messaging; not local/private.
Ease3.3★
The free host flow works, but the configuration screen has many dating-specific options that can overwhelm basic networking use cases.
Design3.2★
Plain but practical host dashboard. It feels operational rather than polished.
Account friction4.7★
The tested free event required no signup or credit card.
Review notes

Summary

A useful small-event speed-dating/networking rotation host, with better matching controls than FastRounds but the same strict free cap.

Pricing: Free host event with no signup or credit card for up to six attendees. Larger events require paid event packs or subscriptions.

Pros

  • No signup or credit card for a capped free event
  • Dating and networking matching modes
  • Age preference controls
  • Mutual-match messaging option
  • Self-join attendee code

Cons

  • Free version capped at six attendees
  • Dating-specific assumptions can be irrelevant
  • No static export workflow observed
  • No roster spreadsheet import
  • Hosted matching privacy tradeoffs

Quality2.7★

Features2.8★
Number-of-teams and players-per-team modes, label toggle, shuffle, reset, and shareable links. No constraints or repeat planning.
Exports2.5★
Copy Shareable Link is a useful output path; no file exports were observed.
Privacy4.2★
No ads or analytics detected in fetched HTML, and GitHub source is linked. Share links may encode roster/result data in URLs.

Clear actions and settings; live test generated teams successfully.

Account friction5★

Review notes

Summary

A clean, privacy-friendly basic splitter with shareable links and good settings, but no solver-grade constraints.

Pricing: Free web tool; source linked on GitHub.

Pros

  • Generated tested roster successfully
  • Number-of-teams and players-per-team settings
  • Shuffle and reset controls
  • Copy shareable link
  • No account required
  • GitHub source linked
  • No ads/analytics detected in HTML

Cons

  • No CSV/Excel/PDF export
  • No constraints or attributes
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No quality diagnostics
  • Share links can expose encoded roster/result data

Single-session browser grouping with copy/export/share controls and lightweight balance modes

Free with ads/upsellsconstraintsstrong-exports
Attribute balance: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: YesZoom CSV: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialResult quality diagnostics: YesCopy results to clipboard: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: PartialAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: PartialFixed person placement: PartialGender/label balancing: YesGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: PartialPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: YesBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: PartialSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: YesSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: YesCSV import: YesStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: Partial
Quality3★
Better than plain random splitting because balance modes, auto-balance, drag-and-drop refinement, and analysis exist. Not comparable to a repeat-optimization or hard-constraint solver.
Features3.3★
Good for one session: file upload, normal/level/gender modes, regenerate, auto-balance, drag-and-drop refinement, analysis score, export, share, and login-gated history. No verified multi-round repeat optimization or serious constraint solver.
Exports4★
Strong output surface: copy result, export, share, and login-gated saving/history are available from the result panel. Not a verified Zoom-specific CSV workflow despite the marketing copy.
Privacy1.7★
Mixed to bad. The tool says names are processed locally and the privacy policy says saved/account data is handled for sync, but the page also loads Google Ads, Funding Choices, Google Tag Manager, and Microsoft Clarity.
Ease4.1★
The basic flow is straightforward: paste names, set group count, generate. Extra mode/export/save controls are visible without making the quick path too hard.
Design4★
Modern, readable, and more product-like than most randomizer pages. The long marketing page and ad/consent layer make it feel less clean.
Account friction4.2★
Basic generation works without login. Saving/history and some reuse workflows are account-gated.
Review notes

Summary

A genuinely useful one-session group generator with good outputs and some balancing controls, held back by ad-tech/privacy issues and marketing claims that go beyond the visible product.

Pricing: Free for basic generation. Saving/history uses login. The page advertises optional sync/account features rather than a clearly priced paid plan.

Pros

  • Paste names or upload file
  • Normal, level, and gender modes
  • Regenerate and auto-balance controls
  • Copy result and export buttons
  • Share and login-gated save/history
  • Drag-and-drop refinement
  • Group analysis score

Cons

  • No verified multi-session repeat optimization
  • No hard constraint model
  • Some marketing claims exceed the visible tool
  • Saving requires login
  • Google Ads, Funding Choices, GTM, and Microsoft Clarity
  • No clearly documented pricing for account features
#45 3.3★

Free round-robin team schedules with links and exports

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: YesMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: YesNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: PartialPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: PartialResult comments: PartialResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.1★
Round-robin output was coherent for teams. It does not optimize arbitrary participant attributes, repeat goals beyond the schedule format, or constraints.
Features3.4★
Team count, custom names, weeks, dates/times, public/admin links, rosters, scores, standings, venues, and schedule tabs are useful. It lacks general grouping constraints.
Exports4.3★
CSV, PDF, copyable admin/public links, public schedule pages, and week-by-week schedule views are strong for a free generator.
Privacy2.6★
Schedules and team names are hosted. That is normal for league sharing but weaker than local/browser-only generators.
Ease3.9★
Entering teams and generating a schedule was straightforward. The post-generation admin surface is large because it is a league product.
Design3.6★
Readable modern interface with clear steps and generated weeks, though there are many Pro prompts and management tabs.
Account friction4.7★
The tested schedule was created without signup. Some editing/management features are locked behind Pro.
Review notes

Summary

Strong for free league round-robin schedule generation; only moderately relevant for people who need arbitrary workshop/classroom grouping.

Pricing: Free schedule generator with no signup required for the tested workflow. Pro features are advertised for deeper league management, extra edits, and larger/managed workflows.

Pros

  • No signup required for tested schedule creation
  • Custom team names
  • Round-robin week-by-week schedule output
  • CSV and PDF controls
  • Admin and public links

Cons

  • League schedules only, not general participant grouping
  • No roster spreadsheet import in the tested flow
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • Many management controls are Pro-oriented
  • Hosted schedule privacy tradeoff

Browser: YesNo account: YesCopy results to clipboard: YesEditable/random team names: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialPaste names: YesShareable result links: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.2★
Balanced random teams only.
Features2.8★
Two split modes, custom team names, reshuffle, and share are useful for a basic splitter.
Exports3.8★
Copy all, per-team copy, text download, and share link were visible after generation.
Privacy4★
Only WebRandom and Google Fonts hosts were observed; the page claims names are not stored.
Ease4★
Paste, choose split mode/count, and generate. The workflow is direct.
Design3.4★
Clean, focused tool with readable result cards.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

One of the better simple random splitters thanks to copy/download/share controls and a light footprint.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • By team count or members per team
  • Optional custom team names
  • Copy all and per-team copy
  • Download as text
  • Share link
  • Low observed third-party footprint

Cons

  • No constraints
  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No saved roster workflow
  • No diagnostics
#47 3.3★

Wooclap Team Picker↗ opens tool website

Polished simple random teams with strong export options

Free with ads/upsellsprivacy-friendlystrong-exports
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialCopy results to clipboard: YesEditable/random team names: YesImage/PDF export: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: YesUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.1★
Random balanced groups only, with a small practical lift from leader and team-name handling. Fine for one-off splits, but no constraints, repeat reduction, or quality diagnostics.
Features2.4★
Better than a bare random splitter: team leaders, random/editable team names, two split modes, shuffle/regenerate, and local session restore. No constraints, repeat avoidance, balancing rules, or multi-round planning.
Exports4.8★
Excellent output options for this category: CSV, JPG, PNG, PDF, and copy to clipboard. Not full marks because it is a simple export menu, not integration-grade output.
Privacy4.2★
Much better than the ad-heavy tools. In testing the free group-generator page was light, and Wooclap has a serious GDPR/ISO 27001 privacy posture. Not 5/5 because participant names are stored in browser localStorage and Wooclap is a hosted product with broader analytics, marketing, and cookie policies.
Ease4.8★
Very easy to use: paste names, choose number of teams or participants per team, and generate. Visible login/signup/product-marketing buttons keep it just shy of the cleanest tiny utilities.
Design4.4★
One of the best-designed simple tools in this list. Clean, modern, readable, and much nicer than the older utility pages, though some Wooclap marketing remains.
Account friction5★
No account required. Login and signup are visible, but the group maker works without them.
Review notes

Summary

One of the best simple random group makers: polished, no-account, and excellent exports. The assignment logic uses basic random grouping, not real group optimization.

Pricing: Free standalone group-generator page. No account is required for this specific free team picker; do not confuse it with Wooclap’s broader paid audience-engagement product.

Pros

  • Clean modern design
  • Excellent export options
  • Team leaders and editable/random team names
  • No account required

Cons

  • Random only
  • No advanced rules or repeat reduction
  • Surrounded by Wooclap product marketing
  • Names are kept in browser localStorage
#48 3.2★

FastRounds↗ opens tool website

Small free speed-networking round rotation events

Free with ads/upsellsmulti-roundconstraintsself-join
Multi-round: YesRepeat encounter limits: YesUnique-contact: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesSelf-join participant link: YesPair meeting count targets: PartialResult comments: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: YesNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: PartialPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: YesResult comments: PartialResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: YesShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: PartialVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.4★
Round-robin matching is a legitimate quality feature for live speed networking, though the free cap is tiny and diagnostics are minimal.
Features3.4★
True round-robin, half-room-fixed mode, round count, timing, manual/auto starts, transition timing, sounds, messaging, and attendee join codes are meaningful live-event controls.
Exports2.3★
The product is designed for live operation rather than exporting a static schedule. No strong CSV/PDF-style output was observed in the tested setup.
Privacy2.6★
Attendees join a hosted event and may use messaging. Better than ad-heavy randomizers, but not local/private.
Ease3.4★
Creating the host event was quick once the anti-spam question was answered. Running a live event requires attendees to join by code.
Design3.2★
Functional and readable, but plain. The host dashboard prioritizes operations over polished presentation.
Account friction4.7★
The free capped event required no signup or credit card in testing.
Review notes

Summary

A real self-serve live rotation tool with a strict free cap and limited static export/reporting value.

Pricing: Free host event with no signup or credit card for up to six attendees. Larger events require paid event packs or subscriptions.

Pros

  • No signup or credit card for a capped free event
  • Self-join attendee code
  • True round-robin mode
  • Manual or automatic round starts
  • Live-event timing controls

Cons

  • Free version capped at six attendees
  • No static roster paste/import workflow
  • Weak export story for offline planning
  • Hosted event privacy tradeoffs
  • Narrow speed-networking use case
#49 3.2★

Flipiffy Random Group Generator↗ opens tool website

Basic random groups with leaders, exclusions, saved list, CSV, and copy

Free, no accountconstraintsstrong-exports
Hard keep-apart: YesBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialCopy results to clipboard: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialPaste names: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: YesUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.7★
Better than plain random only because leaders and keep-apart rules can shape groups, but there is no optimization or scoring.
Features3.3★
Good for a free randomizer: upload, by-count/by-size modes, leader spreading, keep-apart exclusions, local save, CSV, and copy-all. No attributes, multi-round repeat optimization, diagnostics, or manual editor.
Exports3.3★
CSV download and copy-all are visible after generation. No PDF/image, share link, or Zoom-specific export.
Privacy0.9★
Very weak for rosters: Google ads/consent, Microsoft Clarity, AddToAny, Google Tag Manager/Analytics, and related third-party scripts loaded.
Ease3.3★
The main controls are understandable, though the page and consent layer make interaction less clean than it should be.
Design2.9★
Functional but generic. The form is usable, surrounded by SEO content and site chrome.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A surprisingly featureful random-group tool, held back by rough execution and poor tracking/privacy.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • TXT/CSV upload
  • By group count or people per group
  • Leader spreading via * prefix
  • Keep-apart exclusion rules
  • CSV export and copy-all
  • Local saved list

Cons

  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No quality diagnostics
  • Rough implementation/interaction
  • Heavy ad/analytics/sharing scripts
#50 3.2★

Grouperapp.net↗ opens tool website

Google Classroom teachers who want paid group history and preferences

Free, no accountconstraintsstrong-exports
Multi-round: PartialRepeat encounter limits: PartialHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialBrowser: YesCSV export: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesResult quality diagnostics: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: PartialHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: PartialNo account: NoPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: PartialResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: YesSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: YesSoft prefer-together: YesSolver telemetry/result metadata: PartialSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.2★
Likely better than random if blocked/preferred pairs and group history work as advertised, but the algorithm was not independently tested.
Features3.6★
Advertised features are relevant: smart formation, preferred/blocked pairs, group history, CSV export, analytics, and Google Classroom focus. Public page does not expose the actual controls.
Exports3★
CSV export is advertised. No hands-on export test was possible without signing in.
Privacy2.4★
The site claims encryption plus FERPA/COPPA compliance. It also loads Google/Atlassian/Loom/Sentry-related third-party services, and classroom data requires an account connection.
Ease2.8★
The landing page is clear, but use requires Google sign-in and a trial before the real workflow can be tested.
Design3.7★
Modern teacher-SaaS presentation with clear feature/pricing sections.
Account friction1.8★
High: Google sign-in and paid-trial workflow are required.
Review notes

Summary

Promising for Google Classroom group management, but account/paywall friction and limited public verification hold it back.

Pricing: 14-day free trial, then advertised at $6.99/month on the Professional plan.

Pros

  • Built for Google Classroom teachers
  • Preferred and blocked pair claims
  • Group history
  • CSV export claim
  • Data analytics claim
  • FERPA/COPPA compliance claim

Cons

  • Requires Google sign-in
  • Paid after trial
  • Actual grouping workflow not publicly testable
  • No no-account one-off generator
  • Third-party services loaded
#51 3.2★

Keamk↗ opens tool website

Random teams with basic gender or skill balancing

Free, no accountconstraintsstrong-exports
Attribute balance: YesNo account: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesCopy results to clipboard: PartialEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: YesExcel export: YesGender/label balancing: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: PartialCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: YesExcel export: YesFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: YesGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: YesResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: NoSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: YesSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.1★
Better than pure random because it can balance by gender or skill level. No advanced constraints, repeat reduction, capacity modeling, or diagnostics.
Features3.4★
Real features compared with most randomizers: normal random teams, gender balancing, skill-level balancing, participant/team imports, saved lists, editable team names, draw management, comments, embed, and history/account workflows.
Exports4.3★
Strong output options for this category: public share link, private admin link, Excel export, embed code, social sharing, and comments. Not Zoom-friendly and not as polished as Wooclap.
Privacy1★
Bad. The privacy policy says IP addresses are stored when you make a draw or post a comment, Google Analytics is used, and data is hosted in Canada. The page also uses a heavy Google ad-consent setup with many vendor/device-storage options.
Ease2.9★
Usable, but not effortless. The form is dense, participants are entered into rows unless you use the import control, and gender/skill/team controls make it feel heavier than it needs to be.
Design2★
Dated and clunky. It looks like an old web form, and the results page is functional but messy.
Account friction4★
No account is required for a basic draw, but several useful persistence and management features push account or email workflows, including saved lists, draw history, and administration links.
Review notes

Summary

One of the more capable random-team tools, with real balancing and sharing/export features, but the clunky UI and terrible ad-consent/privacy setup hold it back hard.

Pricing: Free basic team generator with optional account/admin features for saving and managing groups. The free workflow is usable, but persistence and classroom-management convenience depend more on the account side.

Pros

  • Gender and skill balancing
  • Share link, admin link, embed, and Excel export
  • Saved lists and draw management
  • Participant and team import controls

Cons

  • Clunky old form UI
  • Bad privacy/ad-consent setup
  • Many management features push account/email workflows
  • No repeat reduction or advanced constraints
#52 3.2★

PrintYourBrackets Round Robin Generator↗ opens tool website

Tournament organizers who need printable round-robin schedules, pools, Excel export, and share links

Free with ads/upsellsmulti-roundconstraintsstrong-exports
Multi-round: YesRepeat encounter limits: YesUnique-contact: YesCustom group capacities: PartialBrowser: PartialNo account: YesCSV export: PartialResult quality diagnostics: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: PartialEmbeddable results: PartialExcel export: YesFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: YesNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: YesPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: PartialPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: PartialPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: YesResult comments: PartialResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: PartialSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: PartialSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: PartialCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: YesVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.6★
Good for the narrow round-robin tournament problem because it produces repeated rounds where teams meet. It is not a solver for balanced social groups, repeated workshop groups, or attribute constraints.
Features3.3★
Good for tournament scheduling: pools, team counts, bulk team names, optional locations, random/manual pool assignment, logo/rules, printable schedules, Excel export, and share links. Weak for actual people-to-groups constraints or classroom balancing.
Exports4★
One of its strongest areas: preview schedule, printable page, Excel export, save/share link, and optional online management workflow.
Privacy1.2★
Poor. The page shows a consent banner and disclosed processing by 210 partners with precise geolocation language. The privacy policy says Google ad cookies are used and Google automatically receives IP addresses. Not a good fit for sensitive roster data.
Ease3.3★
The basic form is understandable, with bulk team entry and dropdowns. The page is old-fashioned and a bit cluttered, and some advanced options are easier to miss than they should be.
Design2★
Dated tournament-site layout with lots of navigation, ads, and promotional blocks around the generator.
Account friction5★
No account required for the generator itself; the online manager is optional.
Review notes

Summary

Strong enough for tournament schedules, but it is a niche round-robin bracket tool rather than a general group generator. Output options are good; privacy and UI are weak.

Pricing: Free web generator. No account required for the printable schedule flow. The site promotes a free online tournament manager as a separate upgrade path/workflow.

Pros

  • Free
  • No account required
  • Bulk team entry
  • Supports 3–64 teams
  • Supports 1–32 pools
  • Random or manual pool assignment
  • Optional locations
  • Printable schedule
  • Excel export
  • Save/share link
  • Optional online tournament manager path

Cons

  • Not a general people-to-groups generator
  • No participant attribute balancing
  • No keep-together/keep-apart constraints
  • Dated, ad-heavy UI
  • Poor privacy/ad-tech footprint
  • Some options are tournament-specific and clunky for non-sports use
#53 3.2★

RandomTeamBuilder.com↗ opens tool website

Visual random teams with drag-and-drop adjustment and exclusion-rule claims

Free with ads/upsellsconstraints
Hard keep-apart: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialEditable/random team names: YesGoogle Sheets import: PartialMinimum group sizes: PartialPaste names: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: PartialHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: PartialPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: YesWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.7★
Better than plain random if exclusion rules are used, but no scoring or optimization transparency was found.
Features3.4★
Notable features include exclusion-rule claims, drag-and-drop adjustments, custom team names, animations, saved lists, and Google Classroom import. No attribute balancing, multi-round repeat optimization, or diagnostics.
Exports1.8★
No strong export workflow was verified. The tool emphasizes display, drag/drop, saving lists, and sharing the site more than CSV/PDF output.
Privacy1.5★
Loads Google sign-in/classroom APIs, Google Tag Manager/Analytics, ipapi, many CDNs, and other third-party assets. Not ideal for student rosters.
Ease3.5★
Pasting names and randomizing is easy. Advanced features such as exclusions and saved lists are less obvious.
Design3.1★
Interactive and projector-friendly, with animations/confetti, but the page text output and controls are somewhat rough.
Account friction3.8★
Basic randomization works without signup, but list/account features and Google Classroom integration introduce account friction.
Review notes

Summary

Featureful and interactive for classrooms, but rough technically and surrounded by account/Google integrations.

Pricing: Free for the visible randomizer. Account/login surfaces exist for list/account features.

Pros

  • Visible no-account team randomizing
  • Number-of-teams and names-per-team controls
  • Exclusion-rule claims
  • Drag-and-drop adjustment claims
  • Custom team names
  • Animations and confetti
  • Saved-list and Google Classroom surfaces

Cons

  • Repeated JavaScript errors in live testing
  • No verified CSV/PDF export
  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • Heavy Google/API/tracking surface
  • Advanced features are not especially transparent
#54 3.2★

Raugen Random Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Quick random teams with custom team names, copy-all output, basic stats, and local generation history

Free with ads/upsellsai-assisted
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesResult quality diagnostics: PartialCopy results to clipboard: YesEditable/random team names: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialPaste names: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: YesSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.3★
Slightly above bare random because balanced distribution and team counts are explicit, but assignments are random-only.
Features2.7★
Good for a simple splitter: add/remove players, 2–12 teams, balanced/random distribution, editable team names, history, and stats. No constraints, attributes, pair tracking, or repeat avoidance.
Exports3.2★
Copy All Teams, visible team cards, statistics, and local history are better than most simple tools. No CSV, PDF, image, or shareable result link.
Privacy2.5★
Mixed. No account is needed and it appears browser-based, but the page loads GTM/Google scripts, Cloudflare analytics, and Google sign-in state cookies.
Ease4★
The workflow is clear, though the add-players step and broader AI-tool navigation add some noise.
Design3.7★
Modern and readable. The broader RauGen navigation is busy, but the generator itself is well laid out.
Account friction4.8★
Generation works without signing in. Sign-in is only for the wider platform.
Review notes

Summary

One of the better basic random splitters: useful controls and output, but random-only and attached to a broader tracking/sign-in site.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required for team generation; sign-in exists for the broader RauGen site.

Pros

  • Free
  • No account required
  • Paste names or comma-separated players
  • Balanced/random distribution toggle
  • Editable team names
  • Copy all teams
  • Generation history and statistics

Cons

  • No real constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat optimization
  • No CSV/PDF/export bundle
  • Busy parent AI-tool site
  • Google/GTM/Cloudflare tracking surface
  • Sign-in chrome is visible
#55 3.2★

TextGround Random Group Generator↗ opens tool website

Simple group splitting with bulk import, reshuffle, copy, and download

Free, no account

Quality2.2★
Balanced random groups only.
Features2.8★
Useful basics: bulk import, two split modes, prefix, reshuffle, copy, and download. No advanced grouping logic.
Exports3.4★
Copy and download controls are available after generation.
Privacy2★
No account is needed, but Ahrefs, Google Analytics/Tag Manager, and Google scripts loaded.
Ease3.8★
Bulk paste plus import made the tested workflow straightforward.
Design3.3★
Clean enough tool layout with entry list, settings, and result cards.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A solid basic tool with better import/output controls than most simple randomizers.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • Bulk paste
  • TXT/CSV upload path
  • By group count or members per group
  • Custom group prefix
  • Reshuffle
  • Copy and download controls

Cons

  • No constraints
  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No diagnostics
  • Analytics/ad-related scripts present

afreetools Smart Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Small smart-team experiments with skill, role, avoid-pair, and share-link controls

Quality3★
Skill totals and role labels make the results more useful than plain random groups, but there is no diagnostic report or solver transparency.
Features3.3★
Random, balanced, role-based, draft mode, avoid-pair, multi-round, skills, roles, and share link are relevant grouping features.
Exports1.8★
Generated teams display on the page and a share-link button exists. No CSV/TXT/PDF/image export was verified.
Privacy0.8★
Very weak for participant lists: Google ads/consent, Ahrefs, Microsoft Clarity, Tag Manager/Analytics, FontAwesome, and other third-party services loaded.
Ease3.2★
The input format is terse but learnable: one row per person with optional skill and role values.
Design2.7★
Functional WordPress-style page. Generated teams are readable, but the surrounding page is cluttered.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A useful lightweight smart generator, held back by rough UI, weak exports, and ad/consent bloat.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Skill values
  • Role labels
  • Random/balanced/role-based/draft modes
  • Avoid-pair field
  • Multi-round option
  • Share-link button

Cons

  • No verified CSV/PDF export
  • No repeat matrix or diagnostics
  • Terse input format
  • Basic implementation
  • Heavy ad/analytics consent footprint
#57 3.1★

AppSorteos Random Teams Generator↗ opens tool website

Polished free random teams with leaders and share/export buttons

Free with ads/upsellsstrong-exports
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: PartialCSV export: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialCopy results to clipboard: YesExcel export: PartialImage/PDF export: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: PartialFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: PartialPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: PartialCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: YesUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.1★
Slightly above plain random splitting because leaders can be placed first in each team. Otherwise it is standard random equal teams with no quality optimization.
Features2.4★
Good for a basic splitter: number-of-teams mode, participants-per-team mode, automatic equal distribution, optional group leaders, title editing, and regenerate. No constraints, balancing, repeats, attributes, or diagnostics.
Exports4★
Strong for a simple tool. Generated results have download, print, copy, copy-for-Excel, save, and share actions. Save/share require account creation, and there is no Zoom-specific export or true spreadsheet import.
Privacy2★
Weak for classroom/personnel rosters. The broader AppSorteos platform collects account, payment, social-media, log, cookie, web beacon, and third-party-button data; the page loads Google/analytics, AddThis/ShareThis-style sharing, Stripe, and social integrations. Better than the worst ad farms, but not privacy-first.
Ease4.6★
Very easy. Paste names, choose number of teams or participants per team, optionally add a title/leaders, then generate. The leader convention using * is simple once explained.
Design3.9★
Clean and modern compared with many randomizer pages. It is surrounded by giveaway-platform navigation, share widgets, and marketing content, but the actual tool is readable and straightforward.
Account friction4.6★
Generating, downloading, printing, and copying works without an account. Saving or sharing directly from the app triggers login/signup.
Review notes

Summary

A good simple random team generator with better output tools than most. Random-only, with no balancing or repeat optimization, and the privacy/ad-tech/social-platform footprint is not great.

Pricing: The random team generator is free and works without an account. AppSorteos also has paid plans for its broader giveaway/promotion platform; saving or sharing results directly from the app requires login/account creation.

Pros

  • Very easy paste-and-generate workflow
  • Number-of-teams or participants-per-team mode
  • Optional group leaders using * prefix
  • Clean result display with title
  • Download, print, copy, and copy-for-Excel actions
  • No account required for basic generation
  • Supports up to 50 teams

Cons

  • Random-only grouping
  • No balancing, constraints, or repeat optimization
  • Save/share require account creation
  • No CSV/spreadsheet import
  • No Zoom-specific export
  • Privacy footprint includes analytics, social widgets, and broader giveaway-platform data collection
