RandTools Random Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Privacy-conscious basic random team splitting with copy output

Free, no accountprivacy-friendlybasic-randomizer
Browser: YesNo account: YesCopy results to clipboard: YesPaste names: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.1★
Fair random balanced sizes via Fisher-Yates, but no skill balance, attribute balance, constraints, repeat avoidance, or quality diagnostics.
Features1.8★
Covers the basic random-split workflow well: paste input, 2–6 team buttons, balanced team sizes, regenerate, and copy all. No team-size mode, custom team names, imports, attributes, constraints, repeats, saved lists, or solver controls.
Exports2★
Generated teams are displayed clearly and can be copied with Copy All. No CSV, Excel, PDF/image, share-link, Zoom, or saved-result export was visible.
Privacy4.2★
Strong for this category. The page is labeled browser-only, the privacy page says randomization and outputs stay on-device with no accounts and no first-party cookies, and live testing showed Google Analytics plus internal Next.js requests but no ad-tech auction traffic. Google Analytics and Cloudflare logs remain caveats.
Ease4.4★
Simple and fast: paste names, choose 2–6 teams, click Generate. Names stay in the input for reshuffling.
Design3.8★
Clean, modern, and uncluttered compared with many ad-heavy randomizer sites. The tool area is clear, though the site-wide tool catalog/sidebar takes some space.
Account friction5★
No signup, login, payment, or verification was required.
Review notes

Summary

A very good minimal splitter: clean, browser-only, and copyable, but intentionally lacks advanced grouping features.

Pricing: Free web tool. No signup, download, or account is required.

Pros

  • Generated teams successfully in live testing
  • Very simple paste-and-generate workflow
  • Balanced team sizes
  • Copy All output button
  • Browser-only generation claim
  • No account required
  • No ad-tech scripts observed beyond Google Analytics

Cons

  • Only supports 2–6 teams via visible controls
  • No team-size mode
  • No custom team names
  • No CSV/Excel/PDF/image export or share links
  • No attributes, constraints, skill balancing, or repeat handling
  • Google Analytics still collects usage data

Pros

  • Supports number-of-teams and members-per-team modes
  • Copy button
  • Clean focused UI
  • Documents crypto-backed Fisher–Yates shuffle
  • No account required

Cons

  • No file import/export
  • No custom team names
  • No constraints or attributes
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No share links or rich diagnostics

Quality2.4★
The rule model is promising, but the tested simple case produced badly uneven group sizes, so output quality is not consistently trustworthy.
Features4★
Strong controls: tags, hard/soft together/separate rules, priority grouping, drag/drop, violation list, auto-fix, restore clean state, and Excel export.
Exports3.3★
Excel export is available after grouping and includes violation details according to the UI. No CSV/PDF/share output was verified.
Privacy0.9★
Weak despite client-side claims: Google ads/consent, Microsoft Clarity, Facebook, Google Tag Manager/Analytics/DoubleClick, Cloudflare, and ad-quality domains loaded.
Ease2.9★
The simple parse/generate path is manageable, but tags, hard/soft rules, priority tags, violations, and fixes create a dense workflow.
Design3.2★
Organized into clear steps with violation lists and group cards. Some labels/results mix languages, such as group headings shown in Chinese.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

Ambitious and potentially useful for rules, but a basic balancing miss keeps it out of the top tier.

Pricing: Free. No account is required. The page includes ads/consent and donation prompts.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Tag system
  • Hard and soft together/separate rules
  • Priority tags
  • Violation list
  • Drag-and-drop adjustment
  • Auto-fix and restore clean state
  • Excel export

Cons

  • Simple balance test produced a 5/5/2 split for 12 people into 3 groups
  • Dense UI
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No pair matrix
  • Heavy ad/analytics/consent footprint

API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: YesUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.2★
Even random distribution plus optional organizer marking. No deeper optimization.
Features2.6★
Good for basic needs: two split methods, upload text, random organizer per team, sample/reset, and regeneration. No constraints, attributes, repeats, or saved rosters.
Exports3.5★
Copy results, CSV export, and TXT export are visible after generation.
Privacy3.1★
No account is needed. Google Tag Manager/Analytics and Cloudflare insights loaded, but the page is much less ad-heavy than several peers.
Ease4.2★
The workflow is direct: paste, choose split method, optionally enable organizers, and generate.
Design3.4★
Readable tool cards and clear generated-team cards. The surrounding site has many unrelated tool links below.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A solid basic randomizer because it includes leader assignment and CSV/TXT/copy output.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • By number of teams or members per team
  • Random organizer per team
  • Text upload
  • Copy results
  • CSV export
  • TXT export

Cons

  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No saved roster workflow
  • No manual result editor
  • No diagnostics
#67 3★

ChatGPT (Free Version)↗ opens tool website

Natural-language grouping when users are willing to verify the output

Free with ads/upsellsconstraintsai-assisted
Multi-round: PartialRepeat encounter limits: PartialUnique-contact: PartialHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialAttribute balance: PartialCustom group capacities: PartialPartial attendance by session: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: PartialAttribute total/sum: PartialCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: PartialFixed person placement: PartialGender/label balancing: PartialGoogle Sheets import: PartialHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: PartialNo account: PartialPair meeting count targets: PartialPair meeting matrix: PartialPartial attendance by session: PartialPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: PartialPer-session: PartialPerson/group visit count: PartialPinned people / fixed placements: PartialPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: PartialResult comments: YesResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: PartialSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: PartialSoft prefer-together: PartialSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: PartialCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: PartialUnique-contact: PartialVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: PartialWeighted soft: PartialZoom CSV: Partial
Quality2.8★
Can be surprisingly good for small, clearly specified grouping tasks, but it can also make silent mistakes. No deterministic seed, no guaranteed constraint satisfaction, no repeat matrix, and no solver validation. Manual checking is mandatory.
Features3.4★
High flexibility, but not productized group-generation depth. It can attempt arbitrary team counts, group sizes, balancing, keep-together/keep-apart rules, multi-round schedules, comments, and custom output formats. The catch is that these are language-model behaviors, not validated solver features; free-tier limits/context can also get in the way.
Exports3.2★
Can produce tables, Markdown, CSV-like text, explanations, and revised versions on request. There is no native group roster export button, Zoom export, persistent scenario file, or structured diagnostics unless the user asks and verifies the text.
Privacy2★
Poor fit for sensitive rosters unless configured carefully. Free/consumer ChatGPT may use submitted content to improve models depending on settings; chats are stored unless deleted or temporary chat is used; and users should not paste student/personnel data without the right account, consent, and data controls.
Ease4★
Easy if the request is simple and the user knows how to prompt. Harder than a purpose-built splitter because you need to describe rules clearly and then audit the answer for missing names, duplicates, or violated constraints.
Design4.4★
The chat interface is polished and readable. It is not a group-generator UI, but the general interaction design is much better than most randomizer sites.
Account friction3.5★
Free access exists and some use is possible without signing in, but practical saved workflows, higher limits, file/context features, and data controls depend on account state and plan limits.
Review notes

Summary

Flexible enough to be useful, but risky as a group generator because correctness is not guaranteed. Good for drafts, not trustworthy without manual verification.

Pricing: Free tier available with usage/model/tool limits. Higher limits, stronger models, and business privacy defaults require paid ChatGPT plans; free consumer chats may be used to improve models depending on data controls/settings.

Pros

  • Natural-language grouping instructions
  • Can handle unusual constraints in a draft
  • Can explain and revise assignments
  • Can format output as tables or CSV-like text
  • Useful for brainstorming grouping rules

Cons

  • Not a deterministic solver
  • Must manually check duplicates, omissions, and rule violations
  • Poor default fit for sensitive rosters
  • Free-tier limits and model variability
  • No native group-generator export or diagnostics
#68 3★

Chirag Mehta Team Maker↗ opens tool website

Lightweight random teams with editable team names and CSV output

Free, no accountstrong-exports
Browser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: YesCopy results to clipboard: PartialEditable/random team names: YesExcel export: YesPaste names: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: PartialCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: YesFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.2★
Fine random teams, with custom labels. It does not optimize membership quality or manage repeat pairings.
Features2.7★
Good conveniences for a simple randomizer: preset team-name themes, editable team names, HTML preview/new-window output, and CSV export. It has no constraints, balancing, repeat avoidance, saved rosters, or multi-round planning.
Exports3.5★
Stronger than most basic splitters because it can show HTML output in the page, open a new-window HTML result, and export CSV for Excel. There is no dedicated copy button or Zoom breakout export.
Privacy2.7★
No account and no giant consent/ad wall, but the page loaded Google Tag Manager/Analytics/DoubleClick and generation appears to post names to the site for output, so it is not ideal for sensitive rosters.
Ease4.3★
Paste names, set the number of teams, optionally edit team names, and generate. The slider-only team count is a little awkward, but the workflow is mostly obvious.
Design2.5★
Old but compact and readable. It is far less noisy than many ad-heavy randomizer pages, though visually dated.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

One of the better old-school simple team makers because it has custom/random team names and CSV export, but it remains a basic random splitter.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • CSV/Excel export
  • HTML preview and new-window output
  • Editable team names
  • Preset themed team names
  • Simple compact interface

Cons

  • No constraints or balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No saved rosters
  • No group-size mode
  • Dated interface
  • Names appear to be posted to the site for generation

Quick random teams with copy/download/share and local browser processing claims

Free, no account

API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: PartialSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: PartialSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.1★
Slightly above the bare minimum because it supports team-size mode and uses Web Crypto according to page copy, but assignment quality remains random-only.
Features2.1★
Number-of-teams and team-size modes are useful basics. No constraints, leaders, attributes, repeats, saved rosters, or diagnostics.
Exports3.4★
Good for this tier: copy, download, and share buttons are available after generation. No spreadsheet/Zoom-specific export.
Privacy2.7★
The tool claims local browser processing and the privacy policy says no personal data collection, but the page loads Google Tag Manager/analytics-style scripts and Yandex-related storage.
Ease4.3★
Straightforward paste, choose method, generate workflow.
Design3.6★
Modern dark UI and readable results. The broad tools-hub navigation adds some noise but not much.
Account friction5★
No registration required.
Review notes

Summary

A solid simple random team tool with better outputs than many. Feature depth uses basic, and the site has analytics/tracking surface despite local-processing claims.

Pricing: Free web tool. No registration required.

Pros

  • No registration required
  • Number-of-teams or team-size mode
  • Copy, download, and share controls
  • Claims local browser processing
  • Claims Web Crypto randomness
  • Modern UI

Cons

  • No constraints or balancing
  • No leaders or roles
  • No saved rosters/history
  • No repeat optimization
  • No pair diagnostics
  • Analytics/tracking scripts despite privacy-friendly copy

Pros

  • No account required
  • By size or by number of groups
  • Remaining-member handling
  • Multiple group naming styles
  • Shuffle and regenerate
  • Statistics
  • Export and print controls

Cons

  • No constraints
  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No saved roster workflow

#71 3★

Diverse Group Generation↗ opens tool website

Small attribute-balanced grouping experiments

Free, no accountconstraints
Attribute balance: YesBrowser: YesNo account: YesResult quality diagnostics: PartialAttribute total/sum: PartialMinimum group sizes: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: YesAttribute total/sum: PartialCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3.6★
For attribute-balanced grouping, the assignment logic is more serious than random splitting. It only optimizes the simple attribute table it is given and offers no constraints or repeat history.
Features3★
The important feature is real attribute-distribution balancing across multiple attributes. It lacks many practical features: import/export, named rosters, constraints, repeat handling, saved scenarios, and result editing.
Exports1.4★
Results are shown as tables of member IDs and attribute distributions. There is no obvious CSV, copy, PDF/image, share link, or structured export.
Privacy2★
No account is required and the app appears lightweight, but the page loads Google advertising infrastructure and Vercel analytics. There is no strong privacy posture for roster data.
Ease2.2★
The concept is clear once understood, but the names-first workflow most people expect is missing. You have to build attributes/options and edit member rows manually.
Design3.1★
Clean enough for a prototype, with collapsible sections and visible distribution counts. Some UI text and duplicate-looking options are rough.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A genuinely interesting balancing prototype with better assignment logic than most simple randomizers, but too rough and output-limited for normal classroom or workshop use.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • Balances multiple attributes
  • Shows per-group attribute distributions
  • Uses an optimization/genetic-algorithm approach
  • No account required
  • Custom attributes/options

Cons

  • No names-first paste workflow
  • No import or export
  • No constraints or repeat avoidance
  • Members are displayed as IDs rather than normal roster names
  • Prototype-like UI and limited practical workflow
  • Loads Google advertising infrastructure
#72 3★

FateWheel Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Visual random team assignment with team-count/team-size modes

Free with ads/upsells
Browser: YesNo account: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialGender/label balancing: PartialPaste names: PartialSaved result history: PartialCSV import: YesTeam leader / representative assignment: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: PartialGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: YesStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: YesUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.2★
Balanced random team sizes and optional random leaders, but no attribute balancing beyond WIP controls, no constraints, no repeat minimization, and no quality diagnostics.
Features2.5★
Useful basics: CSV/Excel/TXT import, editable member rows, drag reorder, number-of-teams mode, team-size mode, random team leader assignment, and effects. Gender/label balancing is explicitly marked work-in-progress, and there are no real constraints, repeat handling, or multi-session controls.
Exports1★
Weak. Results are visible on screen and the FAQ suggests copying text or taking a screenshot, but no copy, CSV, PDF/image, share-link, or Zoom breakout export button was visible in live testing.
Privacy2.8★
Mixed. The privacy policy says local wheel entries/settings stay in browser unless explicitly saved/shared, and the tested assignment did not require an account. However, the site uses Google Analytics/Clarity-style tracking, records spin statistics, supports ads, and saved/shared/account features send data to servers.
Ease3.8★
The default roster makes it immediately testable, and generation worked without login. Adding members is primarily row/input based, while bulk workflows rely more on file import than a clear paste textarea.
Design4★
Polished, modern, and visual. The animated assignment flow, effects, dark mode, and readable team cards are stronger than many basic randomizers.
Account friction5★
No account, signup, payment, or verification was required to generate teams in the tested flow.
Review notes

Summary

A good visual random splitter with file import, team-size mode, and leader assignment, held back by weak outputs and no real balancing/constraint solver.

Pricing: Free web tool with no account required for the tested generation flow. The wider site has login, saved-wheel, pricing, and ad-supported surfaces.

Pros

  • Generated teams successfully in live testing
  • CSV, Excel, and TXT import
  • Generate by number of teams or desired team size
  • Optional random team leader assignment
  • Editable member rows with drag reorder
  • Polished visual assignment flow
  • No account required

Cons

  • No dedicated copy/export/share controls visible for results
  • Gender/label balancing is marked work-in-progress
  • No constraints or attribute balancing verified
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No result quality diagnostics
  • Analytics, ads, and saved/shared account features complicate privacy

Exports1.8★
Copy Results is useful, but there is no file export.
Privacy2.2★

Design2.8★

Account friction5★

Review notes

Summary

A narrow but useful constraint-aware splitter. The exclusion-pair support is valuable, but this is not a general-purpose group planning product.

Pricing: Free, ad-supported web tool. No account is needed for the generator.

Pros

  • Minimum and maximum group-size limits
  • Hard exclusion pairs
  • Browser-side processing claim
  • Clear error behavior for impossible constraints
  • Copy Results button
  • No account required

Cons

  • Narrow Secret Santa framing
  • No CSV/download/export
  • No team-count mode
  • No custom team names
  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat planning or diagnostics
  • Google ad/analytics privacy caveats

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.2★
Balanced random grouping only. It is practical for one-off classroom groups, not for constraints or optimized group quality.
Features2.6★
Useful basic grouping features: by-size/by-count modes, presets, shuffle, copy, CSV, and local current-list storage. No constraints, attributes, repeated rounds, or saved multi-class roster management.
Exports3.4★
Copy and CSV export both exist, and CSV download worked. No PDF/image, share link, Zoom export, or richer result format.
Privacy1.9★
The tool says names stay in browser local storage, which is a good design choice. The page also loaded Google Analytics/Tag Manager, Microsoft Clarity, Contentsquare, Ahrefs analytics, Stripe, and other product-site scripts.
Ease3.8★
The grouping workflow works, but it requires applying names before group creation. That extra step makes it less immediate than the simplest splitters.
Design3.4★
Modern and classroom-oriented. Some emoji/icons rendered garbled in testing, and the page is embedded in a broader product-marketing site.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed for the free tool.
Review notes

Summary

A good classroom utility with CSV/copy output and a name picker, limited to basic random grouping.

Pricing: Free tool. No account is required for the picker/group maker, though the surrounding Lekktura product has login, school, pricing, and Stripe/payment surfaces.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Name picker and group maker in one page
  • By size or by count
  • Quick presets
  • Copy results
  • CSV export
  • Local current-list storage

Cons

  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No saved multi-class roster manager
  • Requires an apply-names step
  • Heavy product-site analytics/tracking surface

API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: PartialEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: PartialUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.1★
Slightly above bare random because captains mode can nominate leaders, but it is random-only assignment.
Features2.2★
Better than the bare minimum thanks to random captains, regenerate, share, copy, and recent history. No constraints, balancing attributes, saved rosters, or repeat planning.
Exports3.1★
Copy All Teams, Share, and local recent results are useful. No CSV, PDF, spreadsheet, or Zoom-specific export.
Privacy2.3★
The tool says randomization is client-side and stores history locally, which is good. The page loads Google AdSense, Funding Choices, and Google Analytics.
Ease4.6★
Very straightforward: paste names, pick team count, generate. Preset team-count buttons are quick but less flexible than arbitrary counts.
Design3.6★
Clean and readable, with useful guidance below the tool. Ads add some noise.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

A strong basic random team generator with nice copy/share/history touches. It is not a solver, but it does the simple job well.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

Pros

  • Free
  • No account required
  • Paste names textarea
  • Equal teams or random captains
  • Regenerate button
  • Copy all teams
  • Share button
  • Recent results stored locally
  • Client-side cryptographic randomness claim

Cons

  • Preset team counts only
  • No group-size mode
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No saved rosters
  • No CSV/PDF export
  • AdSense/Funding Choices privacy footprint
#77 3★

Pandos Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Guided team formation workflow when email/server processing is acceptable

Free with ads/upsellsconstraints
Attribute balance: PartialNo account: PartialCSV export: PartialSelf-join participant link: PartialGender/label balancing: PartialShareable result links: PartialSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV import: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: PartialAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: PartialGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: PartialPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: NoSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: PartialShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: PartialCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.8★
Random mode produced balanced team sizes. Criteria mode is visible, including availability/gender/personality, but it was not verified end-to-end and no solver diagnostics are shown.
Features3.1★
Notable workflow features: workspace creation, random or criteria generation, visible availability/gender/personality criteria options, manual entry, CSV import, invitation links, team-size or team-count generation, and automatic/manual team names. Missing direct paste, result editing, repeat planning, capacity controls, and quality diagnostics.
Exports2★
Preview works. CSV file and result link are offered only after entering an email. No direct copy, CSV download, Excel, PDF/image, Zoom, or public share button was visible in live testing.
Privacy1.7★
Weak-to-mixed. Member data and workspace state were sent to app.pandos.io during live testing, and the page loads Google Analytics, Microsoft Clarity, and Freshworks support. The privacy policy covers personal data, usage data, cookies, analytics, and transfer/storage in Canada.
Ease2.3★
The wizard is understandable, but it is much slower than paste-and-generate tools. Manual entry is one row at a time, and export/share requires an email address.
Design3.2★
Professional SaaS-style wizard with clear steps and progress, but the interaction is clunky for quick ad hoc team splitting.
Account friction3★
No signup was required to generate and preview a small team set, but the tool has sign-in prompts, a 20-member free limit, and requires email for CSV/link delivery.
Review notes

Summary

More structured than most basic splitters, but slow, server-heavy, and email-gated for exports/links.

Pricing: Free tool flow allows up to 20 members without signup. More members require signup/contact. CSV/link delivery asks for an email address; the broader Pandos product has paid/business surfaces.

Pros

  • Generated and previewed teams successfully in live testing
  • Guided four-step workflow
  • Supports team-count and members-per-team generation modes
  • CSV import option
  • Invitation-link member collection option
  • Criteria mode advertises availability, gender, and personality
  • Automatic or manual team naming

Cons

  • No simple paste textarea for quick rosters
  • CSV/link output requires email
  • Member data is sent to Pandos servers
  • No direct copy/download controls visible
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No transparent quality diagnostics
  • Analytics/Clarity/Freshworks are privacy caveats

Review notes

Summary

A polished casual team generator with useful copy/share flow, not an advanced grouping tool.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Paste names panel
  • By teams or by size
  • Saved lists
  • Captain mode
  • Shuffle/regenerate
  • Copy All and Share controls

Cons

  • Paste panel is hidden by default
  • No constraints
  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No file export verified
  • Ad/consent scripts present

Quality2.7★
Balanced random teams using a documented CSPRNG shuffle, but no constraints or repeat minimization.
Features2.1★
Team-count slider, roster management, fullscreen, and strong algorithm/privacy explanation. No advanced grouping controls.
Exports0.8★
Team cards display on page, but no copy, download, CSV, PDF, or share button was visible.
Privacy4.3★
Strong claim and implementation posture: in-browser Web Crypto/Fisher-Yates, no account, no storage, and no obvious ad-tech scripts in source inspection.

Design3.2★

Account friction5★

Review notes

Summary

A privacy-first basic splitter with good randomness documentation, but very limited outputs and no advanced rules.

Pricing: Free web tool. No signup required.

Pros

  • Generated balanced team cards
  • Web Crypto + Fisher-Yates fairness documentation
  • Strong in-browser/no-storage privacy positioning
  • No account required
  • Team-count slider
  • Fullscreen mode

Cons

  • No copy/download/share/export controls visible
  • Separate Add step adds friction
  • No team-size mode
  • No custom team names
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat planning or diagnostics

Review notes

Summary

A clean simple generator with multiple draws; good for alternate random arrangements, not constraint solving.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Clean pasted-list workflow
  • Number of groups control
  • Number of draws control
  • Multiple generated arrangements
  • Readable group cards
  • Light observed third-party footprint

Cons

  • No constraints
  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat minimization across draws
  • No saved roster workflow
  • No file export verified
  • Headless automation observed React hydration flakiness

Strong for presentation-style batch picking, especially with weights/local rosters, but not a full group-assignment optimizer.

Pros

  • Ad-free positioning
  • No signup required
  • Pick-count mode can draw a full team per spin
  • Weighted entries
  • Saved rosters in localStorage
  • Per-name colors and fullscreen

Cons

  • Wheel workflow, not one-click full partitioning
  • No direct export verified
  • No hard constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No quality diagnostics

Quality2.1★
Balanced random team sizes and a claimed Fisher-Yates/secure-random approach, but no skill/attribute balancing, constraints, repeat avoidance, or diagnostics.
Features1.8★
Basic random splitting only: paste input, number-of-teams spinner, 2–5 quick team buttons, shuffle again, clear/reset, and copy. No team names, imports, attributes, constraints, saved lists, or advanced modes were visible.
Exports1.8★
Generated teams can be copied. No CSV, Excel, PDF/image, Zoom, share-link, or saved-result output was visible in live testing.
Privacy3.2★
Moderately good. The privacy policy says tool input is processed locally in the browser and not collected/stored/transmitted, but the site uses Google AdSense, Cloudflare RUM, cookies, and possible analytics/advertising cookies.
Ease4.1★
Paste names, choose a count, and generate. The name counter, quick team-count buttons, shuffle again, and clear controls are helpful. The page copy mentions team-size mode, but that was not visible in the tested UI.
Design3.4★
Clean utility-site layout with a clear tool card and dark-mode support. The large sidebar and ad space make it less focused than the best minimal tools.
Account friction5★
No signup, login, payment, or verification was needed.
Review notes

Summary

A competent minimal random splitter with copy output and browser-side processing claims, but little beyond basic team-count generation.

Pricing: Free ad-supported web tool. No account is required.

Pros

  • Generated teams successfully in live testing
  • Simple one-name-per-line paste input
  • Balanced team sizes
  • Shuffle Again and Copy controls
  • Quick buttons for 2–5 teams
  • No account required
  • Privacy policy claims local browser processing for tool input

Cons

  • Team-size mode is claimed in copy but not visible in tested UI
  • No team names or result editing
  • No CSV, Excel, PDF/image export, or share link visible
  • No constraints or attribute/skill balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • Google AdSense and Cloudflare RUM are privacy caveats

Balanced random groups with optional skill-balancing claim. No transparent scoring or solver report.
Features3★
Player list, import, team count, custom team names, skill-balance option, share/export, and generated team cards are useful. No constraints, repeats, or diagnostics.
Exports3★
Share and Export buttons appear after generation. Exact export format was not deeply verified.
Privacy2.2★
No account is needed, but Google Tag Manager and Google ad/quality domains loaded. React errors appeared in testing.
Ease3★
Adding players one by one works, but is slower than paste-first roster tools. Import exists but was not deeply tested.
Design3.2★
Result cards are readable and the page explains the workflow, but the huge tool navigation around it is noisy.
Account friction4.8★
No signup is needed for generation, though a Sign In button exists.
Review notes

Summary

A decent basic team generator with share/export and skill-balance claims, slowed by manual input and rough implementation.

Pricing: Free. No account is required for the generator.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Player import path
  • Custom team names
  • Skill balancing option
  • Share button
  • Export button
  • Clear result cards

Cons

  • No paste-first roster workflow found
  • No keep-apart/keep-together rules
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No diagnostics
  • React hydration errors
  • Ad infrastructure present
#87 3★

WheelPlay Team Picker Wheel↗ opens tool website

Visual team picking with CSV export, representatives, and label/gender distribution options

Free, no accountconstraintsstrong-exports
Hard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialAttribute balance: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: YesPresentation: PartialSaved participant/team lists: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: PartialAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: PartialGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: PartialRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: YesUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.6★
Can create balanced random groups; advanced balancing/rules were not deeply validated.
Features3.7★
Gender/label distribution, representative picking, CSV download, custom team names, and preset same/different group options are stronger than a plain splitter.
Exports3.2★
Groups Board and Download Result (.csv) were visible after successful generation.
Privacy1.4★
No login is needed, but CloudFront, Google fonts, a QuikNode endpoint, and suspicious .beer script hosts loaded.
Ease2.1★
The live test required discovering that pick quantity must be set to all participants before normal grouping works.
Design3★
Visual wheel/group-board interface is useful, but the control model is cluttered.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

Potentially powerful for a free visual picker, but easy to misconfigure and not privacy-clean.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • CSV download
  • Group board
  • Representative picker
  • Gender/label distribution options
  • Custom team names
  • Preset same/different group option

Cons

  • Confusing pick quantity control
  • Easy to generate the wrong result
  • Unusual third-party script hosts
  • No repeat avoidance
  • Advanced rules not deeply verified
#88 2.9★

Classroom.Group Online Group Generator↗ opens tool website

Teachers who want a simple local web splitter and may later upgrade to the iOS Groups app

Free with ads/upsells
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialExcel export: PartialGender/label balancing: PartialImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: PartialFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: PartialGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: YesSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.2★
Slightly above a bare splitter because it avoids impossible/singleton group outputs and keeps previous groups, but the web tool is random-only.
Features2.2★
The web tool has group size/count and previous groups. Avoided peers, gender balancing, classes, bulk import, export/print, and sync are app features, not web features.
Exports1.4★
Weak on the web: visible results and previous groups, but no clear copy, CSV, PDF, print, or share workflow. The native app has export and print.
Privacy3.6★
Good for the web tool because generation and names are described as local. The site uses Google Analytics, and the app privacy policy lists Firebase/Crashlytics/TelemetryDeck usage and diagnostics.
Ease3.6★
The web flow is understandable, but adding students one by one is slower than pasting a whole roster into a textarea.
Design3.7★
Clean, teacher-friendly, and uncluttered. Much better than generic ad-heavy randomizer pages.
Account friction4.4★
The web generator does not require an account. Advanced app features require installing the app and some features are Pro/in-app purchase.
Review notes

Summary

A clean teacher-focused web splitter with good local-data posture, but most serious features live in the paid/native app.

Pricing: Online generator is free. The iOS app is free with in-app purchases: Pro Monthly $2.99, Pro Yearly $14.99, or Pro $39.99.

Pros

  • Clean teacher-focused UI
  • Group by size or count
  • Names saved locally on device
  • Generation described as local
  • Previous groups visible
  • Native app offers avoided peers, gender balancing, import, export, and print

Cons

  • Web tool lacks paste-a-roster textarea
  • Most advanced features require the app
  • No web copy/CSV/export workflow
  • No multi-session optimizer
  • Google Analytics on the site
  • App has Firebase/Crashlytics/TelemetryDeck diagnostics

Summary

A pleasant basic team generator with local roster saving, limited to simple random teams.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Simple pasted-list workflow
  • Quick team-count buttons
  • Regenerate
  • Copy results
  • Local saved player lists
  • Light observed host footprint

Cons

  • No constraints
  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No file export verified
  • Team count capped by preset buttons

Review notes

Summary

A decent basic team picker with a couple of distribution modes. Privacy and feature depth keep it firmly in the middle of the pack.

Pricing: Free, ad-supported web tool. No account is needed for the team picker.

Pros

  • Textarea input
  • Team-count and optional team-size controls
  • Balanced/random/snake distribution choices
  • Copy Teams button
  • Shuffle/sort/clear helpers
  • No account required

Cons

  • Google ad/analytics privacy caveats
  • No CSV/download/export
  • No custom team names
  • No keep-apart or keep-together rules
  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat planning or diagnostics

Review notes

Summary

A solid basic team picker with copy/share/fullscreen, dragged down by severe ad-tech privacy issues.

Pricing: Free ad-supported web tool. No account required in the observed team workflow.

Pros

  • Generated balanced team cards
  • One-name-per-line input
  • File import option
  • Copy button
  • Share link control
  • Fullscreen mode
  • No account required

Cons

  • Heavy ad-tech consent flow
  • No team-size mode
  • No custom team names
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No diagnostics
#93 2.9★

Microsoft Excel↗ opens tool website

Spreadsheet users who want local files or organization-controlled rosters

Paid or subscriptionconstraintsstrong-exports
Multi-round: PartialRepeat encounter limits: PartialUnique-contact: PartialHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialAttribute balance: PartialCustom group capacities: PartialPartial attendance by session: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: PartialAttribute balance: PartialAttribute total/sum: PartialCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: PartialEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: YesFixed person placement: PartialGender/label balancing: PartialGoogle Sheets import: PartialHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialImage/PDF export: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: PartialNo account: PartialPair meeting count targets: PartialPair meeting matrix: PartialPartial attendance by session: PartialPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: PartialPer-session: PartialPerson/group visit count: PartialPinned people / fixed placements: PartialPresentation: PartialRepeat encounter limits: PartialResult comments: YesResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: PartialSaved class roster import: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: YesSaved scenarios/workspaces: YesScenario bundle: YesSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: PartialSoft prefer-together: PartialSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: YesStorage management/cleanup: YesTeam leader / representative assignment: PartialUnique-contact: PartialVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: PartialWeighted soft: PartialZoom CSV: Partial
Quality2.6★
Basic formula-based random groups are normal random-splitter quality. Better logic is possible with formulas/macros, but Excel has no built-in group solver, repeat minimizer, validation, or diagnostics.
Features2.8★
Flexible but DIY. Excel can handle arbitrary team counts, group sizes, saved rosters, notes, formulas, filters, imports, exports, macros, Power Query, and custom templates. Constraints, balancing, and repeat handling are only partial because users must design the logic themselves.
Exports4.8★
Excellent output surface: copy/paste, XLSX, CSV, PDF/print, local files, OneDrive/SharePoint sharing, versioning, and organization workflows. Stronger than most dedicated simple randomizers on export alone.
Privacy3.8★
Better than many web tools because workbooks can stay local/offline or inside an organization-controlled Microsoft 365 tenant. Not automatically private: cloud storage, connected experiences, diagnostic/service data, links, macros, and sharing permissions all need care.
Ease2★
Not easy as a group generator. Excel users can build a random grouping sheet with formulas or templates, but this is setup work, not paste names and click generate.
Design3.7★
Excellent spreadsheet UI for people who know Excel, but no purpose-built grouping interface unless you build one with a template, macro, or workbook design.
Account friction2.2★
Desktop Excel requires a license or Microsoft 365 access, and Excel for the web requires a Microsoft account. Organizations may already have it, but it is not a no-account web tool.
Review notes

Summary

Slightly better than Google Sheets for privacy/offline control, but a DIY spreadsheet workflow rather than a real group-generator product.

Pricing: Included in Microsoft 365 plans or standalone Office licenses; Excel for the web is available with a Microsoft account, and organization/education pricing varies.

Pros

  • Very flexible for spreadsheet users
  • Excellent CSV/XLSX/PDF/print output
  • Can work offline or in controlled organization storage
  • Good for saved rosters, notes, and versioned workbooks
  • Extensible with formulas, templates, macros, and Power Query

Cons

  • Not purpose-built for group generation
  • Requires formulas/templates/macros
  • Microsoft account or license required for normal use
  • No built-in constraints or repeat optimizer
  • Privacy depends on local/cloud/sharing/admin setup
#94 2.9★

QuickWheelSpin Team Picker↗ opens tool website

Visual basic team picking with copy/share-image outputs

Free, no account
Browser: YesNo account: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialCopy results to clipboard: YesImage/PDF export: YesPaste names: PartialShareable result links: PartialStorage management/cleanup: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: YesMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.1★
Balanced random team sizes, but no skill balancing, gender/attribute balancing, constraints, repeat avoidance, or quality diagnostics.
Features2.3★
Basic team generation with entry add/edit/remove/clear, presets, number-of-teams controls, a split-method control for team count versus players-per-team behavior, dark mode, and local stored entries. No file import/export, attributes, constraints, saved scenarios, repeat planning, or advanced balancing.
Exports2.6★
Better than many basic splitters: Copy Teams, Share Results, social share buttons, and image downloads for share/Instagram story are visible. Live share-link creation produced an error-fallback URL because the Supabase endpoint failed, and there is no CSV, Excel, PDF, or Zoom export.
Privacy2.8★
Mixed. The privacy policy says wheel lists/settings are stored in browser local storage and shared-result data is temporarily stored on servers for about five minutes. The site uses Google Analytics, cookies/tracking language, and a Supabase endpoint for share links.
Ease3.7★
The built-in sample names make the tool immediately testable, and generate/copy are straightforward. Bulk paste is claimed in the page copy, but the visible tested workflow centered on entry rows and add/edit/remove controls rather than a clear paste textarea.
Design3.8★
Modern and pleasant, with a focused app panel, modal results, dark-mode/language controls, and clear entry management. Some icon-only controls require exploration.
Account friction5★
No signup, login, payment, or verification was needed.
Review notes

Summary

A polished basic random team picker with good copy/social-image sharing, but no real balancing logic and a share-link failure in live testing.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account is required.

Pros

  • Generated balanced teams successfully in live testing
  • Entry add, edit, remove, and clear controls
  • Number-of-teams and advertised players-per-team split modes
  • Copy Teams output
  • Share modal with image/social options
  • Dark mode and language controls
  • No account required

Cons

  • Share-link generation produced an error-fallback URL in live testing
  • No CSV, Excel, PDF, or Zoom export
  • No constraints or attribute/skill balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No result quality diagnostics
  • Google Analytics and temporary server-side share storage are privacy caveats

  • Custom separators

Quality2.1★
Balanced random distribution only. Good enough for quick teams, not for constraints or repeat reduction.
Features2.4★
Covers the basic random grouping workflow with pasted names, file upload, by-count/by-size modes, and regeneration. No grouping rules, attributes, repeats, or optimization controls.
Exports3.5★
TXT and CSV exports are useful and visible after generation. No PDF/image, share link, copy button, or Zoom-specific format.
Privacy3★
Better than ad-heavy utilities because no account is required and the site claims browser-side processing with no stored information. The page loaded Google Analytics/Tag Manager and an additional third-party domain, so it is not tracker-free.
Ease4.5★
Very easy: paste names, choose a split mode, set the number, and generate.
Design3.5★
Clean and readable with multilingual navigation. The page is generic but not chaotic.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A solid basic splitter because TXT/CSV export is built in. Assignment quality remains random-only.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • By group count or group size
  • File upload
  • TXT export
  • CSV export
  • Multilingual interface

Cons

  • No constraints or balancing attributes
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No saved roster/history workflow
  • No manual result editing
  • Tracking scripts present

Iframe-based random teams with custom names, leaders, copy, and image export

Quality2.2★
Plain balanced random groups with optional leader labels.
Features2.5★
Basic split modes, custom team names, leader assignment, shuffle, copy, and image export. No constraints, attributes, repeat planning, saved rosters, or diagnostics.
Exports3★
Copy Text and Save as Image are available. No CSV/TXT/PDF or shareable result link was verified.
Privacy0.8★
Very weak for roster data: Google ads/consent, AddToAny, Google Tag Manager/Analytics, Cloudflare/CDN, and many ad-related requests loaded on the host page.
Ease3.7★
The embedded app is simple once located. The outer page makes it harder to focus.
Design2.7★
The iframe form and team cards are clear; the surrounding RapidWebApp page is cluttered with unrelated content and ads.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

Useful basic iframe tool with image export and leaders, weakened by a noisy ad-heavy host page.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • By number of teams or people per team
  • Custom team names
  • Optional team leader assignment
  • Shuffle results
  • Copy text
  • Save as image

Cons

  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No CSV export
  • No saved roster workflow
  • Ad-heavy outer page and consent system

Spinwheely Random Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Visual browser-side random teams with PDF, CSV, and text export

Free with ads/upsellsstrong-exports
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: YesEditable/random team names: PartialImage/PDF export: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: PartialEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: YesMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.1★
Plain balanced random teams. Useful for one-off splitting, but no constraints, attribute balancing, repeat minimization, or quality diagnostics.
Features2.3★
Decent simple-tool features: team count, naming style, max-members/equal-size setting, one-by-one member entry, results tab, clear controls, and exports. No bulk paste, imports, attributes, constraints, saved rosters, repeat planning, or diagnostics.
Exports4★
Strong for a simple randomizer: export menu includes PDF, CSV, and text. No Zoom-specific export, shareable result links, spreadsheet import, or richer report/history workflow.
Privacy2★
Mixed. The privacy policy says wheel content is local by default and the generator appeared browser-side in testing, but the site is ad-supported with Google/partner advertising, local storage, account/save/share paths, and consent-based ad tracking.
Ease3.2★
The UI is clear once loaded, but adding members one by one is slower than paste-based team generators. The first load also showed a transient React hydration error before the actual tool appeared.
Design3.7★
Polished modern UI with clear setup/results tabs and visual team cards. It feels more like an app than an old utility page, though ads/account chrome add some clutter.
Account friction4.8★
No login is needed for the random team generator itself, although login/signup buttons are present for broader Spinwheely features.
Review notes

Summary

A good-looking simple random team splitter with unusually strong exports, but no bulk paste and no real assignment intelligence.

Pricing: Free ad-supported web tool. No account is required for the generator; account buttons exist for broader Spinwheely features.

Pros

  • Polished app-style interface
  • Runs assignment in the browser
  • Team count, naming style, and max-members settings
  • PDF, CSV, and text export options
  • No account required for generation

Cons

  • No bulk paste roster workflow
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No saved roster/result history workflow
  • Ad-supported privacy posture

TeamShufflr↗ opens tool website

In-person workshops that want printable team cards rather than a normal result table

Free, no account
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesPresentation: PartialEditable/random team names: YesImage/PDF export: PartialPaste names: PartialSaved class roster import: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: PartialRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: YesWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.2★
Random card assignment can mix people during activities, but it does not optimize assignments or reduce repeats algorithmically.
Features2.7★
Niche but real: participant/card setup, team sizes, card features, names/colors/icons, printable cards, and repeated shuffling during workshops. No constraints, attributes, diagnostics, or repeat optimization.
Exports3.4★
Good if you want printable cards or workshop handouts. Weak if you just want copy/CSV/Zoom-ready groups.
Privacy2.6★
German/GDPR-style privacy pages and cookie settings are better than many utilities, but the site uses Google Analytics/GTM cookies.
Ease2.8★
More setup than a normal random splitter because the product centers on creating cards and using them during an event.
Design4★
Polished and friendly. The app and landing page are much better designed than most randomizer pages.
Account friction4.8★
The simple card workflow can be used without an account.
Review notes

Summary

A clever physical-workshop card system, not a normal group generator. Useful niche, but it should not be rated like a solver.

Pricing: Basic version is free. The site mentions custom branded cards by contacting them.

Pros

  • Polished workshop-focused design
  • Printable team cards
  • Can use names, colors, and icons
  • Useful for repeated in-person shuffles
  • No account needed for basic card creation
  • Free basic version

Cons

  • Indirect card workflow
  • Not a normal group result table
  • No copy/CSV/Zoom export
  • No constraints or balancing solver
  • No repeat-minimization engine
  • Google analytics/tag cookies

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: YesFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: PartialSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Standard random grouping. Fisher-Yates shuffle and spreadsheet output are useful, but result quality remains random-only.
Features2★
Basic: number-of-groups, people-per-group, live name count, shuffle again, reset. No constraints, balancing, multi-session repeats, leaders, attributes, or diagnostics.
Exports4★
Strong for a simple splitter: copy-to-clipboard in spreadsheet-friendly columns, CSV download, and print view. No Zoom-specific export or share links.
Privacy1.5★
Bad for sensitive rosters despite local tool processing. The page shows a consent dialog for 141 partners, including precise geolocation and ad/personalization purposes, plus Google tags and a heavy WordPress/ad-tech environment.
Ease4.5★
Very easy: paste names, choose split method, generate. The surrounding blog page is busy, but the embedded tool itself is clear.
Design3.7★
The tool UI is clean and modern enough, but it sits inside a busy WordPress/Excel blog page with newsletter/promo/navigation clutter.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

A good basic splitter with strong spreadsheet-oriented exports. Privacy is the major drag because the surrounding site has a heavy consent/ad-tech layer.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

Pros

  • Easy paste-and-generate workflow
  • Number-of-groups or people-per-group mode
  • Shuffle again
  • Copy in spreadsheet-friendly columns
  • CSV download
  • Print view
  • Runs locally according to page copy

Cons

  • No constraints or balancing
  • No repeat optimization
  • No leaders or pinned placements
  • No Zoom-specific export
  • Heavy 141-partner privacy/ad-tech consent layer
  • Busy blog-page wrapper
