Polished no-account browser-side random teams from a saved roster

Free, no account
Browser: YesNo account: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialEditable/random team names: PartialPaste names: YesShareable result links: PartialVisual/theme customization: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: PartialEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.1★
Balanced random team sizes using a claimed Fisher-Yates shuffle, but no skill/attribute balancing, constraints, repeat avoidance, or diagnostics.
Features2.2★
Good roster ergonomics for a simple random splitter: editable entries, paste-list import, shuffle/clear, local remembered list, increment/decrement team count, and generate again. No team-size mode, custom constraints, attributes, saved scenarios, file import/export, or multi-round features.
Exports0.8★
Weak. Generated teams are shown on screen, but no dedicated copy, CSV, Excel, PDF/image, Zoom, or explicit team-result share control was visible in live testing. Share-by-link is claimed in marketing copy but not verified as a UI action.
Privacy3.5★
Fairly good but underdocumented. No third-party analytics/ad scripts were observed during the tested page load, and the page says lists are remembered on device with no signup. However, no privacy policy page was found, and share-by-link behavior was not verifiable.
Ease4★
Easy to try because it ships with a sample roster. Editing rows, adding names, importing a pasted list, changing team count, and generating again are straightforward.
Design4.2★
One of the nicer basic splitters visually: clean, responsive, and readable with tasteful team cards and randomized team names.
Account friction5★
No signup, login, payment, or verification was required.
Review notes

Summary

A very pleasant basic random splitter with good roster input/editing, but weak outputs and no advanced balancing logic.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account is required.

Pros

  • Generated balanced teams successfully in live testing
  • Polished visual design
  • Editable roster rows plus add/remove controls
  • Paste-list import
  • Generate-again reshuffling
  • Local saved roster behavior is advertised
  • No account required
  • No third-party tracking scripts observed in live testing

Cons

  • No visible copy/export controls for generated teams
  • No team-size mode
  • No constraints or attribute/skill balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • Share-by-link is claimed but no explicit team-result share action was visible
  • No privacy policy page found
#103 2.9★

Zoom breakout room assignment↗ opens tool website

Assigning live meeting participants directly into Zoom rooms

Free, no accountconstraints
Multi-round: PartialHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialCustom group capacities: PartialPartial attendance by session: PartialBrowser: PartialZoom CSV: YesSelf-join participant link: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: PartialAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: YesGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: PartialNo account: NoPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: PartialPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: PartialPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: YesPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: PartialSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: PartialSelf-join participant link: PartialShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: PartialSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: YesStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: Yes
Quality2.3★
Automatic assignment creates evenly sized rooms, which is fine for live one-off breakouts. It does not balance attributes, satisfy constraints, reduce repeats across varied sessions, or explain quality.
Features3★
Moderate for Zoom-specific grouping: automatic even assignment, manual assignment, participant self-select, room renaming, moving/swapping, up to 100 rooms, CSV pre-assignment, saved assignments for recurring meetings, timers, broadcast, and activity status. Missing general group-generator features: constraints, balancing attributes, repeat optimization, multi-session planning outside recurring-room saves, and diagnostics.
Exports2.8★
The output is directly actionable inside Zoom, which is valuable. Outside Zoom it is weak: pre-assignment CSV import exists, but there is no normal roster export, spreadsheet/report output, or shareable assignment page for other tools.
Privacy3.2★
Zoom has serious enterprise/security documentation, DPAs, and education compliance materials, but this is a hosted meeting platform processing participant identities, room names, telemetry/service data, chats/recordings depending on settings, and account/admin data. Better than ad-tech utilities, not private like a local tool.
Ease3★
Easy enough for hosts already comfortable with Zoom, but awkward as a group generator: breakout settings must be enabled, pre-assignment lives in the web portal, CSV setup uses email addresses, and participants often need to be signed in correctly.
Design3.6★
The in-meeting breakout UI is functional and integrated into the meeting experience. It is not a dedicated planning interface, but it is much better than exporting from a randomizer and then manually recreating rooms.
Account friction2★
High friction for a “group generator”: the host needs a Zoom account/meeting, breakout settings enabled, and pre-assigned participants need matching Zoom accounts/emails for assignments to apply reliably.
Review notes

Summary

Very useful if the only destination is a live Zoom meeting. Not a general-purpose group generator and not a quality optimizer.

Pricing: Breakout rooms are available in Zoom meetings, including free/basic contexts, but meeting duration, participant capacity, admin controls, and large-meeting needs depend on Zoom plan and account settings.

Pros

  • Built directly into Zoom meetings
  • Automatic, manual, and self-select room assignment
  • CSV pre-assignment import
  • Can save breakout assignments for recurring meetings
  • Hosts can move/swap participants live
  • Timers, broadcast, and activity status help live facilitation

Cons

  • Only useful inside Zoom
  • Host/account/settings friction
  • Pre-assignment depends on participant Zoom account emails
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No general export/report output
  • No repeat optimization across arbitrary sessions

Quality2.4★

Exports3.8★

Privacy1.8★
No account or obvious third-party tracking in the targeted run, but the invalid certificate is a serious trust issue.

Account friction5★

Review notes

Summary

Featureful for a simple tool, but an invalid HTTPS certificate sharply reduces trust.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • By number of groups or members per team
  • Custom team names and presets
  • Random leaders
  • Save/open names
  • Text/CSV/Markdown/PNG style export options

Cons

  • Invalid HTTPS certificate
  • Trust/access warning for users
  • No constraints
  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • Basic UI

  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No saved rosters
  • No CSV export verified
  • Google analytics scripts present
#107 2.8★

Games by Marco Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Minimal private-ish random teams with team-count or team-size mode

Free, no accountprivacy-friendlybasic-randomizer
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialEditable/random team names: PartialMinimum group sizes: PartialPaste names: YesStorage management/cleanup: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: PartialEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.1★
Plain balanced random groups. Good enough for one-off random teams, with no constraints, attribute balancing, repeat minimization, or quality diagnostics.
Features1.8★
Only basic random splitting features: paste names, number-of-teams mode, team-size mode, team prefix, shuffle again, clear, and local saved state. No constraints, attributes, imports, exports, team editing, saved histories, repeat planning, or diagnostics.
Exports1★
Very weak output surface. Results appear on the page, but there is no copy button, CSV/TXT/JSON export, print/PDF/image export, share link, Zoom export, or result history.
Privacy4.7★
Strong for a hosted web app. No account, ads, analytics, consent banner, or generation API requests were observed; only a favicon 404 appeared in console. Input/config appear to persist via localStorage on the device. No privacy policy was found, so it is not a perfect score.
Ease4.7★
Extremely simple: paste one name per line, choose number of teams or team size, optionally change the team prefix, and generate. The page has almost no distractions.
Design3.8★
Clean dark single-page UI with clear panels. It is plain, but much nicer and less cluttered than many randomizer pages.
Account friction5★
No signup, login, or payment is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A very clean minimalist random splitter. Privacy and simplicity are good; output and advanced grouping capability are almost nonexistent.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account is required.

Pros

  • Very easy focused interface
  • Number-of-teams and team-size modes
  • Team name prefix field
  • Shuffle again and clear controls
  • No account, ads, analytics, or consent banner observed
  • Runs in the browser with local saved state

Cons

  • No copy or export buttons
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No result diagnostics
  • No custom per-team names
  • No visible privacy policy
#108 2.8★

Generate-Random.org Random Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Simple random teams with strong text/CSV/JSON exports if hosted processing is acceptable

Free, no accountstrong-exports
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: PartialNo account: YesCSV export: YesAPI/programmatic access: PartialCopy results to clipboard: YesGoogle Sheets import: PartialMinimum group sizes: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: PartialAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: PartialHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: PartialSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.1★
Plain balanced random teams. Useful for one-off random splitting, but there is no constraint handling, attribute balancing, repeat minimization, or quality diagnostics.
Features2.5★
Good for a basic splitter: by-team-count and by-size modes, multiple separators, duplicate/empty cleanup, CSPRNG/Fisher-Yates claims, Copy All, TXT/CSV/JSON export, and an API-style request. No constraints, attributes, team naming, saved rosters, multi-round planning, or diagnostics.
Exports4★
Stronger than most simple randomizers: copy all plus TXT, CSV, and JSON export buttons. No Excel-native, Zoom-specific, image/PDF, share-link, or report-style output.
Privacy0.8★
Bad. Despite a privacy policy claiming no tracking, no analytics, no third-party services, and no advertising, live testing showed a Google-managed consent dialog, Google Analytics, Funding Choices, AdSense/PageAd requests, and a 210-partner consent message. The entered names were sent to a server API in a GET query string.
Ease4.1★
Easy basic workflow: paste names, choose number of teams or members per team, generate. Separator controls and terminal-style labels add some clutter but are understandable.
Design3.2★
Distinct terminal-style design and reasonably readable output. The page is long and SEO-heavy, but the tool area itself is clear.
Account friction5★
No account, signup, or payment is needed to generate teams.
Review notes

Summary

A capable basic random team splitter with unusually good exports, dragged down hard by hosted generation and a privacy story that conflicts with the observed ad/analytics consent stack.

Pricing: Free web tool. No signup is required.

Pros

  • Easy paste-and-generate workflow
  • Number-of-teams and members-per-team modes
  • Multiple separators and duplicate cleanup
  • Copy All plus TXT, CSV, and JSON exports
  • No account required
  • Generated balanced group sizes in live testing

Cons

  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No saved rosters or result history
  • Names are sent to a server API in the URL query
  • Observed ads/analytics contradict the privacy policy
  • Long SEO page around the tool
#109 2.8★

Google Sheets↗ opens tool website

Spreadsheet users willing to build their own random grouping template

Free with ads/upsellsconstraintsstrong-exports
Multi-round: PartialRepeat encounter limits: PartialUnique-contact: PartialHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialAttribute balance: PartialCustom group capacities: PartialPartial attendance by session: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: YesAttribute balance: PartialAttribute total/sum: PartialCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: PartialEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: PartialExcel export: YesFixed person placement: PartialGender/label balancing: PartialGoogle Sheets import: YesHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialImage/PDF export: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: PartialNo account: NoPair meeting count targets: PartialPair meeting matrix: PartialPartial attendance by session: PartialPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: PartialPer-session: PartialPerson/group visit count: PartialPinned people / fixed placements: PartialPresentation: PartialRepeat encounter limits: PartialResult comments: YesResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: YesSaved scenarios/workspaces: YesScenario bundle: PartialSelf-join participant link: PartialShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: PartialSoft prefer-together: PartialSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: YesStorage management/cleanup: YesTeam leader / representative assignment: PartialUnique-contact: PartialVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: PartialWeighted soft: PartialZoom CSV: Partial
Quality2.6★
Basic formula-based random groups are roughly normal random-splitter quality. Better results are possible with custom formulas/scripts, but there is no built-in solver, repeat minimization, constraint validation, or diagnostics.
Features2.8★
Flexible but DIY. Sheets can handle arbitrary team counts, group sizes, saved rosters, notes, formulas, filters, imports, exports, and custom scripts. Attribute balancing, keep-apart rules, fixed placement, or repeats are only partial because users must design formulas/scripts themselves; none of it is a built-in group-generator workflow.
Exports4.7★
Excellent export and sharing surface: copy/paste, CSV, XLSX, PDF/print, share links, collaboration, version history, and integration with the broader Google ecosystem. Output is where Sheets is much stronger than most simple web randomizers.
Privacy3.4★
Mixed. There is no ad-tech utility-page mess inside Sheets, and Workspace for Education has serious privacy terms. Rosters live in Google cloud under an account, sharing permissions can leak data if misconfigured, and consumer Google account data handling is not the same as a local/browser-only tool.
Ease2★
Not easy as a group generator. Entering a roster is easy, but random grouping requires formulas, sorting, a copied template, or Apps Script. Spreadsheet-comfortable users will be fine; everyone else gets setup work.
Design3.7★
The spreadsheet interface is mature, readable, collaborative, and reliable. It is not tailored to group generation, so the UX depends entirely on the sheet/template you build.
Account friction2★
A Google account is required for the normal workflow, and organizational users may need Workspace permissions. Not a no-account web splitter.
Review notes

Summary

Powerful as a platform, mediocre as a group generator. It can outperform many tiny randomizers if you know Sheets, but the actual group-generation workflow is DIY.

Pricing: Free with a Google account for consumer use; Google Workspace and Workspace for Education plans vary by organization.

Pros

  • Very flexible for spreadsheet users
  • Excellent CSV/XLSX/PDF/share output
  • Good for saved rosters and notes
  • Collaboration and version history
  • Can be extended with formulas, templates, and Apps Script

Cons

  • Not purpose-built for group generation
  • Requires formulas/templates/scripts
  • Google account required
  • No built-in constraints or repeat optimizer
  • Privacy depends on Google account/admin/sharing setup
#110 2.8★

GoOnlineTools Random Group Generator↗ opens tool website

Simple random groups with a modern UI, regenerate, statistics, and possible copy/download/share icons

Free with ads/upsellsconstraints
Hard keep-apart: PartialCustom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesResult quality diagnostics: PartialCopy results to clipboard: PartialEditable/random team names: PartialMinimum group sizes: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: PartialCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: PartialEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: PartialSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: YesSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: PartialUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.2★
Slightly above bare random because by-members mode and stats help, but assignments are random-only unless the advanced options prove more substantial.
Features2.5★
Better than the bare minimum: by groups/by members, regenerate, statistics, and advertised advanced options. No proven hard constraints, repeat optimization, or real diagnostics.
Exports2.8★
Result action icons and page copy indicate copy/download/share, but output formats are not as clear or strong as CSV/PDF-focused tools.
Privacy1.3★
Poor. The page runs ads/Funding Choices, Google account/sign-in scripts, Microsoft Clarity, and stores ad/recent/favorite/color-mode data. The policy also allows ad cookies and IP collection.
Ease4.3★
Easy paste-and-generate flow with by-groups/by-members modes and a clear regenerate button.
Design3.8★
Modern and readable, though ads and surrounding tool-site chrome add clutter.
Account friction4.8★
Generation works without sign-in. Sign-in is visible for site features such as favorites.
Review notes

Summary

A decent modern simple splitter, but the privacy/ad-tech load is bad and the advertised advanced controls are not enough to make it a solver.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required for generation; sign-in exists for the broader GoOnlineTools site.

Pros

  • Modern UI
  • Paste names textarea
  • By groups or by members
  • Regenerate button
  • Simple result statistics
  • No account needed for generation
  • PWA/offline claim

Cons

  • Heavy ad and consent stack
  • Microsoft Clarity and Google scripts
  • No verified hard constraint solver
  • No repeat optimization
  • Output formats unclear
  • Advanced claims feel stronger than the visible product
#111 2.8★

Gynzy Group Maker↗ opens tool website

Interactive whiteboard random groups inside Gynzy

Free with ads/upsells
Browser: YesNo account: YesPresentation: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialPaste names: YesSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialVisual/theme customization: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: YesRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.1★
Result quality is basic random splitting. Fine for quick classroom grouping, but it does not improve assignment quality beyond random groups of a chosen size.
Features2★
Basic group-generation depth: names, number of students per group, random layout, and reshuffle. No constraints, attribute balancing, imports, repeat handling, diagnostics, or group-count mode was visible.
Exports2.3★
The output is useful for live display on a Gynzy board, but there is no obvious CSV, copy, PDF/image, share, or Zoom-friendly export workflow for the generated groups.
Privacy1.8★
Poor for sensitive roster use. The site exposes a large Cookiebot surface with statistics/marketing categories and providers such as Google, RudderStack, Segment, Typeform, Vimeo, Intercom, Stripe, and Sprig. Necessary-only cookies can be selected, but the surrounding tracking stack is heavy.
Ease3.6★
Once the tool is open, the workflow is simple: paste names, set students per group, save, shuffle. The Cookiebot banner and onboarding screens add friction before the actual tool appears.
Design4.3★
Good interactive-whiteboard design. The output is large, visual, and classroom-friendly, which is the main reason to use it.
Account friction5★
The specific Group Maker tool opened without signing in.
Review notes

Summary

A nice presentation-oriented classroom randomizer, not a serious group planner. It is much better as a whiteboard reveal tool than as a constraint-aware generator.

Pricing: The Group Maker opens with “No account needed” from the Gynzy library page. Gynzy itself is a broader paid/education whiteboard platform, but this specific tool can be opened without signing in.

Pros

  • No account required for the tool
  • Good classroom whiteboard presentation
  • Simple name entry
  • Choose students per group
  • Shuffle/regenerate button
  • Clean visual output

Cons

  • Basic random splitter only
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No spreadsheet/copy/share export workflow
  • Heavy cookie/tracking surface around the site
  • Onboarding and cookie screens add friction

  • No repeat planning or diagnostics

Review notes

Summary

A clean no-account splitter with copy and reshuffle, limited to random grouping.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • By number of teams or team size
  • Reshuffle
  • Copy
  • Clear focused UI
  • Cryptographic randomization claim

Cons

  • No constraints
  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No saved rosters
  • No file export verified
  • Ad/analytics scripts present

Quality2.1★

Exports3.5★
Clear, Copy, and Download .CSV controls appeared after generation.
Privacy0.8★
Weak for participant names: the roster was sent to Thunderbit public-tool API endpoints, and Amplitude, Reddit, Bing, Iubenda, Google, and other marketing services loaded.
Ease3.4★

Design3.2★

Account friction3.8★

Review notes

Summary

Usable output and CSV export, but privacy and product-funnel concerns keep it below cleaner browser-side tools.

Pricing: Free public tool; broader Thunderbit product has sign-in and paid-product context.

Pros

  • No login required for public test
  • Simple pasted-list form
  • Table output
  • Copy control
  • CSV download

Cons

  • Roster sent to hosted API
  • Heavy marketing/analytics footprint
  • Chrome extension/sign-in funnel
  • No constraints
  • No attributes or repeat avoidance

Quality2.1★
Balanced random groups only.
Features2.3★
Good basic controls: by-team-count, by-people-per-team, custom team prefix, trim, dedupe, reshuffle, and local history controls. No constraints, attributes, repeats, or diagnostics.
Exports2.7★
An export menu is present after generation, but no specialized CSV/PDF/Zoom output was verified. Result cards are clear.
Privacy2.9★
The site prominently claims no login, data-private, and local save. Baidu analytics and CDN scripts loaded, so it is not tracker-free.

Design3.4★
Modern toolkit interface with readable result cards, though the page includes a lot of unrelated tool navigation.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A solid basic splitter with nice cleanup/prefix touches, but not a serious assignment optimizer.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • By team count or people per team
  • Trim empty lines and deduplicate
  • Custom team prefix
  • Reshuffle
  • Export menu
  • Local/history claims

Cons

  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No diagnostics
  • No verified specialized export format
  • Large toolkit navigation and analytics

Vertex42 Random Group Generator Template↗ opens tool website

Spreadsheet users who want a downloadable Excel/Google Sheets random grouping template

Free, no accountstrong-exports
Browser: PartialNo account: YesCSV export: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesCopy results to clipboard: YesEditable/random team names: YesExcel export: YesGoogle Sheets import: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: YesFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: YesHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: PartialSaved class roster import: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.2★
Slightly above plain web randomizers because spreadsheet sorting gives manual control, but random-only and explicitly not social-golfer solving.
Features2.4★
Useful spreadsheet basics: editable roster, group names, random sorting, two grouping layouts, and custom sorting. No constraints, attributes, repeat optimization, or diagnostics.
Exports4★
Strong because the result lives in Excel or Google Sheets and can be copied, printed, saved, or exported through spreadsheet tools.
Privacy3★
Roster data can stay in your own spreadsheet after download, which is good. The website page itself runs ads and Google tags.
Ease2.8★
Not as fast as a web splitter because you need to download/copy a spreadsheet and understand the RAND-sort workflow. Easy enough for spreadsheet users.
Design3★
The template page is dated but clear, and the spreadsheet screenshot explains the workflow well.
Account friction4.7★
No account required for the template page. Google Sheets use may involve a Google account.
Review notes

Summary

A useful spreadsheet workaround with honest documentation. Better than many web toys for people already in Excel, but not a serious group-generation product.

Pricing: Free template for private use. Downloadable Excel file and Google Sheets option; no account required for the template page.

Pros

  • Excel and Google Sheets options
  • No macros
  • Editable roster and group names
  • Reusable spreadsheet workflow
  • Spreadsheet-native saving/printing/export
  • Honest about not solving social golfer repeat minimization

Cons

  • Not a quick web generator
  • Requires spreadsheet comfort
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No repeat optimization
  • Website has ads/tracking
  • Private-use license limits redistribution
#121 2.8★

YesNoWheel Picker Random Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Basic browser-side random teams with bulk add and file import

Free, no account
Browser: YesNo account: YesGoogle Sheets import: PartialPaste names: YesCSV import: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: PartialHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain random balanced teams. No constraints, attribute balancing, repeat minimization, or quality diagnostics.
Features2★
Basic team splitting with better-than-average input options: single add, bulk paste, TXT/CSV/XLSX upload, CSV template, quick team-count buttons, and regenerate. No team-size mode was visible, and there are no constraints, attributes, saved rosters, repeat planning, or diagnostics.
Exports0.8★
Weak. Generated teams are shown on the page and can be regenerated, but no copy, CSV, text, PDF/image, share-link, Zoom, or saved-result output was visible in live testing.
Privacy3★
Mixed. Generation appeared browser-side with only Next.js RSC requests observed during testing. The privacy policy says data is not permanently stored, but it also describes cookies, Google Analytics, AdSense/advertising cookies, technical logs, and analytics data.
Ease4★
The default sample list makes it easy to try. Bulk paste and file upload are helpful, but the long page and lack of obvious result copy/export keep it from being top-tier simple.
Design3.3★
Modern enough and readable, with a clear app area. The surrounding article is very long and SEO-heavy.
Account friction5★
No signup, login, or payment is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A decent basic random splitter with unusually good input options, but weak outputs and no advanced grouping logic.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account is required.

Pros

  • Bulk paste names by line or comma
  • TXT, CSV, and XLSX upload support
  • CSV template download
  • Quick team-count buttons
  • Regenerate button
  • No account required

Cons

  • No visible copy/export for generated teams
  • No team-size mode visible in tested UI
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No saved roster or result history
  • Very long SEO article around the tool
#122 2.7★

ClassDojo Group Maker↗ opens tool website

Teachers already using ClassDojo who want quick classroom groups

Free with ads/upsellsconstraints
Hard keep-apart: YesCustom group capacities: PartialPartial attendance by session: PartialBrowser: PartialPresentation: PartialSaved participant/team lists: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialSaved class roster import: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: YesHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: NoPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: PartialPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: PartialRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: PartialSaved class roster import: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.4★
Slightly above a plain random splitter because keep-apart sets can prevent bad pairings. It does not optimize balance, preferences, repeated encounters, or multi-session coverage.
Features2.6★
Better than the weakest random splitters because it uses a saved class roster and supports keep-apart sets. Limited: random groups only, group size choices, no attribute balancing, no multi-session repeat minimization, no solver diagnostics, and no serious planning controls.
Exports1.5★
Very weak for export. The visible product positioning is in-app classroom display/use, not CSV, Excel, Zoom breakout export, share links, PDFs, or reusable result reports.
Privacy3.7★
ClassDojo has a much more serious student-privacy posture than ad-heavy utility sites: COPPA/FERPA/GDPR language, iKeepSafe certifications, Student Privacy Pledge, no selling/renting, and no student behaviorally targeted advertising. It is a hosted classroom platform collecting rosters, account data, device/usage data, and service-provider analytics, so it is not comparable to local/no-account tools.
Ease3.1★
For an existing ClassDojo teacher with a roster, group generation is probably very easy. For anyone else, it starts with downloading/signing into ClassDojo and setting up a class, which is a lot of friction for one random split.
Design4.1★
ClassDojo is polished, classroom-friendly, and much nicer than most randomizer pages. The public Group Maker page is clear, but it mostly advertises the app rather than exposing the tool directly.
Account friction1.5★
High. The Group Maker is part of the ClassDojo app, not a no-login webpage. You need the ClassDojo ecosystem and likely a teacher account/class roster for real use.
Review notes

Summary

Useful if your class already lives in ClassDojo, but weak as a general-purpose group generator. The keep-apart sets are real value; the app/account wall and limited export surface are the problem.

Pricing: ClassDojo Toolkit is advertised as free and included in the ClassDojo app. ClassDojo also has optional paid/premium family features, but the Group Maker workflow itself is positioned as free.

Pros

  • Polished classroom-friendly UI
  • Uses existing ClassDojo class roster
  • Random groups of any size
  • Keep-apart sets for students who should not be grouped together
  • Free inside ClassDojo Toolkit
  • Strong education privacy posture compared with ad-heavy utilities

Cons

  • Not a standalone group generator
  • Requires ClassDojo app/account ecosystem
  • No CSV/Excel/Zoom export surface
  • No balancing or repeat optimization
  • Hosted student roster data
  • Mostly useful only for existing ClassDojo classrooms

Quality2.1★

Features2.2★

Exports2.6★
Per-team copy links and a Download button are visible. No CSV/Excel/Zoom/share workflow was verified.
Privacy1.8★
HTML loads Google Tag Manager/gtag, a pub.network ad script, and consent-management UI. Not a privacy-first choice.
Ease3.2★
Straightforward textarea plus team presets, but the page is busy and a consent overlay interfered with automated clicks.
Design2.5★
Functional utility-site layout with lots of navigation and advertising chrome.
Account friction4.5★
No account needed for the generator; login is only for site features such as favorites.
Review notes

Summary

Useful basic splitter with copy/download affordances, held back by ad/tracking clutter and no advanced grouping logic.

Pricing: Free web tool. Login/favorites are optional; ads and consent management are present.

Pros

  • Textarea roster input
  • 2/3/5 team presets plus numeric team count
  • Duplicate-removal and split-by-space toggles
  • Per-team copy links
  • Download button
  • No account required

Cons

  • Ad/tracker heavy
  • Consent overlay can get in the way
  • No custom team names
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No quality diagnostics

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: PartialSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain random grouping with even distribution. Useful for one-off splits, but no meaningful balancing or optimization.
Features2.4★
Above the bare minimum for a random splitter: 1–20 groups, edit list, re-randomize members, random themed group names, and download workflow. No constraints, balancing attributes, saved rosters, or repeat planning.
Exports3.2★
TXT/PDF downloads are advertised and the page exposes a download workflow, which is better than copy-only tools. No spreadsheet/CSV, share link, or integration-specific export.
Privacy1★
Very poor. The page loads a large ad/consent stack including consentmanager-style storage, Journey/Mediavine/OpenX/Criteo/ID5/Amazon ad scripts, Clarity, Grow, and many localStorage/cookie identifiers. Not a good place for classroom rosters.
Ease4★
The intended workflow is easy: paste names, process list, choose group count, randomize. Extra edit/re-randomize controls are understandable.
Design2.3★
The widget is usable, but it sits inside a generic WordPress/content page with a lot of surrounding article/ad noise.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

A useful basic splitter with fun group names and advertised downloads, dragged down hard by a noisy ad-tech wrapper and lack of real balancing.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

Pros

  • Free
  • No account required
  • Paste names textarea
  • 1–20 group-count control
  • Edit list after generating
  • Re-randomize members
  • Random themed group names
  • Advertised TXT/PDF download workflow

Cons

  • Heavy ad/consent/tracking footprint
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No saved roster/history
  • No CSV/spreadsheet export
  • Generic content-site wrapper
  • Random-only assignment quality

Review notes

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

  • Auto-Optimize mode
  • Specific group-count buttons
  • Random team names
  • Shuffle control
  • Focus Mode
  • Browser-saved names/results

Cons

  • No CSV/spreadsheet export
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No repeat optimization

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: PartialUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain balanced random teams. Fine for one-off game-night groups, with no constraints, attribute balancing, repeat minimization, or quality diagnostics.
Features1.7★
Minimal random splitter features: pasted names, 1–10 teams, demo data, reset, shuffle again, optional captain marker, and copy. No team-size mode, custom team names, constraints, attributes, imports, saved rosters, repeat planning, or diagnostics.
Exports2.2★
Copy results is useful and better than display-only tools. There are no CSV/TXT/JSON exports, print/PDF/image output, share links, Zoom export, or saved result history.
Privacy4.5★
Strong for this category. No ads, analytics, consent banner, account flow, or generation API request was observed; the app appears to run in the browser. No dedicated privacy policy was found, so it is not a perfect score.
Ease4.5★
Very easy: paste names, choose a team count from 1 to 10, and generate. Demo and reset buttons help, though the UI is sparse.
Design2.8★
Plain Bootstrap-era interface. Clear enough to use, but visually basic and not especially polished.
Account friction5★
No signup, login, or payment is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A privacy-friendly casual team splitter with copy and captain markers, but only basic random grouping features.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account is required.

Pros

  • Very easy paste-and-generate workflow
  • Shuffle again, reset, and demo controls
  • Optional team captain markers
  • Copy results button
  • No account required
  • No ads or analytics observed

Cons

  • No team-size mode
  • No custom team names
  • No CSV/spreadsheet or other exports beyond copy
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • Plain dated interface
#127 2.7★

Online Stopwatch Random Group Generator↗ opens tool website

Classroom presentation-style random groups with animated reveals

Free with ads/upsellsstrong-exports
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: YesPresentation: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialCopy results to clipboard: YesExcel export: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: PartialFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: YesRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain random grouping with even-ish sizes. Animations, themes, and stronger randomness presentation do not improve assignment quality.
Features2.3★
Decent for a basic random splitter: number-of-groups mode, max-group-size mode, animated reveals, fullscreen, sound/transition toggles, jump-to-group, copy, CSV export, and premium saved lists. No constraints, balancing, repeats, attributes, or diagnostics.
Exports3.6★
Better than many simple randomizers. It can copy one group or all groups, download a CSV, show all groups together, use fullscreen presentation, and save lists/results through premium workflows. No Zoom-specific export, spreadsheet import, PDF/image export, or serious report format.
Privacy1.7★
Poor. The free site is ad-supported and uses Google Analytics/Google Tag Manager, Google AdSense-style advertising, Publift/Fuse ad scripts, cookies, Cloudflare, and premium account storage. The user-data terms explicitly warn not to upload personal/sensitive data and to treat uploaded data as non-sensitive/non-personal.
Ease3.8★
The basic workflow is not hard: paste names, choose a number, generate. The old animated UI, settings cog, iframed app, and many themed variants make it less clean than the best simple splitters.
Design2.8★
Fun for classrooms, but busy and dated. The site is full of navigation, premium prompts, ads, animations, sounds, and themed variants. That is presentation value, not a clean modern group-generator UI.
Account friction4.8★
Generating groups does not require an account. Premium is only needed for saved lists, ad-free use, and account convenience features.
Review notes

Summary

Good classroom showmanship around a basic random splitter. The exports and fullscreen presentation are useful, but the tool has no real grouping intelligence and the ad/tracking/privacy story is weak.

Pricing: Free with ads. Premium removes ads and adds account features such as saved lists/My Page; the premium page advertises one-off access options and group accounts, with cookies required for premium login.

Pros

  • Many fun classroom themes
  • No account required for basic use
  • Number-of-groups and max-group-size modes
  • Fullscreen animated reveal mode
  • Copy results to clipboard
  • CSV download
  • Premium saved lists for repeated rosters

Cons

  • Random-only grouping
  • No constraints, balancing, or repeat optimization
  • Ad-heavy/tracking-heavy site
  • Dated busy UI
  • Saved-list workflow requires premium
  • User-data terms warn against personal/sensitive data
  • No Zoom-specific export

Quality2.1★
Balanced random distribution only.
Features2.2★
Basic team count, optional team size, balanced/uneven mode, copy output, and recent-history log. No constraints, attributes, repeats, imports, or diagnostics.
Exports2.3★
Copy List is available. There is no CSV, PDF/image, share link, or specialized export.
Privacy2.9★
The page says everything runs locally. Google Tag Manager/Analytics and Google fonts loaded, so it is not fully tracker-free.
Ease4★
Simple pasted-name workflow. Users need to notice that target team size overrides number of teams.
Design3.3★
Clear and compact with useful summary stats and recent-draw history.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A good lightweight randomizer thanks to copy output and history, but it remains basic random splitting.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Multiline or comma-separated input
  • Balanced or uneven mode
  • Optional target team size
  • Copy list
  • Recent draw history

Cons

  • No CSV/PDF/share export
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No saved roster manager
  • Target-size override can surprise users

Quick local random team splits with copy-only output

Browser: YesNo account: YesCopy results to clipboard: YesGoogle Sheets import: PartialPaste names: YesCSV import: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: PartialHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.1★
Plain balanced random teams. Good enough for a one-off split, with no constraints, balancing dimensions, repeat minimization, or quality diagnostics.
Features1.8★
Basic random splitting only: pasted names, team count from 2 to 10, shuffle toggle, regenerate, copy results, and browser-side processing. No group-size mode, custom names, constraints, attributes, imports, saved rosters, repeat planning, or diagnostics.
Exports2★
Copy Results is useful, but there are no CSV/spreadsheet, TXT, JSON, PDF/image, Zoom, print, or result-share exports. Social buttons share the page, not the generated teams.
Privacy2★
Mixed. The tool claims names stay in the browser and live testing did not show names being sent to a generation API. The page still uses Google Analytics, Google AdSense, Funding Choices, and a consent dialog saying data may be shared with 210 partners including precise geolocation processing.
Ease4.4★
Very easy: paste names, choose team count, optionally toggle shuffle, and generate. The 2–10 team limit and number-of-teams-only setup keep it simple but limited.
Design3.5★
Clean, modern, and more polished than many throwaway randomizer pages. The surrounding WordPress/article layout and ad infrastructure add some noise.
Account friction5★
No signup, login, or payment is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A pleasant copy-only random splitter. Fine for quick one-off teams, but feature depth and output options are thin, and the page has a heavy ad/consent setup.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account is required.

Pros

  • Very easy paste-and-generate workflow
  • Runs grouping in the browser
  • No account required
  • Regenerate and copy results buttons
  • Clean visual team cards
  • Generated balanced group sizes in live testing

Cons

  • No group-size mode
  • Limited to 2–10 teams
  • No custom team names
  • No CSV/spreadsheet or other real exports
  • No constraints, attributes, or repeat avoidance
  • Ad/consent stack says data may be shared with 210 partners
#130 2.7★

Super Teacher Tools Classroom Seating / Groups↗ opens tool website

Teachers who want saved class rosters plus simple random classroom groups

Free, no account
Browser: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialPaste names: YesSaved class roster import: YesSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: NoPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.2★
Good enough random classroom splitting, including uneven remainder handling. It does not optimize group quality or avoid repeated pairings.
Features2.6★
Better than a one-shot splitter because it saves class rosters and supports both group-size and group-count generation plus remainder distribution. It does not support constraints, attribute balancing, repeat minimization, multiple sessions, pinned placements, or solver diagnostics.
Exports2★
Results are shown on a web page as Group #1, Group #2, etc. There is no obvious CSV, Excel, copy-to-clipboard, PDF/image, Zoom breakout, or structured export workflow.
Privacy1.1★
Weak for student-roster entry. Testing showed Google advertising/analytics/consent infrastructure, DoubleClick, AddThis on the landing flow, and a Funding Choices consent dialog mentioning data sharing with 210 partners. Rosters are stored server-side in an account-like classroom group.
Ease2.4★
After setup, generating groups is straightforward: choose students per group or number of groups and click Create Groups. Getting there requires account creation, password login, old-style roster forms, and sometimes hidden/awkward bulk-upload controls.
Design1.8★
Very dated classroom-web layout with image-button navigation and ad blocks. It works, but it feels much older and less polished than modern tools.
Account friction1.4★
The demo can be viewed freely, but real class roster use requires creating a named group account with email, display name, and password, then logging in.
Review notes

Summary

A functional saved-roster classroom randomizer with useful group-size/group-count controls, held back by account friction, a dated interface, ad/consent load, and no optimization beyond random grouping.

Pricing: Free. The product page says “100% Free forever,” but actual custom class use requires creating a Super Teacher Tools / Instant Classroom group account.

Pros

  • Free to use
  • Saved class rosters up to 100 students
  • Can create groups by students per group or by number of groups
  • Remainder distribution option for uneven groups
  • Adjacent seating-chart and random-name tools for the same roster
  • Working demo is available

Cons

  • Requires account-style setup for real class rosters
  • Very dated interface
  • No constraints, balancing, or repeat avoidance
  • No clean export/copy workflow for generated groups
  • Ad and consent stack is heavy for student roster data
  • Roster setup is clunky despite bulk/import support

Quality2★
Random balanced team sizes only. No skill/attribute balancing, constraints, repeat minimization, or diagnostics.
Features1.8★
Basic random splitting with both number-of-teams and people-per-team modes. No team naming, imports, saved rosters, constraints, attributes, repeat planning, or diagnostics.
Exports0.4★
Weak. Results are shown on screen only; no copy, CSV, Excel, PDF/image, share link, Zoom, or saved-result output was visible.
Privacy2.6★
Mixed. The tool appears to run in the browser and the site has a no-referrer meta tag and homepage claims browser-side/privacy-oriented tools. However, no working privacy page was found for this tool, and the page loads Google AdSense scripts.

Design2.8★

Account friction5★

Review notes

Summary

Functional and tiny, with a useful people-per-team mode, but it has almost no output or advanced grouping features.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account is required.

Pros

  • Generated teams successfully in live testing
  • One-name-per-line paste input
  • Supports number-of-teams mode
  • Supports people-per-team mode
  • No account required
  • Dark/cyberpunk theme toggles

Cons

  • No copy/export/share controls for generated teams
  • No custom team names
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No saved rosters or result history
  • Loads Google AdSense and no working privacy page was found

Summary

A pleasant basic classroom splitter with reveal mode and local saved names, but output options are weak.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

  • By group count or group size
  • Reveal mode
  • Shuffle again
  • Names saved locally in browser
  • Light analytics footprint

Cons

  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance

Pros

  • Shuffle again

Cons

#137 2.6★

Euan Riggans Team Builder↗ opens tool website

Tiny local random team splitting with save/load for the roster

Free, no accountprivacy-friendlybasic-randomizer
Browser: YesNo account: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialPaste names: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Basic random balanced team sizes by round-robin dealing after shuffle. No skill/attribute balancing, constraints, repeat avoidance, or diagnostics.
Features1.6★
Only core random splitting plus browser-local save/load. No team-size mode, team names, imports, constraints, attributes, repeat planning, or advanced options.
Exports0.4★
Results are shown on screen only. No copy, CSV, Excel, PDF/image, share link, Zoom, or download/export control was visible.
Privacy4★
Good for the category. The tool appears to run client-side, stores saved inputs in localStorage, and no analytics/ad scripts were found in the page HTML. It does load Tailwind from unpkg and Cloudflare Rocket Loader, and no dedicated privacy policy was found.
Ease4★
Very simple: paste names, enter a team count, click Generate. Save/Load is also straightforward, though there is little guidance or validation polish.
Design2.6★
Plain Tailwind-style page with a small centered form and basic team cards. Functional but sparse.
Account friction5★
No signup, login, payment, or verification was needed.
Review notes

Summary

A tiny, privacy-friendly basic splitter with local save/load, but almost no output or advanced features.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account is required.

Pros

  • Generated teams successfully in live testing
  • Simple one-name-per-line paste input
  • Balanced team sizes
  • Browser-local Save and Load buttons
  • No account required
  • No analytics or ads observed in page HTML

Cons

  • No copy/export/share controls
  • No team-size mode
  • No custom team names
  • No constraints or attribute/skill balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • No result diagnostics or history
#138 2.6★

Fotify Speed Dating Event Planner↗ opens tool website

Quick speed-dating event timing plans

Free, no accountmulti-round
Multi-round: YesBrowser: YesNo account: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: YesNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality1.9★
It calculates a plausible event timeline, not actual participant assignments. For group generation, that is a serious limitation.
Features2.2★
It handles attendee count, timing, classic/hybrid format, and minute-by-minute event planning. It does not generate named pairs, tables, constraints, or repeat-aware assignments.
Exports2★
The output is readable on-page, but no strong CSV/PDF/export workflow was observed for the free planner.
Privacy3★
The free planner can be used without participant names. Cookie/analytics and the paid hosted app are separate considerations.
Ease4.3★
The planner is extremely easy: attendee count, format, start time, round length, buffer, generate.
Design4★
Clean modern page with understandable generated schedule blocks and explanatory copy.
Account friction5★
No signup is needed for the planner.
Review notes

Summary

Useful as a free timing planner for speed-dating/networking events, but weak as a group generator because it does not output named pairings.

Pricing: The planner page is free and requires no account. Fotify advertises Match & Connect at $19.99 per event for the matchmaking app layer.

Pros

  • Free planner with no account
  • Fast event-timeline generation
  • Good for speed-dating and speed-networking timing
  • Clean interface
  • Does not require participant personal data

Cons

  • Does not assign named participants
  • No roster import
  • No constraints or repeat optimization
  • No strong export observed
  • Actual matchmaking layer is a paid Fotify product

Games4esl Random Group Generator↗ opens tool website

Teachers who want quick random classroom groups and a printable PDF

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain random classroom grouping. Fine for one-off teams, not for fairness beyond group sizes.
Features1.7★
Group-size mode, 2–10 size choices, and uneven-leftover handling are useful basics. No constraints, balancing, saved lists, or repeat planning.
Exports3.4★
Better than most simple splitters because it has a PDF download. No CSV/spreadsheet export or share link.
Privacy1★
Poor. The page loads Google ads/AdSense, Google Analytics, Ezoic/gatekeeper consent scripts, and Cloudflare analytics, and the policy names Google, Amazon, and Ezoic advertising partners.
Ease4★
Easy classroom flow: paste names, choose group size, generate. The cookie/consent overlay gets in the way.
Design3.1★
Readable and teacher-focused, with less chaos than many old randomizer pages. Embedded in a busy content site.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

A good teacher-friendly basic splitter with PDF output. The ad/consent stack is the big downside.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

Pros

  • Free
  • No account required
  • Teacher-focused workflow
  • Paste names textarea
  • Group size from 2 to 10
  • PDF download
  • Uneven leftover handling described

Cons

  • Ad/consent stack is heavy
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No group-count mode visible
  • No CSV/spreadsheet export
  • No saved roster/history
  • No repeat minimization
#140 2.6★

RakkoTools Random Group Generator↗ opens tool website

Basic random groups with CSV export

Free with ads/upsellsstrong-exports
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialPaste names: YesTeam leader / representative assignment: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: YesUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.1★
Mostly random-only, but representative selection adds a tiny bit of assignment usefulness. There is no real balancing, constraints, repeat reduction, or quality explanation.
Features2.4★
Better than the weakest randomizers: number-of-teams mode, members-per-team mode, representative selection, and CSV export. No constraints, balancing attributes, repeat avoidance, or diagnostics.
Exports4★
CSV export is real and worked in testing. No spreadsheet import, Zoom export, shareable result link, or richer result workflow.
Privacy1★
Bad privacy setup. The consent banner mentions personal data sharing with 210 partners, precise geolocation, ad personalization, and device storage. The privacy policy is generic and includes marketing/newsletter use.
Ease4.3★
The form is simple, but the page is cluttered and consent/ad friction prevents a clean 5/5.
Design2.2★
Cluttered Rakko network utility page, but the actual tool area is usable.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

A better-than-average basic randomizer because it has CSV export, two split modes, and representatives. Not a real group-planning tool, and the privacy/ad-tech setup is bad.

Pricing: Free, ad-supported web tool. No account or payment is needed for the basic generator; pricing is not the issue, the weak privacy/ad-tech environment is.

Pros

  • CSV export
  • Can divide by teams or members per team
  • Can select representatives
  • No account required
  • Paste names workflow

Cons

  • Cluttered page
  • Bad privacy/ad-consent setup
  • No advanced rules
  • No balancing or constraints
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No shareable result link
