Quality2★
Plain random balanced groups. There is no result-quality logic beyond even distribution.
Features1.9★
Only the essentials: pasted names, number-of-groups mode, live stats, regenerate, and copy all. No group-size mode, constraints, imports, saved lists, repeat handling, or diagnostics.
Exports2★
Copy-all output is useful. There is no CSV, TXT, PDF/image, share link, or spreadsheet workflow.
Privacy3.6★
No account is required and the tested page did not show a large ad/consent stack. It is a hosted web tool, so it is not equivalent to an offline/local app.
Ease4.4★
The workflow is obvious and fast: paste names, set group count, generate. Keyboard shortcut support helps.
Design3.8★
Distinct, compact cyber-style interface with clear statistics and result cards.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A clean minimal splitter with copy output, but feature depth and exports are thin.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Fast pasted-name workflow
  • Copy all results
  • Live member/group statistics
  • Keyboard shortcut

Cons

  • No group-size mode
  • No CSV/PDF/export files
  • No constraints or balancing attributes
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No saved rosters
#142 2.6★

SpinTheWheel.io Team Draw Generator↗ opens tool website

Randomly pairing teams from two pots, not forming teams from people

Free with ads/upsells
Browser: YesNo account: YesPresentation: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialEditable/random team names: YesPaste names: PartialSaved result history: PartialShareable result links: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: PartialRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: YesWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality1.7★
Fair random matchup selection from two pots, but no optimization or balancing beyond equal random chance. It does not solve the harder problem of forming balanced groups from participants.
Features2.2★
Strong for its narrow purpose: two separate team pots, individual or all-at-once spins, selected-team removal, editable entries, result tab, sorting/clearing results, save/share, and design customization. Missing actual participant grouping, team-size/team-count generation, imports/exports, constraints, attributes, repeat planning, and diagnostics.
Exports1.8★
Results are recorded in a results tab and can be sorted or cleared. No dedicated copy, CSV, spreadsheet, PDF/image, Zoom, or structured export was visible in live testing.
Privacy1.3★
Weak. The consent dialog described 1728 partners, cookies/device identifiers, personal data, personalized advertising, content measurement, audience insights, precise geolocation, and device scanning. The site also loads Google Tag Manager/Analytics and advertising scripts.
Ease3.1★
The sample wheels make it immediately usable, and the all-spin button worked. The cookie-consent wall, icon-heavy controls, German-only reviewed page, and two-wheel mental model make it less straightforward for ordinary team splitting.
Design3.6★
Polished visual wheel experience with configurable design/theme controls, full-screen-style UI controls, and animated draw behavior. Ads and dense icon buttons add clutter.
Account friction5★
No signup or login was needed for the tested spin/result flow.
Review notes

Summary

Useful for transparent tournament-style team draws, but it is a matchup picker rather than a full group/team generator.

Pricing: Free ad-supported web tool. No account was required to spin and record a matchup; saving custom wheels may require account/login behavior on the wider site.

Pros

  • Two-pot matchup draw works in live testing
  • Selected teams are removed after a draw
  • Results tab records prior draws
  • Can spin wheels individually or together
  • Editable team names/wheel entries
  • Save/share/design controls
  • No account required for basic spinning

Cons

  • Not a participant-to-team group generator
  • No team-size or number-of-teams splitting mode
  • No constraints, attributes, or balancing solver
  • No repeat minimization or multi-round schedule planning
  • No structured export visible
  • Very broad ad/tracking consent with many partners

A usable classroom splitter with export, but limited features and poor consent/privacy posture.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Add students individually
  • Bulk Add and Import controls
  • By count or by size
  • Balanced classroom result cards
  • Export control

Cons

  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No diagnostics
  • No verified CSV/PDF output
  • Heavy Google ad/consent surface

TeachQuill Classroom Group Generator↗ opens tool website

AI-assisted classroom grouping drafts from natural-language constraints

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: PartialAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: NoPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: YesResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: PartialSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: PartialSoft prefer-together: PartialSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.7★
Could be useful for drafts when a teacher writes good instructions, but the actual grouping behavior was not verifiable without an account and should not be credited like a proven constraint solver.
Features2.8★
Natural-language constraints and guided fields are useful, but the group-generator-specific controls are mostly prompt-based and output was not verifiable without signing in.
Exports1.8★
No useful output/export surface was verified because generation led to login. The page promises professional output, but not concrete CSV/copy/share controls in the free visible workflow.
Privacy2.3★
The policy is broad and generic, including account data, user content, cookies, automatic collection, third-party providers, marketing contact, and tracking that may count as sale/sharing under California law. Not ideal for student rosters.
Ease3★
The prompt and guided form are easy to understand, but the workflow stops at account creation before showing generated groups.
Design4.1★
Polished modern educator-platform page with good examples and a clean form.
Account friction1.4★
Generation required sign-in or account creation in the tested workflow.
Review notes

Summary

Interesting as an AI grouping draft tool, but account friction and unverified output keep it well below purpose-built group generators. Useful if you already want TeachQuill’s broader AI platform; not a good no-account group maker.

Pricing: Account required to generate in the tested workflow. Pricing is part of TeachQuill’s broader AI educator platform; the page pushes “Get started free” and login rather than a no-account group-generator workflow.

Pros

  • Clean prompt-based interface
  • Guided form for class size, names, timing, and constraints
  • Can express unusual grouping instructions in natural language
  • Modern design

Cons

  • Account required to generate in tested workflow
  • Output/export not verifiable without login
  • Prompt-based behavior is harder to audit than a deterministic grouping UI
  • Broad privacy policy and tracking/sale-sharing language
  • Not a multi-round optimizer

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: PartialSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain random grouping with even-ish sizes. Fine for one-off random teams, but no assignment quality beyond random distribution.
Features1.8★
Basic feature depth only: two split modes, even size distribution, random/alphabetical/reverse ordering, sample names, live count, and stats. No leaders, constraints, balancing, repeats, imports, saved rosters, or diagnostics.
Exports2.5★
Usable but limited. It provides copy-to-clipboard and text-file download with a simple generated summary. No CSV/Excel structure, no Zoom export, no share link, no PDF/image output, and no saved history.
Privacy4★
Better than most free randomizer pages. The policy says most tools process locally in the browser and input data is not retained, and the tested grouping code is client-side. There is automatic device/usage data, cookies/analytics language, and Cloudflare Insights.
Ease4.7★
Very easy. Paste names, choose number of groups or people per group, and generate. The controls are obvious and there is a sample-name button.
Design3.8★
Clean, readable, and much less cluttered than ad-heavy randomizer sites. It feels like a generic toolkit page rather than a dedicated group-planning product.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

A clean and privacy-friendly basic splitter. Better than many junk randomizers as a utility page, but not a serious group generator.

Pricing: Free. No account or payment required.

Pros

  • Very easy to use
  • No account required
  • Client-side random grouping
  • Number-of-groups and people-per-group modes
  • Clean interface
  • Copy all groups
  • Download as text file

Cons

  • Random-only grouping
  • No balancing, constraints, or repeat optimization
  • No leaders or representative assignment
  • No CSV/Excel/Zoom export
  • No saved rosters or result history
  • Generic toolkit page, not a dedicated planning workflow

Summary

A usable basic splitter with history, undermined by limited features and tracking load.

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Pasted names work
  • History panel
  • Statistics panel
  • Animation/sound settings
  • Readable result cards

Cons

  • No verified export
  • No constraints
  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • Heavy ad/analytics stack

Summary

Pricing: Free. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Pasted names work
  • Clear Fisher-Yates explanation
  • Balanced group sizes
  • Embeddable calculator code

Cons

  • No verified export
  • No constraints
  • No attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • Consent/ad overlay can interfere
#149 2.5★

Comment Picker Random Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Basic random teams with stronger copy/print/Excel output

Free with ads/upsellsstrong-exports
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: PartialCopy results to clipboard: YesExcel export: YesImage/PDF export: PartialPaste names: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: YesFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: PartialCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Standard one-off random grouping with even-ish group sizes. Good enough for a quick split, but no balancing, constraints, repeat reduction, or quality diagnostics.
Features2★
Basic group-generation feature depth. It supports arbitrary team count or members-per-team and even distribution, but no attributes, no custom per-team capacities, no constraints, no multi-session planning, no repeat optimization, and no diagnostics.
Exports4★
Strong for a basic splitter: copy, print, and Excel/Numbers export are all useful. It lacks Zoom breakout export, shareable result links, saved result history, and richer schedule-style output.
Privacy1★
Poor. The tool FAQ says entered names/teams are not saved on Comment Picker servers, which helps, but the page uses Ezoic ad services, Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, AdSense/DoubleClick-style ad infrastructure, consent tooling, cookies, pixels, geolocation/device/ad IDs, and targeted advertising partners. Bad environment for student names.
Ease5★
Straightforward basic workflow: paste names, choose number of teams or participants per team, generate. For simple random teams, it is easy.
Design2.6★
The tool itself is understandable, but the surrounding Comment Picker page is a generic ad-supported utility site with consent/ad infrastructure. Usable, not polished.
Account friction4.5★
No account is needed for the random team generator. There is some friction from the ad/consent-heavy free site and Premium upsell context, but it does not block basic use.
Review notes

Summary

A solid basic random splitter with unusually good output/export for this tier, dragged down by heavy ad-tech/privacy issues and no real grouping intelligence.

Pricing: Free, ad-supported web tool. Comment Picker Premium starts at $9.99 and is positioned as ad-free with higher limits/features, but the random team generator FAQ says team generation is free without limits.

Pros

  • Arbitrary number of teams or people per team
  • Very easy basic workflow
  • Copy, print, and Excel/Numbers export
  • No account required for basic team generation
  • FAQ says entered teams are not saved on their servers

Cons

  • No constraints or balancing
  • No multi-session repeat optimization
  • No custom per-team capacities
  • Heavy Ezoic/ad-tech/consent environment
  • Premium upsell/ad-supported page
  • No Zoom export or shareable result links

Quality2★
Plain random assignment only.
Features1.9★
Names, number of teams, text-file import, duplicate removal, reset, fullscreen, and copy buttons. No group-size mode, constraints, balancing, saved rosters, or repeat planning.
Exports2.6★
Copy buttons per team and a client-side output area are useful. No CSV, PDF, image, or result-specific share link.
Privacy1★
Very poor. The page loads Amazon ads, Google/Funding Choices, BuySellAds, Mixpanel, Microsoft Clarity, Google Tag Manager/Analytics, and other ad/tracking scripts despite the client-side tool label.
Ease3.8★
The core form is straightforward, but the tool page has lots of surrounding chrome, ads, related tools, and share/bookmark controls.
Design2.8★
Functional utility-site design. Not awful, but cluttered.
Account friction4.8★
The generator works without an account. Sign-in is only site chrome.
Review notes

Summary

A usable basic splitter with better-than-minimal output controls, but privacy is bad and there is no real group-planning intelligence.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required for generation; sign-in exists for site features.

Pros

  • Free
  • No account required
  • Text area plus file import
  • Duplicate removal option
  • Copy buttons for each team
  • Client-side output label
  • Fullscreen option

Cons

  • Heavy ad and analytics stack
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No group-size mode
  • No saved roster/history
  • No CSV/PDF export
  • Cluttered utility-site layout

#152 2.5★

Random Choice Generator Random Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Very simple browser team splitting with shareable name-list URLs

Free with ads/upsellsbasic-randomizer
Browser: YesNo account: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialPaste names: YesShareable result links: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Basic random balanced teams by count. No balancing by attributes, constraints, repeats, or quality diagnostics.
Features1.6★
Only the basics are present: name textarea, fixed/custom number-of-teams controls, random split, and a reusable/shareable input link. No team-size mode, team names, constraints, attributes, repeat handling, file import, or solver controls.
Exports1.2★
Teams are displayed on the page, and the input list can be shared via URL. No dedicated copy/export/share for the generated team result was visible.
Privacy0.8★
Very weak. The cookie banner cited 315 partners and purposes including precise geolocation/device scanning, personalized ads/content, measurement, and audience research. The privacy policy/Mediavine notice describes broad ad tracking, sharing/selling for interest-based advertising, and partner processing; live network logs showed many ad/identity-sync calls. The share URL also places participant names directly in the page URL, which can leak through analytics/referrers.
Ease3.7★
Fast and simple: paste names, choose a team count, click Go. Prefilled URL parameters worked. The cookie-consent wall and ad-heavy page add friction.
Design2.7★
Plain but understandable WordPress-style page. The tool area is clear, but surrounding generator lists, tip jar content, ads, and cookie UI make it feel cluttered.
Account friction5★
No signup, login, payment, or verification was needed to use the generator.
Review notes

Summary

Functional for quick random teams, but extremely minimal and dragged down by weak outputs plus heavy ad/tracking privacy concerns.

Pricing: Free ad-supported web tool. No account is required.

Pros

  • Generated teams successfully in live testing
  • Simple paste textarea
  • Quick buttons for common team counts
  • Custom team-count input
  • Shareable/reusable URL for the input list
  • No account required

Cons

  • No team-size mode
  • No copy/export for generated team results
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • Participant names in share URLs can leak via analytics/referrers
  • Very heavy ad/tracking consent and partner ecosystem

Summary

A clean, privacy-friendly basic splitter. Useful for a quick random split, but feature depth and output handling are bare.

Pricing: Free open-source web app. No account required.

Pros

  • Free and open source
  • No account required
  • Clean simple UI
  • Dark mode
  • No ad-tech observed
  • Minimal local storage

Cons

  • No output export
  • No copy button
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No saved roster/history
  • No multi-round optimization
  • Only basic random splitting

Exports1★

Summary

A nice private-feeling basic splitter, but the lack of copy/download/export and group-size mode keeps it below stronger basic utilities.

  • Clean no-clutter interface
  • No account required
  • Runs in browser
  • Claims names are never sent to a server
  • Reshuffle button
  • Fun random group names

Cons

  • No copy/download/export
  • No group-size mode
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No saved rosters/history
  • No leaders or roles

Basic random groups with duplicate removal and custom group title fields

Free, no account
Browser: YesNo account: YesEditable/random team names: PartialPaste names: YesSolver tuning controls: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: PartialEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain random grouping with even-size and duplicate cleanup. No deeper assignment quality.
Features2★
Basic: group title, names, group count, balance-evenly, remove duplicates, and group renaming/reshuffle according to the page copy. No constraints, saved rosters, roles, attributes, or repeats.
Exports1.2★
Weak. No clear copy, CSV, print, PDF, share, or spreadsheet export workflow was evident from the page content.
Privacy1.5★
Poor. The site privacy policy describes third-party advertising, Google Analytics, trackers, usage data, IP/click-pattern collection, and data sharing with advertisers/monitoring parties.
Ease4★
The intended workflow is simple: enter names, choose group count, generate. The page can be harder to access because of bot/security checks.
Design3★
Reasonably clear embedded tool, but it sits inside a broader content/word-tool site rather than a focused group-planning product.
Account friction5★
No account requirement is visible for the generator.
Review notes

Summary

A usable basic splitter with a couple of cleanup toggles. Output/export and privacy are weak.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account requirement visible for the generator.

Pros

  • Simple random grouping workflow
  • Group title field
  • Balance-evenly toggle
  • Remove-duplicates toggle
  • No account requirement visible
  • Group renaming/reshuffle described in page copy

Cons

  • No clear copy/download/export
  • No group-size mode verified
  • No constraints or balancing beyond even size
  • No saved rosters/history
  • Ad/analytics-heavy privacy policy
  • Cloudflare/bot check can block access
#159 2.4★

A School Random Pair Generator↗ opens tool website

Very basic classroom random groups with max-size or group-count modes

Free with ads/upsells
Browser: YesNo account: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialPaste names: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.1★
Fine for one-off random grouping with even-ish sizes. It does not improve group quality beyond simple random splitting.
Features2.2★
Slightly above the weakest random splitters because it supports both max group size and number-of-groups modes plus balanced vs maximum-fill distribution. It has no constraints, attributes, history, repeats, or optimization.
Exports1.4★
Results are just plain text groups on the page. There is no obvious CSV, spreadsheet, copy button, PDF/image, share link, or Zoom-friendly export.
Privacy1★
Very poor for student names. The page showed a Google Funding Choices consent dialog saying data may be shared with 210 partners, plus ad/analytics infrastructure including Google ads/analytics and Infolinks.
Ease4.2★
The core workflow is straightforward: paste names, choose group count or max group size, choose balanced or maximum-fill distribution, submit. The page clutter and consent banner are the main friction.
Design1.7★
Dated, text-heavy utility page with ads and a large consent layer. It works, but it is not pleasant or modern.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A usable basic splitter with slightly better settings than the weakest randomizers, dragged down by an ugly dated page, no exports, and very poor privacy/ad-tech surroundings.

Pricing: Free, with ads and donation links. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Supports number-of-groups and max-group-size modes
  • Balanced or maximum-fill distribution modes
  • Simple pasted-name workflow
  • Free to use

Cons

  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No clean export/copy workflow
  • Very dated design
  • Heavy ad-tech and consent partner surface

Review notes

Summary

A decent basic splitter with fullscreen and some copy affordance. Very thin.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

Pros

  • Free
  • No account required
  • Easy to use
  • Fullscreen mode
  • Click group to copy participants
  • Less ad-tech observed than many utility sites

Cons

  • No group-size mode
  • No real export formats
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No saved roster/history
  • No repeat minimization
  • Privacy documentation is weak

Quality2★
Plain random team assignment with even-ish group sizes. Fisher-Yates is fine, but there are no real assignment-quality controls.
Features1.5★
Only pasted names and number of teams. No group-size mode, constraints, balancing, saved rosters, repeats, or diagnostics.
Exports2★
Better than page-only because each team has a copy icon. No CSV, spreadsheet export, print, share link, or Zoom format.
Privacy1.1★
Bad. The site has a large advertising stack, consent tooling, Google/DoubleClick, Amazon ads, and many local-storage ad identifiers.
Ease4.2★
Simple paste-list, choose team count, generate workflow. The page clutter is the main usability drag.
Design2.6★
Functional calculator-site layout, but visually busy and ad-heavy.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

A competent but very basic random splitter. The per-team copy control is useful; the ad-tech footprint is awful.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

Pros

  • Easy paste-and-generate workflow
  • Per-team copy icons
  • Handles up to 1,000 names according to page copy
  • Uses Fisher-Yates according to page copy
  • No account required

Cons

  • No group-size mode
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No CSV/spreadsheet export
  • No saved rosters/history
  • Ad-heavy page
  • Very poor privacy/ad-tech footprint

Review notes

Summary

A workable wheel-style basic tool, but interaction is multi-step and exports/advanced behavior are weak.

Pricing: Free web tool; no signup observed.

Pros

  • Embedded team picker widget
  • Custom team names claimed
  • Unlimited spin/regenerate positioning
  • No account required
  • Mobile-friendly content positioning

Cons

  • Generated result not completed in automated live test
  • No export/copy controls verified
  • No constraints or attributes
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • Analytics/Clarity caveats

University of Edinburgh Group Allocator↗ opens tool website

Bare-bones random groups with CSV download

Free, no accountprivacy-friendlystrong-exportsbasic-randomizer
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCSV export: YesPaste names: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: YesCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Standard random split quality only. It distributes names into groups, but there is no balancing beyond rough group sizes, no constraints, no repeat reduction, and no diagnostics.
Features1.7★
Low feature depth, but not empty: paste input, number-of-groups mode, people-per-group mode, and CSV download. No constraints, balancing, saved rosters, repeat handling, team names, leaders, diagnostics, or editing controls.
Exports3.2★
CSV download is useful and better than copy-only or page-only tools. Otherwise output is just simple on-page tables; no copy button, print view, share link, Zoom export, or richer formats.
Privacy5★
Strong. The page is static, uses browser-side JavaScript, has no visible analytics/ad scripts, no account, and no server submission for names. It does load jQuery and Bootstrap from CDNs, but roster data appears to stay in the browser.
Ease4.5★
Simple enough: paste names, choose people per group or number of groups, click generate. The toggle is a little clunky, but the workflow is obvious.
Design2.1★
Very plain Bootstrap/static-page design. It is readable, but it feels like a quick internal utility rather than a polished product.
Account friction5★
No account or sign-up. Just open the page.
Review notes

Summary

A very basic random splitter, but privacy-friendly, no-account, and CSV-capable enough to rank above the weakest utility pages.

Pricing: Free static web tool. No account, payment, or sign-up.

Pros

  • Very simple workflow
  • Choose group count or people per group
  • CSV download
  • No account required
  • No visible ads or analytics
  • Runs client-side

Cons

  • Extremely basic
  • Dated/plain UI
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No repeat optimization
  • No copy/share/print controls
  • No saved rosters or result history

Review notes

Summary

Good presentation-wheel wrapper for team picking, but weak as a direct team generator and not privacy-light.

Pricing: Free to start. Sign-in is promoted for saved rosters/wheel sets and other site features.

Pros

  • Polished wheel presentation
  • Fullscreen and party-mode style positioning
  • Saved roster/spin-history concepts
  • Color/theme controls
  • Useful for public/random reveal moments

Cons

  • Not a direct one-click team splitter in tested flow
  • Requires repeated spins/removals to form teams
  • Sign-in promoted for saved rosters
  • Analytics and Clarity present
  • No constraints, repeat planning, or diagnostics

A quick no-account two-or-more group split on Effie’s site

Browser: YesNo account: YesPaste names: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain random grouping. Fine for one-off teams; no quality controls beyond splitting people into groups.
Features1.2★
Almost none for group generation: only pasted names and number of groups. No group-size mode, constraints, balancing, imports, exports, or diagnostics.
Exports1★
Page display only, plus an “Edit in Effie” link into the broader app. No copy, CSV, print, share, or Zoom-friendly output.
Privacy2.8★
No account is needed for the tool, but the page loads Google Tag Manager and the Effie privacy policy is broad because it covers the full app and server-stored encrypted content.
Ease4.6★
Very easy: paste names, choose number of groups, generate. Hard to make this simpler.
Design3.8★
Cleaner and more modern than most utility pages, though it is clearly a funnel into the broader Effie app.
Account friction5★
The generator works without an account.
Review notes

Summary

Clean and easy, but extremely shallow. It is a plain random splitter with almost no output workflow.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required for the generator; the surrounding Effie app has its own product/download flow.

Pros

  • Very easy paste-and-generate flow
  • Clean UI
  • No account required
  • Works in browser
  • Free

Cons

  • Only number-of-groups mode
  • No copy/download/export
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No saved roster/history
  • No diagnostics
  • Google Tag Manager on the page
#169 2.3★

Flippity Random Name Picker↗ opens tool website

Spreadsheet-backed classroom grouping and quick random teams

Free with ads/upsells
Browser: YesNo account: PartialPresentation: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialGoogle Sheets import: YesPaste names: YesResult comments: PartialSaved class roster import: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: YesHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: PartialPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: YesRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: PartialResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.2★
Basic random grouping with hard preset limits. It can make simple classroom groups, but inability to choose arbitrary team counts/group sizes plus no balancing or constraints keeps quality low.
Features1.8★
Low for group generation. It directly offers groups of 2–5 and teams of 2–5, which is better than a pure picker workaround, but the limits are glaring: no arbitrary number of teams, no arbitrary group size beyond the preset tabs, no custom capacities, no constraints, no balancing, no multi-session planning, and no repeat optimization.
Exports2.5★
Useful classroom display modes and bookmark/share links, but weak export surface. It does not provide proper CSV group assignment export, Zoom export, result diagnostics, or schedule-style output.
Privacy3.2★
Better than many ad-heavy randomizers because Flippity says it has no user accounts, does not store tool data on Flippity servers, and reads public Google Sheet data in the browser. It uses Google Analytics and Google AdSense, and Google-Sheets-based rosters are effectively published/accessible to anyone with the long link. Not ideal for sensitive student names.
Ease3.5★
The quick-entry path is easy enough: paste or type names, generate, then use the Groups/Teams tabs. The Google Sheets template path is powerful for teachers but clunky compared with a purpose-built group generator.
Design2.5★
Functional but dated. The tabs for spinner, lineup, groups, teams, chart, timer, and fullscreen are useful, but the visual design feels old-school classroom-web rather than polished.
Account friction3.5★
No Flippity account is needed and quick-entry works directly. The spreadsheet workflow requires a Google account and template copying, which is friction if you just want groups now.
Review notes

Summary

A useful classroom randomizer, but feature depth is thin for group generation because group/team sizes are limited to preset tabs.

Pricing: Free, ad-supported Flippity tool. Quick-entry use is free without a Flippity account; the spreadsheet-template workflow requires a Google account and Google Sheets setup.

Pros

  • Direct groups/teams tabs
  • Google Sheets roster workflow
  • Quick-entry option without a Flippity account
  • Fullscreen classroom display
  • No Flippity server storage of activity data

Cons

  • Google Sheets template setup is clunky
  • Limited to preset group/team sizes
  • No arbitrary number of teams or group size
  • Dated interface
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No repeat optimization
  • AdSense and Google Analytics are present
  • Published spreadsheet links are not truly private

Quality2★
Balanced random distribution only. Good for one-off teams, not for optimized assignments.
Features1.9★
Basic random grouping with group-count and group-size modes. No constraints, saved rosters, repeated rounds, attributes, or diagnostics.
Exports1.7★
Copy All Groups exists. There is no CSV, PDF, image, share link, or Zoom-friendly export.
Privacy0.7★
Poor for participant names: the tested page loaded Google ads/consent, Microsoft Clarity, Cloudflare analytics, Google Tag Manager, and a consent notice referencing 210 partners and precise geolocation.
Ease4.1★
The tool is easy: paste names, choose group count or size, and generate.
Design3.1★
Readable and simple enough, though embedded in a large content/SEO site.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A functional basic splitter, dragged down by limited outputs and a very heavy ad/consent stack.

Pricing: Free. No account is required. The site shows an advertising consent flow.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Group-count and group-size modes
  • Copy all groups
  • Simple pasted-name workflow

Cons

  • No CSV/PDF/export files
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No saved roster workflow
  • Very heavy advertising/consent footprint
#171 2.3★

OpenEduCat AI Group Generator↗ opens tool website

Institutions already evaluating OpenEduCat, not quick public grouping

Free, no accountconstraintsai-assisted
Multi-round: PartialRepeat encounter limits: PartialUnique-contact: PartialHard keep-apart: PartialAttribute balance: PartialBrowser: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialResult quality diagnostics: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: PartialAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: PartialGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: PartialNo account: NoPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: PartialPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: PartialResult comments: YesResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: PartialSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: PartialSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: PartialUnique-contact: PartialVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality3★
If the advertised constraints, history, and balancing work as described, result quality could be better than random splitters. The public page does not provide enough access to verify that quality.
Features3★
The advertised feature set is unusually deep: roster import, criteria-based grouping, constraints, no-repeat history, roles, seating arrangement, and rationale. Because it is gated and not directly testable as a standalone tool, these capabilities should be treated cautiously.
Exports2.8★
The page claims printable role cards, digital assignments, seating views, and rationale export, but it does not expose a public result or export workflow.
Privacy2.2★
OpenEduCat has a formal privacy policy and institutional cloud/on-premise positioning, but this is an account-based platform for student data. The public page loaded analytics/chat/product-tracking infrastructure including Google Tag Manager/Analytics/DoubleClick, PostHog, Cloudflare Insights, and Tawk.
Ease1.3★
There is no public generator workflow on the page. A teacher looking for immediate groups has to enter a demo/trial/account path first.
Design3.8★
The marketing page is polished and readable, with clear explanations of the claimed workflow.
Account friction1★
No public generator is available without entering the OpenEduCat demo/trial/account flow.
Review notes

Summary

Potentially powerful on paper, but not independently usable from the public page. The rating stays conservative because the generator is gated behind OpenEduCat access and the claimed AI grouping workflow is not directly verifiable as a standalone tool.

Pricing: Requires OpenEduCat access/trial. The site advertises a 15-day free trial and modular pricing from $69/year for modules, with cloud or on-premise deployment depending on the plan.

Pros

  • Claims constraint-aware grouping
  • Claims attribute/skill/interest grouping modes
  • Claims no-repeat grouping history
  • Claims cooperative role cards and rationale
  • Institutional platform with cloud/on-premise positioning
  • Polished explanatory page

Cons

  • No public standalone generator form
  • Requires demo/trial/account path
  • Advertised AI workflow is not directly verifiable from the page
  • Likely too heavy for quick one-off grouping
  • Public page loads analytics/chat/product-tracking services

Review notes

A usable but shallow random splitter. It is fine for one quick draw, but weak on output options, rules, privacy, and anything beyond even team counts.

Pricing: Free, ad-supported web tool. No account is needed for the team generator.

Pros

  • Textarea accepts one-per-line or comma-separated names
  • Simple team-count controls
  • Balanced team sizes
  • No account required

Cons

  • No team-size mode
  • No copy/download/share controls visible
  • No custom team names
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or diagnostics
  • Google ad/consent privacy caveats

Only a number-of-teams random split. No group-size mode, constraints, balancing, leaders, imports, saved lists, or diagnostics.
Exports1★
Results are displayed on the page only. No copy button, CSV, PDF, print layout, or share link.
Privacy3.8★
Better than most generic utilities: no account, no observed ad stack, and only Plausible plus first-party scripts on the tool page. The site privacy page describes analytics cookies and Google Analytics language.
Ease4.8★

Design2.7★
Plain blog-page design. Not polished, but readable and much less annoying than ad-heavy randomizer pages.
Account friction5★
No signup, no login, no install.
Review notes

Summary

A clean, low-noise basic splitter. Better privacy and less clutter than many randomizer pages, but the grouping feature set is almost nonexistent.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

Pros

  • Free
  • No account required
  • Very easy to use
  • Low page clutter
  • No heavy ad-tech observed
  • Works for quick one-off random teams

Cons

  • Number-of-teams mode only
  • No copy/export/download controls
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No saved roster or history
  • No diagnostics
  • Very thin compared with real group planners

GroupGenerator.top↗ opens tool website

Browser: YesNo account: YesResult quality diagnostics: PartialImage/PDF export: PartialPaste names: YesSolver tuning controls: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Random group generation worked like a normal basic splitter. The AI smart grouper is the weak point: it generated groups but ignored the instructions given to it.
Features1.5★
Has basic random matching plus an interesting AI smart-grouping concept and fullscreen/export controls. The AI instructions are not reliable enough to count as real constraint support.
Exports1.5★
Fullscreen/export controls exist, but the output surface is light compared with tools that provide CSV, copy, share links, or structured exports.
Privacy1.2★
Poor. It appears to include an AI grouping workflow, loads Google Tag Manager, and gives no clear roster-specific data handling explanation.
Ease4.7★
The UI is simple and the random matching mode is easy to use. The AI mode is less clear because it can ignore the grouping instructions.
Design3.2★
Modern and clean visually.
Account friction5★
No account required was visible.
Review notes

Summary

The premise is interesting and the random mode works, but the AI matching is not trustworthy enough to rate it like a real constraint-aware generator.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account requirement was visible.

Pros

  • Modern-looking UI
  • No visible account requirement
  • Random matching mode works
  • Fullscreen and export controls
  • Interesting natural-language grouping idea

Cons

  • AI matching ignored instructions
  • No clear privacy policy for AI processing
  • No verified real constraints or balancing
  • Google Tag Manager present
  • Output/export surface is light

Group count, label, reroll, copy, and reset are useful basic features.
Exports2★
Copy Results is available; no file export was verified.
Privacy0.8★
Raptive/AdThrive, consent manager, Clarity, Google analytics/tag manager, Slickstream, and related hosts were observed.
Ease2.4★
The embedded form is understandable, but consent/ad overlays and page clutter interfere.
Design1.8★
The generator itself is small and simple; the surrounding article page dominates the experience.
Account friction5★
No signup or login is needed.
Review notes

Summary

A working embedded classroom splitter buried in a cluttered, ad-heavy article page.

Pricing: Free, ad-supported web tool. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Pasted student names
  • Group count control
  • Custom group label
  • Reroll
  • Copy results

Cons

  • Ad/consent overlays
  • Long article clutter
  • Flaky automated click behavior
  • No constraints
  • No attribute balancing
  • No file export verified
#180 2.2★

MiniWebTool Random Group Generator↗ opens tool website

Fast basic web groups with arbitrary group size or team count

Free with ads/upsells
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialResult quality diagnostics: PartialAPI/programmatic access: YesCopy results to clipboard: YesDeterministic seed / reproducibility: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: YesAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: PartialEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Standard one-off random grouping with even-ish group sizes. Consistent with the other plain random splitters: useful for a quick split, but no balancing, constraints, repeat reduction, or quality diagnostics.
Features2★
Basic group-generation feature depth. It supports arbitrary members-per-group or number-of-groups modes, balanced size distribution, local saved input, copy buttons, and an API. That is better than preset-only tools, but only earns about a 2 because there are no constraints, attributes, custom per-group capacities, multi-session planning, repeat optimization, or diagnostics.
Exports2★
Copy buttons are useful, but this is a basic output surface. The consumer page does not provide CSV, Excel, Zoom export, share links, printable/PDF output, or persistent result history.
Privacy2★
The tool page says names are stored only in browser local storage, which is good for roster privacy. The surrounding site is much less clean: ads, Google Analytics/Tag Manager, DoubleClick/AdSense-style advertising, Cloudflare analytics, and an anti-adblock popup pushing allowlisting or Premium.
Ease5★
Very easy: paste one name per line, choose group size or number of groups, generate, copy. This deserves the same easy-workflow score as other obvious basic splitters.
Design2.7★
The generated group cards are readable, but the page is a generic ad-supported utility page and the anti-adblock premium popup is intrusive. Not terrible, but clearly worse than the clean basic tools.
Account friction4★
No account is needed for the web tool, which is good. The anti-adblock popup adds real access friction by asking users to allowlist the site or upgrade to Premium before continuing.
Review notes

Summary

A decent simple splitter, but the ad-supported/anti-adblock page environment drags it down. It is only in the basic-random-generator tier because it has no real grouping rules.

Pricing: Free, ad-supported web tool with an anti-adblock/premium upsell popup. Premium is positioned as ad-free and faster; the related developer API starts free with credits and paid tiers.

Pros

  • Supports group size or number of groups
  • Very easy paste-and-generate workflow
  • One-click copy for groups
  • Saves input locally in browser
  • Developer API exists

Cons

  • No constraints or balancing
  • No multi-session repeat optimization
  • No CSV/Excel/Zoom export on consumer page
  • Ad-supported page with tracking/analytics
  • Anti-adblock/premium popup is intrusive
  • Generic utility-site feel
#181 2.2★

Online Stopwatch Random Magic Hat Group Generator↗ opens tool website

Classroom display novelty when animated sorting matters more than features

Browser: YesNo account: YesPresentation: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialPaste names: YesVisual/theme customization: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: YesRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain random grouping. The animation does not improve assignment quality.
Features1.6★
Basic animated random grouping only. Premium can save names, but there are no constraints, balancing, imports, exports, repeats, or diagnostics.
Exports1.3★
Mostly visual/on-screen. Fullscreen helps presentation; there is no serious copy/CSV/export workflow.
Privacy1★
Terrible for student rosters. The page loads a large ad stack with Google ads, Amazon ads, prebid, Funding Choices, and multiple tracking/storage identifiers.
Ease3.2★
The concept is simple, but the generator is embedded in a cluttered, ad-heavy page and animation adds friction if you just need results.
Design2.4★
The magic-hat theme is fun for a classroom screen, but the surrounding page is messy and dated.
Account friction4★
The basic generator is free, but saved names/ad-free convenience pushes toward premium.
Review notes

Summary

Fun classroom theater, weak group-generation product. The ad-tech load is severe.

Pricing: Free with ads. Premium/ad-free and saved-name convenience are promoted by the site.

Pros

  • Fun magic-hat animation
  • Fullscreen option
  • No account needed for basic use
  • Good for projecting a reveal
  • Premium can save names

Cons

  • Very ad-heavy
  • Poor privacy for student names
  • No copy/download/export
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No repeat optimization
  • Animation slows down simple work
#182 2.2★

PickJa Random Group Generator↗ opens tool website

Quick no-account random groups with group-count or members-per-group mode

Free with ads/upsells
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesResult quality diagnostics: PartialMinimum group sizes: PartialPaste names: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Normal random split quality. It balances group sizes evenly, but has no constraints, repeat minimization, seeded reproducibility, or quality diagnostics.
Features2★
Basic splitter feature depth: paste names, choose number of groups or members per group, preview counts, and regenerate. No constraints, balancing attributes, imports, saved rosters, repeats, or diagnostics beyond counts.
Exports1★
Page display only. No copy button, CSV/spreadsheet export, print/PDF/image export, share link, or saved result history.
Privacy1★
Poor. The tool page loads Google ad/analytics infrastructure and shows a consent prompt referencing 210 partners, device storage/access, personalized ads/content measurement, and precise geolocation. The policy also covers account/share data and Google Analytics. Not appropriate for sensitive rosters.
Ease4.4★
Straightforward one-name-per-line paste flow with group-count and members-per-group modes. The consent banner is intrusive, but the core tool is easy once reachable.
Design3.4★
Modern, readable, and cleaner than many randomizer pages. The surrounding navigation, ads/consent layer, and long SEO content keep it from feeling polished.
Account friction5★
The generator works without an account. Sign-in is present for the broader PickJa site but not required for this workflow.
Review notes

Summary

A pleasant basic splitter, but shallow. It gains points for dual mode and readable output, then loses them on missing exports and severe ad-tech privacy posture.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required for the generator; sign-in exists for the broader PickJa site.

Pros

  • Very easy paste-and-generate workflow
  • Supports number-of-groups mode
  • Supports members-per-group mode
  • Readable color-coded group cards
  • No account required

Cons

  • No copy/download/export controls
  • No constraints or balancing attributes
  • No saved roster or result history
  • No repeat optimization
  • Heavy ad-tech consent banner and tracking environment
#183 2.2★

QuizBreaker Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Not recommended; basic space-separated random teams on a QuizBreaker marketing page

Free with ads/upsellsbasic-randomizer
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCopy results to clipboard: YesPaste names: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: YesCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality1.8★
Plain random teams, and the newline input bug can collapse a roster into one item. There are no constraints, balancing dimensions, repeat minimization, or quality diagnostics.
Features1.4★
Very shallow: number-of-teams input, member textarea, random generation, and copy. No working newline roster paste, no team-size mode visible, no custom names, no constraints, attributes, imports, saved rosters, repeat planning, or diagnostics.
Exports2★
Copy is useful. There are no CSV/TXT/JSON exports, spreadsheet output, print/PDF/image output, share links, Zoom export, or saved result history.
Privacy2.2★
Mixed-to-poor. The generator appears browser-side and no generation API request was observed, but the page uses Google Tag Manager/Google Analytics, and the privacy policy discusses cookies, Google AdSense advertising, remarketing, and third-party behavioral tracking.
Ease2.4★
The intended UI is simple, but the documented one-name-per-line workflow failed in live testing because the script splits on spaces. Users can work around it with space-separated names, which is awkward for real rosters.
Design3.5★
Modern and readable, but the actual generator is small and surrounded by a long QuizBreaker signup/product marketing page.
Account friction4.6★
No account is needed for the free generator itself, although the surrounding page heavily promotes QuizBreaker signup.
Review notes

Summary

A weak random splitter. It technically works with space-separated names, but the documented newline workflow failed in live testing and the feature set is very shallow.

Pricing: Free public tool. No account is required for the generator; the surrounding QuizBreaker product promotes signup and paid/product workflows.

Pros

  • No account required for the generator
  • Modern page design
  • Copy results button
  • Works with space-separated names
  • Appears to run generation in the browser

Cons

  • Documented newline input failed in live testing
  • No visible team-size mode despite page copy
  • No CSV/spreadsheet or other exports beyond copy
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance or multi-round planning
  • Surrounded by QuizBreaker product marketing and analytics/remarketing policy

Show all features

Quality2★
Plain random grouping only. Local storage and extension packaging do not improve assignment quality.
Features1.6★
Very basic: one-name-per-line input, number of groups, random generation, clear, and local-storage persistence. No group-size mode, constraints, balancing, imports, exports, repeats, or diagnostics.
Exports1.4★
Weak. The listing mentions on-screen labeled groups only. No copy, CSV, print, share, or spreadsheet export is advertised.
Privacy4.2★
Strong on paper for a simple tool: the Chrome Web Store disclosure says the developer does not collect or use data, and names are saved locally in browser storage. This is a small extension from a non-trader developer, so trust depends on installing extension code.
Ease3.8★
The described workflow is simple, but needing to install a Chrome extension just to split groups is extra friction compared with a web page.
Design3★
Only the Chrome Web Store listing and screenshots were available here. It appears to be a small functional popup, not a polished planning interface.
Account friction3.2★
No account advertised, but extension installation is a real barrier compared with no-install web tools.
Review notes

Summary

A basic random splitter packaged as a Chrome extension. The local-data posture is good, but the grouping feature set is very thin and installation is unnecessary friction for most users.

Pricing: Free Chrome extension. No account advertised, but it requires installing a browser extension.

Pros

  • Simple Chrome extension workflow
  • No account advertised
  • Names stored locally in browser storage
  • Chrome Web Store disclosure says no data collection
  • Clear button for stored names

Cons

  • Requires installing an extension
  • Random-only grouping
  • No group-size mode advertised
  • No copy/download/export advertised
  • No constraints or balancing
  • Tiny unknown/non-trader developer surface

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain random grouping only. Fine for a quick one-off split, but no balancing, constraints, repeat reduction, or diagnostics.
Features1.8★
It has paste names, arbitrary group count, rerun, edit settings, and URL state. No real grouping intelligence.
Exports2.1★
Shareable URL state is useful, but it exposes entered names in the query string and there is no CSV, PDF, print, spreadsheet, or Zoom-friendly export.
Privacy1★
Bad for anything sensitive. The consent banner mentions personal data sharing with 210 partners, precise geolocation, ad personalization, and device storage. The privacy policy also says information may be shared or sold for marketing. Shared links include the entered names directly in the URL.
Ease4.6★
Simple enough, but the Edit Settings flow plus consent/ad clutter keeps it from being a true 5/5.
Design3.2★
Fairly clean and modern compared with ClassTools, though a generic ad-supported utility site.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

Better than ClassTools, but just a basic random splitter with bad privacy. Its search visibility is much stronger than the product deserves.

Pricing: Free, ad-supported web tool. No paid group-generation tier is needed, but the cost is heavy ad/consent friction and a very basic feature set.

Pros

  • Easy to use
  • Cleaner than ClassTools
  • Paste names
  • Arbitrary group count
  • Rerun shuffle
  • Shareable URL state
  • No account required

Cons

  • No advanced rules
  • No real balancing or constraints
  • No useful export formats
  • Bad privacy/ad-consent setup
  • Shared URLs expose entered names
  • Ranks better in search engines than it deserves

Privacy-conscious basic random groups with cryptographic server randomness

Free, no accountbasic-randomizer
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: PartialNo account: YesPresentation: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialCopy results to clipboard: PartialMinimum group sizes: PartialPaste names: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: PartialCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: YesRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: PartialSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Standard one-off random grouping. Cryptographically secure random selection is nice, but it does not improve group assignment quality beyond random even-ish splitting.
Features1.8★
Low feature depth. It supports number-of-groups, people-per-group, no-incomplete-groups options, sorting/display options, saved name lists in cookies, fullscreen, and a puzzle/expert-group mode. No balancing, constraints, multi-session planning, repeats, attributes, or diagnostics.
Exports2.2★
Basic output only: on-page result, copy-style output, print-friendly behavior, fullscreen display, and cookie-saved name lists. No CSV/Excel export, share links, Zoom export, or result history.
Privacy3★
Mixed. The page says SSL is used and input/output data is not stored or shared, and there is a clear GDPR privacy policy. The generator posts names to a server endpoint for random_int() grouping, stores saved name lists in cookies if used, and the broader site privacy policy includes Google Analytics, AdSense, YouTube, VG WORT pixels, and affiliate tracking.
Ease4★
Straightforward once you find the controls: enter names, choose number of groups or people per group, generate. The page has many surrounding random-generator options, so it is less clean than the best simple splitters.
Design2.8★
Functional but dated and busy. It feels like an old calculator/randomizer site, not a focused classroom/grouping product.
Account friction5★
No account or payment required.
Review notes

Summary

A decent basic splitter with unusually explicit randomness documentation and privacy claims, but only basic random grouping and server-side processing.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account or payment required.

Pros

  • Number of groups or people per group
  • No account required
  • Clear randomness documentation
  • Claims not to store/share input or output
  • Can save name lists in cookies
  • Print/fullscreen-style display options

Cons

  • Server-side processing sends names to endpoint
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No multi-session repeat optimization
  • No CSV/Excel/Zoom export
  • Dated busy UI
  • Broader site includes Analytics/AdSense/tracking components
