API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: PartialPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: YesRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: YesWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality1.2★
One-at-a-time random selection only, with no balancing or group assignment logic.
Features1.8★
Wheel input import, weights, shuffle, fullscreen, local wheels, and account save/share. Almost none of these are actual group-generation features.
Exports1★
Share links and saved wheels require an account; no team-result export exists because the tool does not generate full teams.
Privacy1.2★
Loads Google Tag Manager/gtag and a third-party ad/monetization script, and presents a 210-partner consent flow with precise geolocation language.
Ease2.7★
Easy as a wheel, clunky as a team maker because each team assignment is manual.
Design3.8★
Polished wheel UI with good fullscreen and input-management controls.
Account friction3.5★
No login for basic spinning; login is required for saving and sharing.
Review notes

Summary

A good spinner, but a weak team generator because teams must be assembled manually.

Pricing: Free wheel tool. Account is required for saved wheels and share links; local unsaved use works without login.

Pros

  • Polished visual spinner
  • Copy/paste bulk input workflow
  • Weighted entries via input size
  • Shuffle and show/hide inputs
  • Fullscreen mode
  • Local unsaved wheels without login

Cons

  • Not an automatic team splitter
  • Requires manual spin-and-assign workflow
  • Account required for saved/shared wheels
  • No team-result export
  • Heavy consent/ad-tech privacy caveats
  • No constraints, repeat planning, or diagnostics
#190 2.1★

iLeren Groepenmaker↗ opens tool website

Dutch-speaking teachers who need a tiny no-account classroom splitter

Free, no accountprivacy-friendlybasic-randomizer

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain random grouping with basic size handling. No quality controls beyond splitting people.
Features1.8★
Only the two basic split modes. No constraints, attributes, leaders, repeat handling, saved rosters, or diagnostics.
Exports1★
On-page results only. No copy, download, CSV, print, share, or saved history.
Privacy4.7★
Strong for this category. The tool page has no obvious tracking scripts, ads, cookies, or local storage.
Ease4.1★
Easy if you read Dutch: paste names, choose people-per-group or number-of-groups, generate.
Design1.7★
Extremely plain Bootstrap-style utility page. Functional but visually primitive.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

A bare but honest classroom splitter. Very limited, but cleaner and more private than many ad-stuffed randomizer pages.

Pricing: Free. No account required.

Pros

  • Free and no account required
  • No obvious trackers or ads on the tool page
  • Paste names textarea
  • People-per-group or number-of-groups mode
  • Very simple

Cons

  • Dutch-only UI
  • No copy/download/export
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No saved rosters/history
  • Very plain design
  • Random-only results

Pricing: Free ad-supported web tool.

  • Simple participant list + team count form

  • No copy/export/share observed
  • No team-size mode
  • No custom team names

#192 2.1★

Mega Seating Plan Free Random Group Maker↗ opens tool website

Teachers who already use Seating Plan class lists

Free with ads/upsellsbasic-randomizer
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialPaste names: YesSaved class roster import: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Random balanced groups only. Fine for a one-off split, but no constraints, repeat reduction, attribute balancing, or diagnostics.
Features1.8★
Basic but not completely empty: it supports splitting by number of groups or students per group, plus account-gated class-list use. There are no real grouping rules, constraints, repeat controls, or multi-round options in the free tool.
Exports1★
Very weak. Results appear on the page, but no obvious CSV, copy, print, PDF, download, or share workflow was available in the free tool.
Privacy1.8★
Weak for a simple pasted-name tool. The page loads analytics/marketing scripts and sets Google Analytics/Brevo-style cookies, and Seating Plan’s privacy notice explicitly lists session recording, mouse events, form interactions, file/link clicks, and other analytics data.
Ease4.7★
Very easy: paste names, choose number of groups or students per group, and click make groups. The surrounding sign-in/class-list pitch keeps it slightly below the cleanest tools.
Design2.8★
The actual widget is clear enough, but the page around it is cluttered with navigation, sign-in prompts, and long SEO copy.
Account friction4.8★
No account required for pasted names. Sign-in is pushed for class lists and the smarter product, so it is not quite friction-free.
Review notes

Summary

A working classroom random splitter with two basic split modes, but no useful export controls and a surprisingly weak privacy posture for such a small tool.

Pricing: Free basic random group maker. The broader Mega Seating Plan product has paid plan context, but this specific simple group-maker page is free and does not expose paid grouping controls.

Pros

  • Very easy to use
  • Teacher-focused
  • Supports number of groups or students per group
  • Can use saved class lists if signed in

Cons

  • No useful export options
  • Extremely basic free tool
  • Smarter grouping is a separate product feature
  • Weak analytics/session-recording privacy posture

A minimal team split if you tolerate the consent wall

Browser: YesNo account: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialPaste names: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain balanced random groups. No optimization or constraints.
Features1.2★
Pasted names plus number-of-teams generation only in the tested UI. No meaningful group-generation controls beyond that.
Exports0.8★
Generated teams are displayed on the page. No visible copy, CSV, PDF, share, or export workflow appeared in testing.
Privacy0.8★
Bad fit for sensitive rosters: Google ads/consent, Plausible, Supabase/auth endpoints, and hundreds of consent-vendor entries were present.
Ease3.2★
The actual form is simple, but the consent overlay can block interaction until handled.
Design2.7★
The generated teams are clear. The tool is surrounded by utility-site navigation, membership prompts, and review/sign-in areas.
Account friction4.6★
No account was needed to generate teams, but login/signup/membership prompts are prominent around the tool.
Review notes

Summary

It works as a bare random splitter, but the feature set is tiny and the consent/ad surface is bad.

Pricing: Free. The generator itself did not require an account, though the site has sign-in and membership surfaces.

Pros

  • No account required for generation
  • Simple pasted-name workflow
  • Balanced random team output

Cons

  • No export or copy button found
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No repeat avoidance
  • Consent overlay can block the tool
  • Heavy ad/vendor surface
#194 2.1★

My2lbox Random Group Generator↗ opens tool website

Tiny random or level-weighted groups when privacy/ad-tech tradeoffs are acceptable

Free, no accountconstraints
Attribute balance: PartialBrowser: PartialNo account: YesGender/label balancing: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: PartialAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: PartialGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: PartialSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2.5★
Better than a plain random splitter when weighted-by-level mode is used, because it can spread participant levels. A simple heuristic/form tool with no broader constraints or repeat optimization.
Features2.2★
Above the weakest random splitters because it supports random or weighted grouping by participant level. That is basically it: number of groups, participant names, optional levels. No group-size mode, constraints, leaders, imports, exports, repeats, or diagnostics.
Exports1.1★
Very poor. Results are displayed on the page only. No copy button, CSV/text download, print view, share link, saved history, or spreadsheet-friendly output was visible in testing.
Privacy1.4★
Poor. The legal page says data submitted through tools is not tracked, stored, or archived after processing, which is good. The page shows a Google-managed consent dialog mentioning 210 partners, personalized ads/content, audience measurement, device storage/access, and possible precise geolocation, plus multiple Google AdSense/Tag scripts.
Ease2.6★
Not terrible, but clunky. Instead of pasting a normal list, you enter names into separate fields and manually add more participant rows. Weighted mode adds another level-selection step.
Design2★
Old Bootstrap-style page with ads and awkward layout. The cookie/ad consent layer is intrusive and parts of the surrounding page/content are in French even on the English page.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

Interesting because it has level-weighted grouping, but the UX, output surface, and privacy/ad-tech situation are weak.

Pricing: Free. No account required.

Pros

  • Free and no account required
  • Random grouping mode
  • Weighted grouping by participant level
  • Simple generated group display
  • Legal page says tool-submitted data is not retained
  • Useful for quick level-balanced classroom groups

Cons

  • No paste-list textarea; names are entered field by field
  • No copy/download/export controls
  • No group-size mode
  • No constraints or repeat optimization
  • Intrusive 210-partner Google consent/ad-tech layer
  • Dated mixed-language UI

  • No repeat avoidance

Quality2★
Random-only. Leader markers may distribute marked leaders, but there is no evidence of broader assignment quality.
Features2.3★
Leader markers, custom team names, and two split modes are real features. There are no constraints, balancing categories, imports, history, or repeat optimization.
Exports2★
The page describes a Copy All Teams button, but no CSV, PDF, image, or spreadsheet export. Output depth is low.
Privacy1★
Very poor. The page uses a CMP/ad stack with Mediavine/Journey-style scripts, consent storage, cookies, and a policy that explicitly discusses Google ad cookies and third-party ad servers.
Ease2.3★
The visible flow is more fragile than a simple random team generator should be. The number/team-name controls add friction instead of clarity.
Design2.6★
Modern enough visually, but surrounded by a generic tool-site layout, cookie prompt, and lots of related-tool clutter.
Account friction5★
No account required for the generator.
Review notes

Summary

Interesting leader/team-name ideas, but the tool is clunky and the privacy footprint is ugly. Not a strong recommendation.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

Pros

  • Free
  • No account required
  • Paste names textarea
  • Number-of-teams and participants-per-team modes claimed
  • $ leader marker syntax
  • Custom team names
  • Copy-all output claimed

Cons

  • Brittle form behavior
  • Heavy ad-tech and cookie footprint
  • No real constraints or balancing
  • No CSV/PDF/spreadsheet export
  • No saved rosters/history
  • Generic content-farm feel

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain random grouping. The select/shuffle UI does not improve assignment quality.
Features1.5★
Basic grouping only: add members, select/deselect, shuffle, select random member, choose number of groups, generate. No constraints, attributes, imports, exports, saved rosters, or diagnostics.
Exports1★
Page display only. No copy button, CSV, print, image/PDF export, share link, or saved result history.
Privacy2★
No account required, but there is no clear privacy page for the tool and the site loads Google Tag Manager, Microsoft Clarity, reCAPTCHA, Webflow, and other third-party scripts.
Ease2.6★
Adding names one by one and selecting checkboxes is tolerable for tiny lists and bad for classrooms or workshops. Paste support is the missing basic feature.
Design3.4★
Visually cleaner and more playful than most randomizer pages, as expected from a portfolio site.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

A cute but shallow random grouping widget. The one-at-a-time input makes it worse than many uglier but faster splitters.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

Pros

  • Attractive visual widget
  • No account required
  • Can select/deselect participants
  • Shuffle control
  • Select random member option
  • Group count slider

Cons

  • No paste-a-list input
  • Names must be added one at a time
  • No copy/download/export
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No saved roster/history
  • Google Tag Manager, Microsoft Clarity, and reCAPTCHA scripts

Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality1.8★
Plain random assignment, then names are sorted alphabetically inside each team. That is fine for a trivial split, but it adds no assignment-quality value.
Features1.2★
Just number of teams and a textarea. No group-size mode, constraints, attributes, leaders, saved rosters, repeats, or diagnostics.
Exports1★
On-page results only. No copy button, CSV, spreadsheet export, print view, share link, or saved result history.
Privacy2.4★
No account required, but the page loads Google Tag Manager/Analytics and other third-party scripts. There is no strong roster-specific privacy posture on the tool page.
Ease4.2★
Simple enough: paste names, enter number of teams, generate. The surrounding calculator-site navigation is more clutter than the tool needs.
Design2.1★
Old-fashioned calculator-page design. Functional, but not especially pleasant or focused.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

Usable for a quick split, but very basic and output-poor. It belongs near the bottom of the simple random splitter tier.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

Pros

  • Free and no account required
  • Paste names textarea
  • Simple number-of-teams workflow
  • Can handle large pasted lists according to the page

Cons

  • No copy/download/export
  • No group-size mode
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No saved roster/history
  • Dated page design
  • Google analytics/tag scripts present
#201 2★

GoSpinWheel Random Group Generator↗ opens tool website

Live wheel-based team assignment when presentation matters more than balanced rosters

Free with ads/upsellsbasic-randomizer
Browser: YesNo account: YesPresentation: YesSaved participant/team lists: PartialCopy results to clipboard: PartialEditable/random team names: YesImage/PDF export: PartialPaste names: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: PartialCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: YesEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: PartialPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: YesRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: PartialSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: PartialTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: YesWeighted random entries: YesWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality1.2★
Random team assignment only, one spin at a time. There is no guarantee of balanced group sizes, no constraints, no attribute balancing, no repeat minimization, and no quality diagnostics.
Features1.8★
Strong spinner features do not translate into strong group-generation features. It has editable entries, weights, colors, visual themes, result history, local saves, share-card output, and general wheel import/export, but no real team-size controls, constraints, roster import, attribute balancing, repeat planning, or diagnostics.
Exports2.4★
Useful for sharing a single spin result: copy/download a winner image, social share buttons, and a results tab. Weak for actual groups because it does not produce a complete team roster, CSV/spreadsheet export, Zoom breakout output, or printable group assignment report.
Privacy1.6★
Poor for sensitive rosters. The page uses a Google-managed consent/ad stack, AdSense/Funding Choices, Google Analytics, and ads for anonymous/free users. The privacy policy says anonymous visitors can save locally, but public share links and cloud library entries store wheel payloads, and share links are not private storage.
Ease2.4★
Editing team labels and spinning the wheel is straightforward. As a group generator, the workflow is clunky because participants are not pasted into a roster and split at once; the user has to call each person and spin repeatedly, or build a multi-wheel workaround.
Design3.6★
Modern, colorful, and engaging for live presentation. The wheel UI, sounds, themes, and result modal look much better than most old utility pages, but ads, consent UI, sign-in prompts, and generic wheel-site controls add clutter.
Account friction4.2★
No account is needed to spin or edit the wheel. Sign-in is required for public share links, and paid plans are used for cloud library/ad-free workflows.
Review notes

Summary

A good-looking spinner, but a weak group generator. It is better understood as a presentation-oriented random team assignment workaround than as a tool that generates balanced groups.

Pricing: Free ad-supported web wheel. Core spinning and editing work without an account; sign-in or paid plans are tied to public share links, cloud library, and ad-free use.

Pros

  • Polished live wheel presentation
  • Editable team names, colors, and weights
  • No account required for core spinning
  • Result history for individual spins
  • Copy/download/share card for a spin result
  • Local wheel saving and general wheel import/export

Cons

  • Does not generate full group rosters
  • Requires spinning once per person
  • No guaranteed balanced group sizes
  • No constraints, attributes, or repeat avoidance
  • No CSV/spreadsheet or Zoom group export
  • Ad/consent/analytics-heavy privacy posture
#202 2★

RandomCount Team Generator↗ opens tool website

Tiny groups where adding names one at a time is acceptable

Free, no accountbasic-randomizer
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesMinimum group sizes: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain random grouping with even team size. Fine for trivial one-off use only.
Features1★
Only add/remove members and members-per-team random splitting. No paste import, group-count mode, constraints, balancing, saved lists, or diagnostics.
Exports1★
Results display on the page only. No copy, CSV, PDF, share, or print-focused output.
Privacy2.4★
The footer privacy policy is easy to find and it discloses Google Analytics/AdSense, location-derived information, generated-data language, and legal/service-provider sharing. The tool page also set Google Analytics cookies. Lighter than many ad-tech pages, but not ideal for roster data.
Ease2.8★
Adding members one by one is tedious compared with pasting a class list. The rest is simple enough.
Design2.5★
Plain Bootstrap-style utility page. Usable, but not polished.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

A working but weak random splitter. The one-at-a-time name entry alone makes it worse than most basic paste-and-generate tools.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

Pros

  • Free
  • No account required
  • Add/remove member controls
  • Members-per-team mode
  • Generates simple teams
  • Not overloaded with a huge consent stack

Cons

  • No paste-a-roster textarea
  • No copy/export/share controls
  • No group-count mode
  • No constraints or attribute balancing
  • No saved roster/history
  • Vague privacy policy language around location and generated data
#203 2★

Terrific Tools Random Group Generator↗ opens tool website

People who want a small browser tool with individual member entry and simple group-size controls

Free, no account
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesCopy results to clipboard: PartialEditable/random team names: PartialMinimum group sizes: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: PartialCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: PartialEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Basic random grouping only. Balance/remainder toggles help group sizes, not assignment quality.
Features2★
Slightly above the weakest splitters: group title, members-per-group, dummy names, balance/remainder toggles, and dynamic members. No constraints, attributes, multi-round planning, saved rosters, or diagnostics.
Exports2★
The page advertises copying groups to the clipboard, but there is no spreadsheet, CSV, image, PDF, Zoom, or share workflow visible in the basic form.
Privacy1.9★
The site says tools process data in the browser, which is good for names. The privacy policy also discloses Google Analytics and Mediavine interest-based advertising, and the page sets CMP/ad-consent storage.
Ease2.4★
The tool is usable, but individual member rows are a bad fit for a class or workshop roster. A simple textarea would be faster.
Design2.1★
The actual form is readable, but the repeated launch banner, account/comment widgets, and enormous unrelated-tool grid make the page feel bloated.
Account friction4.6★
Generation itself is free and no-login. The account UI is mainly for ratings, comments, and bookmarks.
Review notes

Summary

A weak random group generator with a few basic controls but worse input ergonomics than many simpler tools. The surrounding site noise and ad/privacy posture drag it down further.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required for generation; account prompts are for comments, ratings, bookmarks, and the broader site.

Pros

  • Free and browser-based
  • Members-per-group control
  • Balance and remainder toggles
  • Dummy-name option
  • Advertises copy-to-clipboard output

Cons

  • No paste-a-list textarea
  • Clunky member entry for real rosters
  • No constraints, balancing, or repeats
  • No CSV/spreadsheet export
  • Heavy surrounding site clutter
  • Google Analytics and Mediavine ad/privacy footprint

Design2★

Account friction5★

Review notes

Summary

A working bare splitter dragged down by a heavy ad/consent stack and almost no features.

Pricing: Free web tool. No account required.

Pros

  • Free
  • No account required
  • Very easy to use
  • Accepts pasted names
  • Generates groups quickly

Cons

  • Heavy ad-tech footprint
  • No result copy/export
  • No group-size mode
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No saved roster/history
  • Very generic tool-page design

  • No group-size mode

#206 1.9★

Sheffield Hallam TEL-e-vision Group Maker↗ opens tool website

Very quick no-account random classroom groups

Free, no accountbasic-randomizer
Browser: YesNo account: YesPaste names: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain random grouping. It makes even-ish groups by shuffling and distributing names round-robin, but there is no balancing, preferences, keep-apart logic, or repeat reduction.
Features1.4★
Almost nothing beyond random splitting by number of groups. The roles field is just displayed alongside each group rather than acting like a real assignment or constraint system. No balancing, constraints, imports, repeat handling, or diagnostics.
Exports1.2★
Extremely weak. Results are just written to a new page view. You can manually select/print through the browser, but there is no export, copy button, CSV, spreadsheet, image, PDF, share link, or saved history.
Privacy3.6★
Better than server-side randomizers because the grouping logic runs in page JavaScript and there is no account. However the page includes Sheffield Hallam Google Tag Manager, and the broader university privacy policy is general website/institutional tracking rather than a dedicated student-roster promise for this tool.
Ease4.2★
Very simple: paste names, choose number of groups, click the button. The only friction is the dated page and confusing optional roles field.
Design1.8★
Very dated. Large old-style controls, table layout, document.write result page, and no modern polish. It is readable, but barely a product UI.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

A bare-bones random splitter. Useful in a pinch, but it is not competitive with modern group generators and has no real planning/export capability.

Pricing: Free web page. No account or payment required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Runs in the browser
  • Fast paste-and-generate workflow
  • Simple number-of-groups control
  • Free university-hosted page

Cons

  • Very dated UI
  • No constraints or balancing
  • Roles field is not real role assignment
  • No export/copy/share controls
  • No group size control beyond number of groups
  • Google Tag Manager on the page

Quality2★

Features1.2★
Almost no feature depth: paste names and choose number of groups. No group-size mode, constraints, balancing, leaders, exports, or diagnostics.
Exports1★
Page display only. No copy, download, CSV, print, share, or saved history.

Good on observed behavior. The tool page loads local JS plus jQuery/Bootstrap and the grouping code runs client-side with Math.random; no obvious ads/analytics scripts were present in the tested page HTML.
Ease3.7★

Design2.4★
Simple Bootstrap-style layout. Not ugly, but generic and partly untranslated.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

A minimal local random splitter. Privacy/page simplicity is good, but the tool is too bare to be competitive.

Pricing: Free. No account required.

Pros

  • Free and no account required
  • Runs client-side
  • Very simple
  • Paste names textarea
  • No obvious ad/analytics scripts on tested page

Cons

  • Partly Italian UI on English page
  • Only number-of-groups mode
  • No copy/download/export
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No saved rosters/history
  • Very bare product surface
#208 1.8★

ClassTools Random Group Generator↗ opens tool website

Very quick classroom group splits

Free with ads/upsellsbasic-randomizer
Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesEmbeddable results: PartialImage/PDF export: PartialPaste names: YesSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: PartialExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: PartialMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: PartialSaved scenarios/workspaces: PartialScenario bundle: PartialSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Random only. This is what is advertised, but nothing more: no balancing, no constraints, no repeat reduction, and no quality diagnostics.
Features1.6★
Very shallow. It has paste names, arbitrary group count, save/link, and print, but no balancing, constraints, repeat avoidance, imports, roster management, or diagnostics.
Exports3.1★
Save/link and print are genuinely useful and better than display-only tools. There is no proper CSV, spreadsheet, PDF, image, or Zoom-friendly export workflow.
Privacy1★
Bad. The page shows a large consent/ad stack, including language about personal data being stored/accessed/shared with 1147 partners, plus Ezoic/Gatekeeper/Google/Funding Choices and multiple tracking identifiers. I would not enter sensitive classroom rosters here.
Ease4.4★
The core workflow is easy because there is almost nothing to configure: paste names, choose group count, generate. Ads, consent prompts, premium nudges, and old UI clutter keep it from being a clean 5/5.
Design1★
Terrible, dated, and cluttered. The tool is usable, but the surrounding page feels like an old ad-heavy template site rather than a modern group generator.
Account friction5★
No account is required, but a premium login exists. For the basic group generator workflow, there is basically no signup friction.
Review notes

Summary

Generally, one of the weakest group generators on this list. It works, but it is a dated, ad-heavy, shallow random splitter whose search-engine visibility is far higher than its product quality deserves.

Pricing: Free, ad-supported web tool. No account or payment is needed for the random group generator. Premium removes ads and adds broader ClassTools workspace/history benefits, but the reviewed free group-generator workflow is surrounded by monetized site clutter.

Pros

  • Fast to use
  • No account required
  • Paste names
  • Arbitrary number of groups
  • Save/share link
  • Print output

Cons

  • Extremely weak despite high search-engine visibility
  • Terrible dated design
  • Heavy ad/consent/tracking footprint
  • No advanced rules
  • No balancing or constraints
  • No repeat avoidance
  • No CSV/spreadsheet export
  • Search ranking far exceeds product quality
#209 1.8★

Musely Team Builder Generator↗ opens tool website

Not recommended unless Musely verification works for the user

Free with ads/upsellsconstraintsai-assisted
Hard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialAttribute balance: PartialNo account: PartialEditable/random team names: PartialGender/label balancing: PartialPaste names: YesSolver tuning controls: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: PartialAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: PartialEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: PartialGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: PartialHard keep-together: PartialImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: PartialPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: NoSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: PartialCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: PartialUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: PartialZoom CSV: No
Quality0.8★
No actual result was verified. If the advertised controls work, it may be more capable than a random splitter, but the reviewed flow produced no teams.
Features2★
Many controls are visible or claimed: distribution method, team names, captains, constraints, skill categories, custom requirements, language, quantity, and smarter AI. Because generation never completed, these remain mostly unverified feature claims rather than proven group-generation capability.
Exports0★
No output was generated in live testing, so no copy/export/share/result format was verified.
Privacy1.6★
Poor-to-mixed. The page uses Cloudflare verification plus PostHog/Plausible-style analytics, and the privacy policy allows collection of personal data, usage data, cookies/tracking, marketing communications, affiliates/partners, and third-party sharing. Entered participant names may be sent to an AI/service if generation works, but live testing did not reach that point.
Ease1.4★
The form is understandable, but live testing could not get past verification; Generate remained disabled after filling inputs and retrying verification.
Design3.4★
Modern SaaS-style interface with clean fields and advanced settings, though the sidebar, upgrade prompts, verification, and generic AI-tool shell add friction.
Account friction1.8★
No explicit login was required to fill the form, but generation was blocked by verification and the product has prominent signup/upgrade surfaces.
Review notes

Summary

Feature claims are ambitious, but the reviewed generator did not actually generate teams in live testing because verification never completed.

Pricing: Public tool page with free-looking generator controls, upgrade/signup surfaces, and a disabled generate flow in live testing. No completed no-account generation was verified.

Pros

  • Modern interface
  • Participant paste field
  • Visible advanced controls for constraints, captains, team names, and skills
  • Custom requirements field
  • Multi-language/quantity controls

Cons

  • Generate stayed disabled behind verification in live testing
  • No actual teams were produced or verified
  • No output/export behavior could be evaluated
  • Prominent signup/upgrade SaaS shell
  • Broad tracking and data-sharing privacy policy
  • Unclear whether participant names are processed by AI/server when generation works
#210 1.8★

Schule Dietmannsried Gruppen Generator↗ opens tool website

Bare-bones German classroom group splitting

Custom group capacities: PartialBrowser: YesNo account: YesMinimum group sizes: PartialPaste names: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: PartialDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: PartialMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: NoRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: NoWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Plain random grouping with even-ish groups. No assignment quality beyond random splitting.
Features1.5★
Only two basic split modes: maximum group size or number of groups. No constraints, balancing, leaders, exports, saved rosters, or diagnostics.
Exports1★
Page display only. No copy button, download, CSV, print view, share link, or saved result history.
Privacy4.2★
Good on the tool page itself: no obvious ad/analytics scripts and the grouping appears client-side. The broader school privacy policy mentions cookies/server logs, but this specific page is lightweight.
Ease3.8★
Simple if you understand German: paste names, choose mode, enter a number, click generate. The labels are minimal and not localized to English.
Design1.5★
Extremely plain school utility page. Functional, but visually primitive.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

A very bare-bones random splitter. Privacy/page simplicity is good, but product capability is almost nonexistent.

Pricing: Free. No account required.

Pros

  • Free and no account required
  • Very simple page
  • Paste names textarea
  • Maximum group size or number-of-groups mode
  • No obvious ad/tracking scripts on tool page

Cons

  • German-only UI
  • Very dated/minimal design
  • No copy/download/export
  • No constraints or balancing
  • No result history
  • No advanced grouping controls

Features1★

Exports0.5★
No team export/copy was observed.
Privacy1.8★
Fetch was blocked by 403 outside the browser; browser page loaded with warnings and likely standard site scripts. No strong privacy claim.
Ease2.4★
Adding segments is simple, but forming teams requires manual repeated spins and bookkeeping.
Design1.8★
Older wheel page with lots of surrounding navigation and stats.
Account friction5★
No account required.
Review notes

Summary

A random wheel repackaged for team picking; useful for manual reveal, weak as a group generator.

Pricing: Free web tool.

Pros

  • Wheel reveal experience
  • Can paste/add segments
  • Shuffle and sort controls
  • Add image option
  • No account required

Cons

  • Does not automatically create balanced teams
  • Requires manual repeated spins/removals
  • No export/copy team result
  • No constraints or attributes
  • No repeat avoidance or diagnostics
#212 1.8★

Wheel of Names↗ opens tool website

Random picking, raffles, and manual group-building workarounds

Free with ads/upsellsbasic-randomizer
Browser: YesNo account: YesPresentation: YesSaved participant/team lists: YesResult quality diagnostics: PartialAPI/programmatic access: YesEmbeddable results: YesGoogle Sheets import: Yes
Show all features
API/programmatic access: YesAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: YesExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: YesHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: YesPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: YesRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: PartialBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: YesSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: YesScenario bundle: PartialSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: YesSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: PartialStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: YesWeighted random entries: YesWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality1.2★
As a random picker, the randomness is strong. As a group generator, result quality is barely applicable: teams have to be built manually, so there is no balancing, no constraint handling, and no repeat minimization.
Features1.2★
For group generation specifically, feature depth is very low. Multi-wheel, weighted entries, themes, embedding, API access, and audio customization are useful spinner features, but they do not create balanced groups. There is no real team splitter, no group capacities, no grouping constraints, no multi-session planning, and no repeat optimization.
Exports2.5★
Strong output/sharing options for a wheel: short links, embeddable iframes, streaming support, local/cloud saved wheels, and API access. Weak as a group-generator output surface because it does not produce proper team rosters, CSV group assignments, Zoom exports, or schedule-style results.
Privacy3★
Mixed. Better than the worst ad-tech utilities because it has local-file saving, automatic device-local state, explicit privacy/security documentation, and an option to close ads for the session. Not great for sensitive classroom names: the site uses ads and Google Analytics, cloud-saved/shared wheels can put entries in Firestore/Google infrastructure, account login is needed for cloud access, and shared URL examples can include entries directly in the URL.
Ease5★
For random picking, it is about as easy as it gets: paste names and spin. For actual group generation, the workflow becomes manual, but the base tool itself is very easy.
Design4.5★
Polished, familiar, and good for live presentation. The wheel, sounds, themes, fullscreen/streaming support, and customization are much stronger than most plain utility sites.
Account friction4.5★
No account is needed for basic spinning, local use, or local file saving. Cloud-saved wheels and cross-device access require login, so it is not completely account-free.
Review notes

Summary

Excellent random picker, but barely a group generator. It only belongs here because people use it as a manual workaround for classroom grouping.

Pricing: Free, ad-supported spinner. No paywall for premium wheel features, but that mostly benefits random picking/presentation rather than actual group generation.

Pros

  • Extremely easy random picker
  • Polished live presentation experience
  • Strong visual/audio customization
  • Save/share/embed/API options
  • Google Sheets import and weighted entries

Cons

  • Not a dedicated group generator
  • Manual workaround needed for team splits
  • No constraints, capacities, or repeat optimization
  • No proper group roster output
  • Ads and analytics reduce privacy
  • Shared/cloud wheels can expose entered names
#213 1.7★

AhaSlides users who already want random-team generation inside an interactive presentation workflow

Browser: PartialNo account: PartialPresentation: PartialShareable result links: PartialVisual/theme customization: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: PartialPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: NoPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: PartialRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: PartialSaved class roster import: NoSaved participant/team lists: NoSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: PartialSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality2★
Assume basic random assignment only from the public description. No visible constraints, balancing, or optimization.
Features1.3★
For group generation specifically, very little is visible. Do not give credit for unrelated presentation, quiz, poll, or AI slide features.
Exports1★
No useful standalone group output/export workflow is exposed on the public page.
Privacy2.7★
AhaSlides has a serious privacy/security policy and GDPR/PDPA language, but the marketing page loads GTM, Microsoft Clarity/Bing, CookieScript, Zoho chat, HubSpot/Webflow-style scripts, and platform data is hosted/processed by AhaSlides.
Ease1.8★
The page does not behave like a straightforward public group generator. It pushes users toward signup and the broader platform.
Design3.8★
The marketing page is polished, but that does not make the group generator itself good.
Account friction1.5★
The page explicitly pushes signup to unlock more features. Bad for a quick public group generator.
Review notes

Summary

Useful only if you are already in AhaSlides. As a standalone group generator, it is weak and hidden behind platform/account context.

Pricing: AhaSlides has a free plan with limits and paid presentation plans. The pricing page describes up to 5 quiz and 3 poll slides on Free, paid upgrades for unlimited interactive slides, and special education/nonprofit rates.

Pros

  • Polished brand and page design
  • Part of a real presentation platform
  • Free plan exists
  • Security/privacy documentation is more serious than content-farm tools

Cons

  • Standalone generator is not clearly accessible
  • Signup pushed for more features
  • No visible export workflow
  • No group-specific constraints or optimization
  • Many marketing/analytics/chat scripts on the page
  • Presentation features are mostly irrelevant to group generation

Quality1.5★

Exports0.8★
No generated teams or export workflow were produced in testing.
Privacy1.4★

Design2.8★

Account friction1.5★

Review notes

Summary

Mostly a generic AI prompt page with group-generator marketing; not a practical transparent assignment tool.

Pricing: BasedLabs account/product pricing applies; the public page did not expose a no-account structured generator.

Pros

  • Natural-language condition examples
  • Mentions skills, roles, restrictions, locks, and scores
  • Could help users draft grouping prompts

Cons

  • No visible structured roster/grouping UI
  • No verified output
  • No transparent algorithm
  • Likely account/product funnel
  • Hosted AI privacy concerns

Summary

Promising UI claims, but the tested public flow accepted names and then failed to show generated teams.

Pricing: Free, ad-supported web tool. No account is required.

Pros

  • No account required
  • Accepts pasted names
  • Participant count detection
  • Modern UI
  • Advertises rich team options

Cons

  • No visible teams generated in testing
  • Ad/consent clutter
  • Advertised exports not verified
  • No constraints verified
  • JavaScript errors observed

Review notes

Summary

Barely qualifies as a group generator; it is mostly a shared wheel activity.

Pricing: Viewing the shared resource is free. Editing/assignment/template features involve Wordwall login and subscription-gated surfaces.

Pros

  • Shareable Wordwall resource
  • Spin-wheel presentation
  • Embed/print/platform controls
  • Many visual style options

Cons

  • Not a direct team generator
  • Fixed/prebuilt resource unless edited
  • Login/subscription friction for editing
  • No balanced team generation
  • No useful team export
  • No constraints or diagnostics

#219 1.1★

Teach Starter Random Name Picker↗ opens tool website

Picking one student at a time, not generating groups

Free with ads/upsellsbasic-randomizer
Browser: YesNo account: PartialPresentation: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialPaste names: YesSaved class roster import: PartialVisual/theme customization: Partial
Show all features
API/programmatic access: NoAttribute balance: NoAttribute total/sum: NoCopy results to clipboard: NoCustom group capacities: NoDeterministic seed / reproducibility: NoEditable/random team names: NoEmbeddable results: NoExcel export: NoFixed person placement: NoGender/label balancing: NoGoogle Sheets import: NoHard keep-apart: NoHard keep-together: NoImage/PDF export: NoMinimum group sizes: NoMulti-round: NoNo account: PartialPair meeting count targets: NoPair meeting matrix: NoPartial attendance by session: NoPaste names: YesPer-session capacities: NoPer-session: NoPerson/group visit count: NoPinned people / fixed placements: NoPresentation: PartialRepeat encounter limits: NoResult comments: NoResult quality diagnostics: NoBrowser: YesSaved class roster import: PartialSaved participant/team lists: PartialSaved result history: NoSaved scenarios/workspaces: NoScenario bundle: NoSelf-join participant link: NoShareable result links: NoSoft prefer-apart: NoSoft prefer-together: NoSolver telemetry/result metadata: NoSolver tuning controls: NoCSV export: NoCSV import: NoStorage management/cleanup: NoTeam leader / representative assignment: NoUnique-contact: NoVisual/theme customization: PartialWeighted random entries: NoWeighted soft: NoZoom CSV: No
Quality0.8★
No group result quality to credit. Random single-name selection may be fair, but it does not create balanced teams or group assignments.
Features0.8★
Almost no group-generation feature depth. It can pick random names and use saved word lists, but it does not choose team count, group size, capacities, balancing, constraints, exports, or repeat avoidance.
Exports1★
No useful group output. At best it displays one selected name or spinner result. No team roster, copy/export surface, CSV, print groups, or shareable generated assignment.
Privacy3.2★
Better than ad-tech-heavy utilities: no obvious AdSense mess on the widget page and Teach Starter has detailed education/privacy language including COPPA/PRIVO posture. A hosted education platform with accounts, cookies, server logs, location/IP/device data, marketing uses, analytics/customer tooling, and saved word-list/account behavior.
Ease4.5★
For picking a single random name, it is easy. For generating groups, the workflow is bad because there is no group-generation workflow at all; you would have to pick names manually.
Design3.8★
Polished and classroom-friendly compared with many utility sites. The issue is not presentation quality; it is that the relevant tool is a name picker, not a group maker.
Account friction4★
The random name picker is presented as free, but the broader Teach Starter ecosystem pushes account/resource workflows, and saved class lists depend on Teach Starter account features. No account is needed for the basic idea, but it is not as frictionless as a tiny standalone splitter.
Review notes

Summary

Not a real group generator. Keep only if the article wants to mention classroom random-picking workarounds; otherwise it is a removal candidate.

Pricing: Free random name picker widget; broader Teach Starter resources and saved-account workflows may sit behind free/paid teacher plans, depending on region/resource.

Pros

  • Polished classroom-friendly random name picker
  • Free widget for simple random selection
  • Saved word lists can help teachers reuse class lists
  • Detailed education privacy documentation

Cons

  • Not a real group generator
  • No team count or group size controls
  • Manual one-name-at-a-time workaround only
  • No group roster output or export
  • Account ecosystem around saved lists/resources
  • Hosted platform collects normal account/log/cookie data

Quality0.8★
No assignment result was generated.
Features0.8★
AI grouping claims were not exposed as verifiable structured controls.
Exports0★
No generated teams or export path were produced in testing.
Privacy1★
Using it appears to require hosted AI/account processing. Mixpanel, Google Analytics/Tag Manager, affiliate/CDN scripts, and Google OAuth appeared.
Ease1.2★
The tested public flow did not expose a working roster-to-groups form.
Design2.4★
Polished marketing/sign-up UI, not a focused generator interface.
Account friction1★
High: the usable path appears to require sign-up or Google login.
Review notes

Summary

Mostly a sign-up gate with group-generator SEO copy; no practical public grouping workflow was verified.

Pricing: Account/product funnel; no standalone free structured generator was verified.

Pros

  • Polished landing page
  • Mentions AI-assisted group creation

Cons

  • No visible structured generator in public test
  • Sign-up/Google OAuth gate
  • No verified output
  • No export
  • Opaque hosted AI/privacy concerns

Review notes

Summary

Inaccessible during review; no usable group-generation capability was verified.

Pricing: Unknown from live testing because the page was inaccessible.

Cons

  • Page timed out in Playwright
  • Direct HTTP requests timed out
  • Content extraction reported blocked/inaccessible access
  • No grouping UI verified
  • No outputs verified

Transparent by design

Review the data and methodology.

The dataset includes the review rubric, structured JSON, spreadsheet-friendly CSV, and issue templates for corrections, rating disputes, and new tool suggestions.
